Name: Serprecova Asociación de Servicios de Prevención Ajenos de la Comunidad Valenciana
Description:
La Asociación de servicios de Prevención SERPRECOVA es una Organización no lucrativa, con ámbito de actuación en la Comunidad Valenciana, y cuyo objetivo es defender los intereses de los asociados y la consolidación del sector de los servicios de Prevención.
Somos una asociación con autonomía, ágil, fuerte y con gran representatividad en las instituciones de la Comunidad, que defiende y proteje los intereses de todos los asociados, y la seguridad y salud de nuestros clientes y sus trabajadores.
Facilita las relaciones entre los trabajadores y empresas y la administración pública de nuestra Comunidad, vehiculizando sus inquietudes y problemáticas, facilitando así el cumplimiento de los requisitos normativos.
Continua con la labor de integración de la PRL en las empresas a partir de pilares básicos como la formación y promoción de la salud.
Traslada al tejido empresarial la importancia de la inversión en prevención.
Defiende un modelo de servicio de prevención ajeno que apuesta por la cercanía, la calidad que otorga la proximidad al cliente, que no sólo permite rapidez de respuesta y el conocimiento de la realidad e idiosincracia del entorno, muy diferente en cada zona, sino también el tener equipos de trabajo más especializados, formados e integrados.

Name: Angels on Patrol
Description:
Angels on Patrol supports families in crisis and youth through direct requests from law enforcement officers.
Angels on Patrol: 
• Has emergency funds available for officers who identify a crisis situation involving youth, families or vulnerable individuals
• Funds recreational and enrichment opportunities for youth 

The mission of Angels on Patrol is to provide officer-initiated support to community members in times of crisis. 

Our vision is to: 
Support law enforcement efforts by enhancing quality of life for the community. 

Due to the nature of their profession, officers often come across horrendous crisis situations. Officers provide immediate assistance and ensure that the situation is stable before leaving. However, many officers have the desire to help families beyond the immediate situation. Angels on Patrol helps officers do this. By helping families and children past their immediate crisis situation, the families and the public have an additional opportunity to see officers as individuals who care about the community they serve.

Angels on Patrol currently serves 14 law enforcement agencies in Arizona, has three employees and a fully active and engaged Board of Directors.

Angels on Patrol was founded in 2009 by a Phoenix Police Lt.

“I created AoP so that officers would have an immediate resource to help those that they meet in the course of their duty.  People are able to be helped, all because an officer had the heart to reach out.”  --Jacqui MacConnell, Founder of Angels on Patrol.

Name: Jefferson County Sheriff's Office (KY)
Description:
The Jefferson County Sheriff’s Office was founded in 1781 as a county agency established by the Virginia legislature for the Commonwealth of Virginia. At that time, what is now known as Louisville, consisted only of a few houses. In 1792 the Commonwealth of Kentucky was granted statehood and Jefferson County, along with the City of Louisville, became a part of Kentucky.

Today, with a rich heritage of more than two hundred years of service, the men and women of the Jefferson County Sheriff’s Office, an Accredited Law Enforcement Agency, proudly serve a metro area of approximately one million residents.

With an authorized strength of 240 paid deputies, 102 reserve deputies, and 60 civilian support personnel, the Jefferson County Sheriff’s Office is the fourth largest law enforcement agency in the Commonwealth of Kentucky.

Name: East Providence Police Department
Description:
The East Providence Police Department is comprised of four major organizational components including the Office of the Chief of Police, the Patrol Division, the Detective Division, and the Services Division. Numerous specialized units such as the Traffic Unit, Juvenile Unit, Prosecution Unit, Vice Unit, Training Unit, Community Policing Unit, and the Office of Professional Standards and School Resource Officers work within these divisions to provide a wide array of law enforcement services. The department is currently authorized to maintain ninety-nine sworn officers and twenty-three civilian personnel.

The overall mission of the police department is to create and maintain a pro-active partnership with our residential and business communities in an effort to enhance public safety and reduce the fear and incidence of crime within the City of East Providence. The police department's primary objective is to improve the quality of life in our City by identifying and addressing public safety and community concerns. The police department recognizes the importance of working with the community at large to develop programs to meet this objective and is committed to accomplishing its mission in a professional manner that promotes respect and dignity of all people.

Our values and goals drive our ambitious agenda to promote peace, strengthen the community, and enhance the quality of life. Through our partnership with the community, we embrace the community's involvement with crime control and public safety.

We will put forth our best effort to maintain the highest standards of integrity, service to the community, and professionalism. We are committed to reducing crime through pro-active law enforcement, education, and prevention. We are committed to you and your family and to making our community a safer place to live.

Name: SCL Forensics
Description:
𝐖𝐞 𝐝𝐨𝐧’𝐭 𝐣𝐮𝐬𝐭 𝐦𝐞𝐞𝐭 𝐬𝐭𝐚𝐧𝐝𝐚𝐫𝐝𝐬, 𝐰𝐞 𝐞𝐱𝐜𝐞𝐞𝐝 𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐦.

At SCL Forensics, we specialize in Firearms and Tool Mark Examinations as well as forensic Quality Management systems—bringing decades of experience to every case, consultation, and accreditation process.

Our integrated approach supports law enforcement agencies, crime laboratories, and legal teams with:
• Onsite Workshops and Training Programs

Vi bidrager overordnet til at sikre disciplinen og opgaveløsningen i forsvaret og retssikkerheden for forsvarets militære personale. 

Militære straffesager er sager, hvor tjenestegørende militært personale overtræder militær straffelov. Militære straffesager omfatter også overtrædelser af straffeloven eller andre strafbelagte civile bestemmelser, hvis overtrædelsen har en forbindelse til tjenesten eller er begået på militært område. 

Med en ansættelse i Auditørkorpset, bliver du en del af en mindre og meget specialiseret efterforsknings- og anklagemyndighed, hvor indsigt i militære forhold er en afgørende forudsætning for, at vi dagligt kan behandle straffesager af høj kvalitet. Du bliver en del af et stærkt og dedikeret team med en tæt sammenhængskraft på tværs af geografiske skel og med daglige kontaktflader på tværs af faggrupper. Vi rekrutterer særligt vores medarbejdere blandt de dygtigste i politiet, den civile anklagemyndighed og forsvaret og fokuserer i rekrutteringen meget på koblingen mellem det militærfaglige og det strafferetlige perspektiv.

Name: Small & Rural Law Enforcement Executives Association
Description:
The Small & Rural Law Enforcement Executives Association (SRLEEA) is the only 501(c)(3) non-profit organization dedicated solely to supporting and promoting law enforcement executives and agencies that serve small, rural & tribal communities across America. SRLEEA will only focus on the concerns and needs of small, rural & tribal agencies so we strongly encourage you to maintain your membership and relations with your state association and any other specific law enforcement associations you may belong, including the large national associations like the International Association of Chiefs of Police, the National Native American Law Enforcement Association or the National Sheriffs Association. They all offer a level of support and resources that could benefit you and your agency. Together we are all stronger.

How we define Small, Rural & Tribal:

Small – Any agency with 100 or less sworn employees

Rural – Any agency serving a community with a population of less than 200 people per square mile. (based on the U.S. Census Bureau)

Tribal – Any federally recognized agency serving a tribal community, band, nation, or other organized group or community of indigenous people of North America, including any Alaska Native village.

SRLEEA is dedicated to the support and promotion of efficient law enforcement and to the protection and security of small, rural & tribal communities. Much of our work is through innovative law enforcement leadership and is done through the activities and special projects of a number of committees and through active liaison with various levels of government, departmental agencies and other nonprofit associations.

Our programs span a wide range of disciplines that address a multitude of concerns in our criminal justice system. We also participate in the creation and advancement of vital legislative and public policy initiatives that affect small, rural and tribal agencies.

Name: Risk Diversion
Description:
About Risk Diversion

Fundamental to the success of any Forensic Investigation is the ability to translate information and allegations into evidence, and to prove the casual link between the acts of the white collar criminal and the prejudice suffered. Our area of speciality includes practical and effective fraud prevention and fraud response (investigative) services, combined with proactive risk management.

Computer Forensic and Cellular Forensic Investigations combine various skills in an investigative methodology, in order to gather strong and sufficient evidence. These include auditing, accounting, legal and investigative skills.  In essence, the Computer Forensic Specialists are used to convert information into evidence which can be utilised to discipline, criminally prosecute or institute civil action against the perpetrator; in essence to prove or refute allegations.

Not only will we find the underlying cause of your problem, we will also go to great lengths to determine which weaknesses in your business processes provided the fraudster with an opportunity to prejudice his/her employer.  This approach ensures that you know what route to follow to prevent a recurrence of the fraud.

We excel in designing and delivering training and capacity building projects across the globe. This includes undertaking training needs analysis, the preparation of training modules, providing supporting documentation, tools and other materials. We have trained police officers, border guards, labour inspectors, prosecutors, Judges, politicians, social services, NGOs and members of other civil society organisations, in investigating and preventing human trafficking with particular emphasis on child trafficking and exploitation.

We have delivered training and capacity building projects in over 30 countries on behalf of governments and international organisations including: the United Nations (UNHCR, UNODC, lLO, IOM), Europol (JlT Experts Group and Heads of National Units Group), INTERPOL'S Human Trafficking Expert Group, Council of Europe/GRETA, Organisation for Security and Cooperation in Europe (OSCE), lnternational Centre for Migration Policy Development (ICMPD), European College of Policing (CEPOL), The European Judicial Network (EJN) and the Academy of European Law (ERA) , Turkish lnternational Academy against Drugs and Organised Crime (TADOC).

As human trafficking and modern slavery specialists we are proud to work closely with human rights solicitors and lawyers to support the victims of human trafficking facing prosecution or deportation.

Name: Naval Special Warfare Advanced Training Command
Property Room Consulting Inc.
Description:
I have been instructing property and evidence management courses to designated custodians since 2008.  It is what I have learned during those courses from my students that has led me down the path of consulting. Since I instruct interactively, part of process deals with application of the concepts we discuss to the student’s particular situation.  There lies the problem, the custodians are often working within systems that are antiquated and very often the department’s command staff has no idea just how bad the situation is.  Most are not compliant with best practices; many do not even follow State direction and State laws.  I have heard comments such as, “we’ve never had an audit” and, “we haven’t gotten rid of anything since the 80’s (or before)”, which on the surface appear amusing, but at its practical heart are foolish and dangerous.

Here is the why; the evidence room is not a place we as administrators see very often.  It is buried in the bowels of the department behind a closed door.  We don’t take photos of it and post them on our website.  It’s not shined up and driven in the town holiday parade.  It is out of sight, out of mind.  That is until there is a problem, which is usually a huge one and results in consequences such as lost cases, outside oversight, lost jobs and headlines. It may or may not have happened to your outfit yet, but chances are you’re aware of a horror story the next town over or the town next to that, intentional or not.

Property Room Consulting Inc. can help!  My depth of expertise in this particular and narrow area is extensive.  We can evaluate where your agency is and lead you toward compliance with best practices.  We can ensure your agency is kept updated and is regularly audited and inspected in an independent and neutral way.  We can assist in disposing of property in the appropriate manner.

Name: LA HIDTA (Los Angeles High Intensity Drug Trafficking Area)
Description:
The High Intensity Drug Trafficking Areas (HIDTA) program, created by the United States Congress with the Anti-Drug Abuse Act of 1988, provides assistance to Federal, state, local, and tribal law enforcement agencies operating in areas determined to be critical drug-trafficking regions of the United States. The purpose of the program is to reduce drug trafficking and production in the United States by facilitating cooperation among Federal, state, local, and tribal law enforcement agencies to share information and implement coordinated enforcement activities; enhancing law enforcement intelligence sharing among Federal, state, local, and tribal law enforcement agencies; providing reliable law enforcement intelligence to law enforcement agencies needed to design effective enforcement strategies and operations; and supporting coordinated law enforcement strategies which maximize use of available resources to reduce the supply of illegal drugs in designated areas and in the United States as a whole.

The Los Angeles High Intensity Drug Trafficking Area (LA HIDTA) was one of the original five HIDTA regions designated as a "Drug Trafficking Gateway."  Now there are 28 designated HIDTA regions.  The LA HIDTA region encompasses the Los Angeles, Orange, Riverside and San Bernardino Counties.  It is composed of officers and executives from more than 170 local, state, and federal law enforcement agencies within the region. The LA HIDTA is governed by an Executive Board comprised of twenty members - ten federal and ten state / local agency executives. The Executive Board facilitates inter-agency drug control efforts to eliminate or reduce drug threats.  Member executives ensure threat specific strategies and initiatives are developed, employed, supported and evaluated.

Aligned with the National Drug Control Strategy, the mission of the LA HIDTA is to measurably reduce drug trafficking, thereby reducing the impact of illicit drugs in this and other areas of the country.

Name: Georgia Peace Officers Standards and Training Council
Description:
It is the mission of the Georgia Peace Officer Standards and Training Council (P.O.S.T.) to provide the citizens of Georgia with qualified, professionally trained, ethical and competent peace officers and criminal justice professionals.

The Georgia P.O.S.T. Council administers the regulatory process, sets the standards for training and certification, and provides essential technical assistance to the law enforcement community.

The Georgia P.O.S.T. Council provides the highest degree of excellence in public safety service and eliminates unqualified individuals from the law enforcement profession.

The Georgia Peace Officer Standards and Training Council (POST) was established in 1970 by the Georgia General Assembly as a regulatory body. The composition of the Council, its power, and function is established in Title 35 Chapter 8 of the Official Code of Georgia, Annotated. The Council is responsible for the certification and regulation of Georgia's peace officers and other various public safety personnel. Additionally, POST is responsible for establishing the minimum training standards and curriculum of the personnel certified by the agency.

The POST Council consists of nineteen (19) voting members and a number of advisory members and meets quarterly. Members of the Council are appointed from state, county and local law enforcement agencies, professional associations, and from the peace officer population. The Council members serve the peace officers and citizens of this state without compensation.

Organizationally, POST staff is composed of an Executive Office and three (3) divisions:

Certification and Training Division;
Operations Division; and
Investigations Division

Name: Arizona Rangers
Description:
Who are the Arizona Rangers - The Arizona Rangers are a uniformed, law enforcement auxiliary reporting to the Governor of Arizona with a rich history dating back to the 1800’s.  

Our Services - The Arizona Rangers provide law enforcement assistance to any federal, state, and local law enforcement agencies within the State of Arizona, security services for community and civic organizations, as well as support for youth and youth organizations within Arizona.  All Arizona Rangers undergo a state and national background check and investigation, must hold a current Arizona Concealed Weapon (CCW) Permit and complete the Arizona Ranger Training Academy (ARTA). Rangers are certified to carry collapsible batons, OC spray, and meet Arizona POST qualification standards in firearms. They also receive Arizona POST training in handcuffing and defensive tactics.  Arizona Rangers may undertake specialized training by and for an agency that requires special duty assistance for which that specific training is given. Many Arizona Rangers have previous law enforcement and/or military experience.

Our History - The Arizona Rangers have a long history of upholding the US and Arizona Constitutions. With roots dating back to the late 1800s, the Arizona Rangers played a key role in the settlement of Arizona.  The history of the Arizona Rangers is one of integrity, pride, and unequaled law enforcement service. Our long commitment to the history of Arizona is built upon the dedication of men and women who, over the decades, committed themselves to a life of public service. No matter how distant, how difficult, or how dangerous, the Arizona Rangers has always answered the call for service.

The Bonneville County Sheriff's Office is responsible for Search and Rescue Operations within our 1900 square miles of East Idaho utilizing more than 40 volunteer SAR members from within our community.  Our area of responsibility stretches from the outer borders of the Idaho National Laboratory to the western Wyoming border and include the South Fork of the Snake River, Palisades Reservoir, and Ririe Reservoir.  Bonneville County is rich in business and agriculture, in addition to being situated among some of the best outdoor recreation and scenic locations in the country.  Our Deputies are dedicated to service and proud of the strong support of our community. Name: Smytec Ltd
Description:
At Smytec we provide cutting edge technology solutions for forensic evidence recovery. We have developed two groundbreaking technologies called DigiHub and BlindSite that are positioned to revolutionise the recovery of forensic evidence. No longer do forensic scientists need to rely on archaic physical and chemical enhancement treatments such as fingerprint powders. Instead they can utilise our non-contact and non-destructive digital evidence retrieval technologies, developed from space research techniques, for the recovery of fingerprints from volume, serious and post-blast scenes, and identification of bodily fluids, drugs and explosives.

Smytec is a forensic science technology company based in Market Harborough in the United Kingdom. We are evolving the forensic technology landscape through the introduction of revolutionary evidence retrieval techniques combined with individual expertise and knowledge. We are dedicated to providing law enforcement, forensic service providers and defence agencies with state-of-the-art technology to transform current physical and chemical processes and bring these into the 21st Century through innovative digital evidence recovery methods.

Alongside this, we are passionate about passing on our expertise and knowledge to budding scientists, academics, students and law enforcement agents worldwide. We work closely with academic institutes, police forces, defence agencies, and forensic providers internationally to help teach and train motivated individuals in a wide range of forensic and policing practices.

Under the leadership of Acting District Attorney Mike Keane, our office is responsible for the just prosecution of nearly every crime under state law committed within Erie County, New York. The work performed by this office is mandated by the Constitution and laws of the State of New York. 

Our office is comprised of approximately Assistant District Attorneys and approximately 100 additional employees, including confidential criminal investigators, paralegals, victim advocates and other support staff. 

The District Attorney’s Office is committed to fairly prosecuting the accused and obtaining justice for the victims. In addition to annually litigating approximately 25,000 cases in local, county, state and specialty courts, our prosecutors present cases before the Grand Jury and handle all post-conviction litigation in state and federal appellate courts.

The Erie County District Attorney's Office provides legal advice and investigative support to federal, state and local law enforcement agencies. Throughout the criminal justice process, our Assistant District Attorneys work alongside our partners in law enforcement to investigate crimes, determine whether charges should be filed and prosecute the cases in court.

Name: Jasper County Sheriff's Office
Description:
Welcome to the Jasper County Sheriff's Office. Serving the community with integrity, dedication, and professionalism, we are committed to maintaining the safety and security of our county. Our office is staffed by a team of highly trained and compassionate law enforcement professionals who work tirelessly to uphold the law and protect the rights of all citizens.

Our Mission:

Our mission is to serve and protect Jasper County through proactive law enforcement, community engagement, and a commitment to justice. We strive to build strong relationships with the communities we serve, fostering trust and collaboration to address the unique challenges and needs of our county.

Our Values:

Integrity: We hold ourselves to the highest ethical standards and are committed to transparency and accountability in all our actions.

Service: We are dedicated to serving the public with respect, compassion, and professionalism, always putting the safety and well-being of our community first.

Excellence: We continuously seek to improve our skills and services through training, innovation, and a commitment to excellence in all aspects of our work.

Teamwork: We believe in the power of collaboration, both within our office and with our community partners, to achieve our shared goals and address the needs of our county.

Our Services:

The Jasper County Sheriff's Office provides a wide range of services, including patrol, criminal investigations, community outreach, and emergency response. Our specialized units and dedicated personnel are equipped to handle everything from routine traffic enforcement to complex criminal investigations, all with a focus on enhancing public safety and fostering a positive community environment.

Name: Business Integrity Commission
Description:
The Business Integrity Commission (BIC) is always looking for new talent including attorneys, investigative auditors, investigators, background intelligence analysts, and more. As a hybrid regulatory and law enforcement agency with a unique model, we are searching for motivated candidates ready to work as part of our team to tackle everything from complex investigations and administrative compliance to data analysis. 

BIC's mission is to eliminate organized crime and other forms of corruption and criminality from the industries it regulates. BIC's goals are numerous: to ensure that the regulated businesses are able to compete fairly; marketplaces remain free from violence, fraud, rackets, and threats; make sure that customers receive fair treatment; and that businesses, which are allowed to operate in these industries, always conduct their affairs with honesty and integrity.

The agency currently regulates the commercial waste (or "trade waste") industry and public wholesale food markets in NYC. Increasingly, BIC has seen anti-competitive forces take shape in financial, tax, and other types of corporate fraud and has adapted to with fervor. While BIC has successfully prevented the wide-scale reemergence of organized crime in these industries, the influence and appearance of these actors and behaviors remains. Clearly, there is still the strong need for investigation, enforcement, and vigilance to prevent theft, fraud, and other manipulation of the industry. BIC's goal is to ensure that the trade waste and wholesale market industries remain a level playing field for honest companies and their customers.

Name: Crime Scene Investigation Equipment Ltd
Description:
Crime Scene Investigation Equipment Ltd is a family run business based in the beautiful County of Buckinghamshire, South East England. We are the oldest established company in the United Kingdom specialising in the manufacture and supply of quality materials for the Fingerprint, Scene of Crime and Forensic Investigator.

Staffed by ex-members of the Police and Security Services (British Military) we have a wealth of knowledge and expertise in all aspects of criminal and forensic investigation and therefore fully understand the problems and difficulties encountered by our customers.

It’s for this reason, that for many years, we have continued to invest in new products and services that allow our customers around the world, to detect, apprehend and prosecute those individuals who choose to live outside the law.

Our commitment to customer care and attention to detail means we are constantly seeking to manufacture and supply the best equipment possible to aid our customers in the never ending fight against crime.

Name: Riley County Police Department
Description:
[Not Monitored 24 hours a day] Riley County Police Official LinkedIn.

The First Consolidated and First Accredited Law Enforcement Agency in Kansas. Serving Riley County, Manhattan, and Ogden. 

*Social Media Policy: http://rileycountypolice.org/social-media-comment-moderation-policy *
We do not accept tips via social media, please contact us by phone or email.

The mission of the Riley County Police Department is "to reduce crime and improve the quality of life for the citizens we serve." 

The Riley County Police Department holds their employees to a high standard of service to the community and prides themselves on living and working by the following values.

Integrity - Adherence to moral and ethical principles, soundness of moral character, honesty.

Teamwork/Cooperation- Cooperative or coordinated effort on the part of a group or persons acting together as a team or in the interest of a common cause. An act or instance of working or acting together for a common purpose or benefit; joint action.

Initiative - An introductory act or step; leading action; readiness and ability in initiating action; one's personal, responsible decision.

Empathy - The intellectual identification with or vicarious experiencing of the feelings, thoughts, or attitudes of another.

Reliability - That which may be relied on; dependable in achievement, accuracy, honest, etc.

Judgment - The ability to judge, make a decision, or form an opinion objectively, authoritatively, and wisely, especially in matters affecting action; good sense; discretion.

Professionalism - Professionalism is defined as, "Professional character, spirit, or methods. The standing, practice, or methods of a professional, as distinguished from an amateur."

Loyalty - Loyalty is defined as, "The state or quality of being loyal; faithfulness to commitments or obligations."

Name: Cyber Protect UK
Description:
Cyber Protect UK is part of the National Fraud Intelligence Bureau within the City of London Police; we work closely with Action Fraud, National Cyber Security Centre and Regional Organised Crime Units to produce intelligence led and evidence based advice.  

Our primary aim is to reduce the impact of cybercrime.  We provide advice to businesses, charities and individuals, promoting secure operating practices and greater cyber awareness.

As part of a complex national cyber ‘protect’ capability our objectives are enhancing the national threat picture, encouraging secure by design and use principles and empowering individuals and organisations to protect themselves. 

As well as generating shareable online content our national network of protect officers are available to speak to you and your staff about improving your cyber defences, whether for your company or your clients. 

Follow us and join the conversation.

Name: Commissaire aux élections fédérales | Commissioner of Canada Elections
Description:
Le commissaire aux élections fédérales est le haut fonctionnaire indépendant chargé de veiller à l'observation et à l'exécution de la Loi électorale du Canada et de la Loi référendaire.

Le commissaire et ses employés s'assurent que les entités politiques, les groupes d'intervenants et les autres personnes qui participent au processus électoral le font dans le respect des règles énoncées dans la Loi, dont notamment les règles sur le financement politique.

---

The Commissioner of Canada Elections is the independent officer responsible for ensuring that the Canada Elections Act (the Act) and the Referendum Act are complied with and enforced.

The Commissioner and his staff work to ensure that political entities and other individuals and entities engaged in the electoral process do so in compliance with the rules set out in the Act, including with the political financing rules.
---

Un petit rappel que nous n’acceptons pas les plaintes liées à la Loi électorale du Canada sur les médias sociaux. Si vous avez vu ou entendu quelque chose qui, selon vous, est contraire à la Loi, c’est ici qu’il faut le signaler : https://www.cef-cce.ca/formulaire-plainte

Please note that we cannot accept Canada Elections Act-related complaints over social media. If you’ve seen or hear something you think might be against the law, you can let us know here: https://www.cef-cce.ca/complaint-form

--

Conditions d’utilisation: https://www.cef-cce.ca/avis


Terms of use: https://www.cef-cce.ca/notices

Name: Delaware River Port Authority Police Department
Description:
The Delaware River Port Authority Police Department protects life, property, and public assets spread across an extensive area, including the four Delaware River toll bridges and the PATCO High Speedline with its 13 stations. Beyond these apparent responsibilities, the department patrols and secures DRPA/PATCO property on the Pennsylvania and New Jersey highways leading to the bridges; parts of the municipalities located at the bridge landings (including Philadelphia and Chester, Pennsylvania, and Camden, N.J.); and the Delaware River waterway itself.

As a bi-state agency, the Delaware River Port Authority Public Safety Department faces special challenges.

The department provides coverage 24 hours a day, 365 days a year, and handles more than 80,000 calls for service per year. DRPA and PATCO facilities border many neighboring cities and towns, so the department works in conjunction with departments from local jurisdictions, including Philadelphia and Chester in Pennsylvania, and Bridgeport, Camden, Gloucester City, and Pennsauken in New Jersey, as well as the state police in both states. In addition, it works with federal law enforcement agencies such as the Coast Guard, the FBI, and Homeland Security.

The department also cooperates with other local, state, and federal agencies. For instance, the department’s Commercial Vehicle Unit (CVU), which inspects trucks crossing the four Authority bridges, works with the U.S. Department of Agriculture to inspect refrigerated vehicles transporting meat, seafood, dairy products, and produce.

Name: The Innocent
Description:
We are dedicated to Protecting and Preserving the INNOCENCE of children, from  child sex trafficking, online exploitation, and sexual assault by providing complimentary training, technology and equipment for online investigations.

The INNOCENCE team is made of active duty law enforcement and Federal task force officers trained and experienced in investigating child sex crimes.  Our team conducts hands-on, in-person training for Police departments throughout the United States to conduct online investigations. We believe teaching a department how to "fish" provide the equipment to "fish," and letting the department "fish," is a much more effective approach to scaling the battle against child sex crimes.

The Innocent is a conduit between the people of the local communities and the local law enforcement. We offer a solution for communities to get involved and keep their funds in their own backyards. The Innocent provides advanced trainings and state of the art equipment on a grant base to local police departments, allowing local communities to clean up their neighborhoods of online predators, making your community a safer place for your children.

It boasts a population of over 70,000, and the department offers our team members the opportunity for growth in specialty assignments, an officer/employee wellness program, a fitness program and incentives, and hiring bonuses. Name: The Institute For Criminal Justice Training Reform
Description:
We're the nation’s only nonprofit advocacy organization solely dedicated to making our streets safer for citizens and police through increased, improved and regulated training for U.S. police, law enforcement and criminal justice employees. 

The Institute for Criminal Justice Training Reform sees the complexity that exists within our broken criminal justice system. We understand that reform cannot depend on one methodology. Using a 3-dimensional approach to end police and law enforcement brutality, we focus on Community Engagement, Improved Curricula, and Legislative Change to reform training.

Travel Industry Authority 旅遊業監管局
Description:
Established in January 2020 under the Travel Industry Ordinance (Cap. 634), the Travel Industry Authority (TIA) is a statutory regulatory body of the travel industry primarily responsible for the licensing and regulation of travel agents, tourist guides and tour escorts. 

Following the full implementation of the new regulatory regime of the travel industry from 1 September 2022, TIA is committed to promoting the integrity, competence and professionalism of the travel industry practitioners as well as regulating shops that inbound tour groups are arranged to patronise. TIA is also responsible for managing the Travel Industry Compensation Fund and collecting levies from travel agents.

Name: Warwick Police Department
Description:
We are a Nationally Accredited, Community Policing Agency that stresses professionalism, integrity and fairness. The Warwick Police Department is comprised of dedicated, professional police officers that are committed to the safety and security of the residents of the City of Warwick.

We encourage you to take some time to review the vast amount of information contained within the Warwick Police Department's Annual Report. The Annual Report provides an overview of the Warwick Police Department and details various yearly statistical information that you may find useful. This includes data relating to calls for service, response times, crime reports, as well as victim and offender information.

The personnel of the Warwick Police Department, both sworn and civilian, are proud to be a part of one of the finest police departments in the United States. Each and every one of our members plays an integral part in the success of our department. Our most important goal is to ensure the safety and well being of the citizens of Warwick.

Name: Newport Beach Police Department
Description:
The Newport Beach Police Department is comprised of officers and professional staff, full-time and part-time employees, volunteers, and explorers. What unites us is our sense of service, our commitment to our community, and our proud tradition of excellence.

Building on our tradition of excellence, we will work as a team with those we serve to meet the challenges of tomorrow. We will anticipate the needs of our community and achieve success through leadership, innovation, and unparalleled service.

Name: Mountain View Police Department
Description:
Founded in 1902, the Mountain View Police Department keeps Mountain View safe and tackles crime through quality policing that secures the trust and support of the people it serves and protects. Our organizational values are: 

Provide Exceptional Service– We value being responsive to the community’s needs and seek to earn the public’s confidence and satisfaction with fair and impartial services that are highly competent, professional, and accessible to all.

Act with Integrity – We value a commitment to the nobility of policing, and the ethical standards of the organization and our profession. We are trustworthy, reliable and committed to doing the right thing, the right way, for the right reason.

Treat Others with Respect – We value approaching every contact with a guardian mindset that embodies treating people with dignity and respect, giving them a voice and listening, being impartial and fair, and building trust in our interactions with the public and our colleagues.

Want to know more about the goings on in Mountain View? Follow us on Facebook, X, Nextdoor and Instagram! Want to join our team? Visit joinmvpd.com.

Name: Estate Agents Authority
Description:
The Estate Agents Authority (EAA) is a statutory body, established in November 1997 under the Estate Agents Ordinance (EAO). Its principal functions are to regulate the practice of estate agency in Hong Kong, promote integrity and competence within the industry, and facilitate training for estate agency practitioners to enhance their standards and status.

The EAA organises qualifying examinations, issues licences to individuals and companies, handles complaints against licensees, conducts compliance inspections and metes out disciplinary sanctions to practitioners who have breached the EAO. The EAA also organises activities for the professional development of the trade and promotion of consumer education.

Description:

Name: TUTELAGE
Description:
Tutelage is a national police-led programme, working closely with all 45 UK police forces to reduce the impact of untaxed, uninsured, unsafe and untraceable vehicles on our roads. 

The initiative is led by the NPCC’s National Roads Policing Operations, Intelligence and Investigation (NRPOII) and supported by the Motor Insurers’ Bureau (MIB). 

Our initial work been to contact registered keepers of vehicles seen on the road that do not appear to have insurance. Our letters encourage registered keepers to check their vehicle's insurance status (via the MIB website www.askMID.co.uk) and warn them of the consequences of uninsured driving.

Since 2020 Operation Tutelage has encouraged over 300,000 motorists across the UK to drive insured. 

Uninsured driving is associated with an increased likelihood of being involved in a serious road crash, and pushes up the costs of insurance for all drivers. It is illegal to drive a vehicle on a road or in a public place without at least 3rd party insurance. Even if the vehicle itself is insured, if you’re not correctly insured to drive it you could be considered to be driving without insurance and could get penalised. You could receive a fixed penalty of £300 and 6 penalty points if you are caught driving a vehicle that you are not insured to drive. If the case goes to court, you could get an unlimited fine and be disqualified from driving. The police also have the power to seize, and in some cases, destroy the vehicle that’s being driven uninsured.

If you are interested in hearing more about Tutelage’s aims and objectives, please email us at tutelage_nrpoii@westmidlands.police.uk

Name: Kenosha County Sheriff's Office
Description:
Kenosha County, with a population of approximately 150,000, is tucked into the southeast corner of Wisconsin. Kenosha County is bordered on the east by Lake Michigan and is situated on the Illinois Stateline, centralized directly between Chicago, IL, and Milwaukee, WI making it a prime location for visitors and major events. 

The Kenosha County Sheriff’s Department (KSD) is a full-service law enforcement agency composed of 136 sworn members, from deputies up to the Sheriff, and 173 correctional professionals. In total, KSD employs over 375 people, including administrative and clerical assistants, and other support staff.

Deputies patrol the county and respond to a variety of calls for service. Deputy Sheriffs are utilized for civil process, School Resource Officers, Deputy Friendly, and the conveyance of inmates. The KSD Detective Bureau investigates major crimes including burglaries, arson, and homicides. Specially trained detectives focus on sensitive crimes and drug investigations. The KSD provides many opportunities within the department such as the following specialty teams; Drone Operations, Tactical Response Team (TRT), Hostage Negotiations, Marine Unit, Bomb Squad, Bike Patrol, Crowd Control, the Sheriff's Mobile Command Unit, and Honor Guard. Practical training in accident investigations, evidence processing/collection, K-9 handler, and more are available.

KSD full and part-time correctional officers (COs) are responsible for the operation of the Kenosha County Jail and Detention Center. Responsibilities include maintaining the custody, security, supervision, discipline, and welfare of those in custody. COs have opportunities for training in Crisis Intervention, being a field training officer, overseeing the electronic monitoring/Huber program, work crew, programs manager, video court specialist and discharge planner, or promotion. COs who would like to become sworn officers will be awarded preference points in the KSD Deputy Sheriff application process.

Name: Lusaka Agreement Task Force
Description:
Lusaka Agreement Task Force is the Secretariat and Operational arm of an intergovernmental treaty known as Lusaka Agreement on Cooperative Enforcement Operations Directed at Illegal Trade in Wild Fauna and Flora in Africa. The treaty is a brainchild of Wildlife Law Enforcement Officers from eight Eastern and Southern African countries meeting in Lusaka, Zambia in December 1992, under the auspices of Zambia’s Ministry of Tourism and UNEP.
The Agreement came into force on 10th December 1996 with the ratification, or formal acceptance, by four signatories. Currently, there are Seven Parties to the Agreement: The Republics of Congo (Brazzaville), Kenya, Liberia, Tanzania, Uganda, Zambia and the Kingdom of Lesotho. Republics of South Africa, Ethiopia and the Kingdom of Swaziland are signatories.

The Agreement provides for setting up of a permanent Task Force that would implement its objectives. Consequently, the Lusaka Agreement Task Force (Task Force) was launched on 1st June 1999, with its headquarters located in Nairobi, Kenya.

Name: Occupational Safety and Health Agency
Description:
The OSH Agency, the enforcement arm of the OSH Authority, officially commenced operation on August 17th 2007. It is the home of the Inspectorate. The Inspectorate is divided into three tiers: Safety and Health Inspector Is, Safety and Health Inspector IIs and Senior Inspectors.  The Chief Inspector is the head of the Inspectorate and responsible for supervising the work of all the inspectors. The OSH Inspectors are vested with legal powers to access all workplaces, acquire any information needed to carry out investigations and to use its legal intervention powers. 

The main objective of the OSH Agency is to ensure compliance with the OSH Act in accordance with its enforcement policy that encourages voluntary compliance in the first instance. Therefore raising public awareness through the engagement of tripartite partners and other stakeholders is a critical component of the Agency’s operational strategy. 

Close co-operation with government ministries and institutions, as well as supporting OSH training structures and the provision of accessible OSH information are also essential to OSHA’s mandate.

Name: Anti-Corruption Commission
Description:
The Anti-Corruption Commission was created under the Anti-Corruption Law, 2008 (the "Law") which came into effect on 1 January 2010.  The Commission is responsible for the administration of the Law.  The Law gives effect to the OECD Convention on Combating Bribery of Foreign Public Officials in International Business Transactions and to the United Nations Convention Against Corruption.  The Law covers a broad range of local corruption offences by public officials, private individuals and entities.  It extends to bribery of foreign public officials outside the Cayman Islands.  Offences under the Law are extraditable.

Principal powers, duties and functions under the Law, the Commission is responsible for the administration of the Law and shall:
 receive, consider and investigate reports to the Commission of corruption 
    offences as set out in the Law
 receive and (including from overseas anti-corruption agencies) request, 
    analyse and disseminate disclosures of information concerning corruption 
    offences, or suspected offences; or required by any law in order to counter 
    corruption; and
 detect and investigate suspected corruption offences, attempts to commit 
    an offence, or conspiracies to commit an offence.

Additional powers, duties and functions under the Law
 arrest any person who has committed or is suspected of having committed 
    a corruption offence;
 obtain evidence by search warrants with court approval;
 freeze assets and confiscate proceeds of corruption offences with court 
    approval;
 refer the results of its investigations to the Director of Public Prosecutions 
   (“DPP”) for disposition where it appears an offence has been committed;
 Assist with overseas investigations;

In accordance with the Law, the Commission consists of not less than five members appointed by the Governor, one of whom is appointed the Chairman by the Governor. The Commission is supported by the Commissions Secretariat.

Name: Pretrial Services Agency for the District of Columbia
Description:
A Leader in the D.C. Criminal Justice Community!

The Pretrial Services Agency for the District of Columbia (PSA) is an independent entity within the Court Services and Offender Supervision Agency.

PSA is a Federal agency that assists judicial officers in both the Superior Court of the District of Columbia and the United States District Court for the District of Columbia by conducting a risk assessment for every arrested person who will be presented in court, identifying detention eligibility and formulating release recommendations, as appropriate, based upon the arrestee's demographic information, criminal history, and substance use and/or mental health information. For defendants who are placed on conditional release pending trial, PSA provides supervision and treatment services that reasonably assure that they return to court and do not engage in criminal activity pending their trial and/or sentencing.

PSA's vision is to thrive as a leader within the justice system through a diverse, inclusive and empowered workforce that embodies integrity, excellence, accountability, and innovation in the delivery of the highest quality services.

Name: ILEAnet Project H2020 Scientific Coordination
Description:
ILEAnet project aims to build a sustainable organisational Law Enforcement Agency (LEA) practitioners network focused on research & innovation addressing LEA challenges, together with a community of individuals interested to exchange and collaborate in the security area. By encouraging such discussion between practitioners and experts from academia, the project will stimulate LEA capabilities to influence, develop and take up research, development and innovation (RDI) that is useful and usable for LEAs, and thus help them to tackle the major challenges they face. 

The ILEAnet Scientific coordination is ensured by the French Police College (ENSP), whose Research Center aims to initiate, implement and develop scientific operational research.  
The Scientific coordination:takes part in the animation of the scientists and experts network community: 
- Ensures constant monitoring of the general scientific evolution at European and international level.
- Provides scientific leadership to the project in view of ensuring the quality of the exchange and information; 
- Sets up and coordinate Quality Assurance process; 
- Optimises collaborative work and use of resources.
The Scientific coordination chairs the Committee of ILEAnet national Scientific Referents. More information:  https://www.ileanet.eu/ileanetconsortium/scientific-coordination/ or direct contact: ensp-ileanet@interieur.gouv.fr

This project has received funding from the European Union’s Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme under grant agreement No. 740714.

Name: ManyLaws
Description:
The ManyLaws Project (EU-wide Legal Text Mining using Big Data Processing Infrastructures) is a platform set up to deliver a set of services for citizens, businesses and administrations in the European Union, Austria and Greece.

The Action's overall objective is to enable access to legal information across the European Union and improve the efficacy of decision making in legislative procedures operated by public bodies. Specifically, the Action will set-up a platform (ManyLaws) which will deliver a set of services for citizens, businesses and administrations built upon text mining, advanced processing and semantic analysis of laws of the European Union, Austria, and Greece. The addition of laws from other Member States will be explored and, if possible, implemented. Moreover, these services will be tested within at least two law making procedures of the Greek and Austrian Parliament.

The Delaware County Sheriff’s Office is as old as the county …
having formed in 1808 with Solomon D. Smith elected as the County’s first Sheriff. It only took a total of 32 votes when he won the election! Some 41 Sheriffs later and with a population reaching 200,000, Delaware County’s rapid growth remains one of the biggest challenges – and opportunities.

Patrolling all unincorporated areas
Enforcement of all laws
Investigation of misdemeanor and felonious crimes
Servicing the courts
Providing and maintaining a jail

The Delaware County Sheriff’s Office has mutual aid relationships with other law enforcement agencies to help provide coverage and safety at all times for all residents. Delaware County consists of 459 square miles, including 1,763 miles of roadways. Also, did you know the word “sheriff” actually comes from the Old English words shire reeve, meaning an official who kept the king’s peace in the countryside?

Name: Special Narcotics Prosecutor's Office
Description:
An independent prosecutors’ office with citywide jurisdiction, the Office of the Special Narcotics Prosecutor (SNP) is responsible for felony narcotics investigations and prosecutions in the five boroughs of New York City. Founded in 1971, it is the only agency of its kind in the United States.

Created in response to a burgeoning heroin epidemic and a related spike in violent crime, SNP was granted broad authority under New York State Judiciary Laws to root out sophisticated narcotics trafficking organizations and track offenders across traditional jurisdictional boundaries. Since its inception, SNP has worked closely with local, state and federal law enforcement partners to improve the lives of New Yorkers by making neighborhoods safer.

The office’s far-reaching cases target major trafficking networks that distribute narcotics in the city, across the United States and around the world. Renowned for its expertise in wiretap investigations, SNP uses cutting-edge electronic technology to identify and pursue members of criminal enterprises from street-level dealers to top suppliers. Recognizing the link between drugs and violence, the office also investigates gang-related activity.

Cases are brought to SNP by federal, state and local law enforcement agencies, including the U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration; the New York Drug Enforcement Task Force; the U.S. Postal Service; the New York City Police Department; the New York State Police; Port Authority Police; and SNP's Investigators Unit.

Name: Puerto Rico Police Department
Description:
The Puerto Rico Police Department (PRPD) was founded in 1837 and is responsible for ensuring public safety across the island. As the primary law enforcement agency in Puerto Rico, the PRPD handles a wide range of duties, from responding to emergencies and investigating crimes to enforcing traffic laws and maintaining order in public spaces. The department operates across various specialized units, including criminal investigations, narcotics enforcement, and counter-terrorism, and works in collaboration with federal agencies to combat major criminal activities like drug trafficking and organized crime.

In recent years, the PRPD has focused on improving its community policing efforts and fostering better relationships with residents. Through programs like neighborhood watch and youth outreach initiatives, the department strives to reduce crime and create a safer environment for everyone. In addition to law enforcement duties, the PRPD plays a crucial role in disaster response and recovery, especially during events like hurricanes, where the department’s training and resources help provide relief and maintain order during emergencies.

Despite facing challenges such as limited resources and staffing shortages, the PRPD remains committed to protecting and serving the people of Puerto Rico. Their continued focus on adapting to modern crime trends, incorporating advanced technology, and enhancing training ensures they can effectively respond to the dynamic needs of the island’s communities. The PRPD also emphasizes the importance of transparency and accountability, working to rebuild public trust and strengthen community partnerships to combat crime more effectively.

Name: Data Protection Authority - MW
Description: The Malawi Data Protection Authority (DPA) is an independent statutory body established under the Data Protection Act, 2024, to oversee and enforce data protection compliance across public and private sectors. Operating under the Malawi Communications Regulatory Authority (MACRA), the DPA promotes lawful, fair, and transparent processing of personal data, raises public awareness on data rights, investigates complaints, and ensures accountability in data handling practices. Through its regulatory and advisory role, the Authority aims to strengthen trust in Malawi’s digital ecosystem, safeguard individuals’ privacy, and align the country’s data governance with international standards.

Name: UMMC Police
Description:
The University of Mississippi Medical Center’s campus police department is charged with providing for the safety and well-being of all the patients, students, faculty, staff, and guests who attend class, work, and visit our campus, and with the security of the university buildings and property.

The University of Mississippi Medical Center’s campus police officers are always working and available to assist you 24/7, 365 days a year. The department provides a comprehensive array of law enforcement services and operations through a dedicated staff of seventy-five sworn, state-commissioned police officers and twenty-five, full-time security officers. All UMMC Police officers have the same authority and must meet the same expectations as law enforcement officers in the State of Mississippi.

Mission Statement - In collaboration with our UMMC partners, the UMMC PD is committed to providing a safe environment for all students, staff, patients, and visitors. We will be empathetic and always treat people with dignity and respect. We will keep our methods transparent and remain accountable to ourselves and our diverse community.

Core Values - Empathy, Professionalism, Respect, Accountability, Transparency, Integrity

We are a forensic research and investigation organization committed to developing forensic science and strengthening criminal justice systems across Africa. Guided by our vision to build and equip a critical network of 10,000 forensic leaders, we are committed to developing and implementing innovative solutions that drive justice, forensic advancements, and sustainable development.
Through hands-on training, strategic partnerships, and research-driven initiatives, we empower forensic professionals, law enforcement agencies, and underserved communities with the knowledge and tools needed to enhance criminal investigations and promote evidence-based justice. We aim to achieve our mission through research, advocacy, training, and employment programs.
By integrating advanced forensic technologies and investigative techniques, we are bridging the forensic science gap, fostering an emerging generation of forensic leaders, and ensuring that no case is left unresolved and no one is left behind. Our work drives forensic innovation, creates new career opportunities, and strengthens justice systems across Africa and beyond.

Description:
Corona Solutions started as a software company in 1995, by Dale Harris – a former police officer, detective, and law enforcement analyst, and a member and first president of the IACA – and his son – Dan Harris, a software developer. They had a vision of providing efficient and effective staffing solutions to patrol departments.

Since its inception, Corona Solutions has not only eased the creation of patrol schedule and resource allocation plans, it has in turn seen the agencies’ service provided to their communities improve. We are proud to see our service help strengthen these relationships.

We continue to pursue the vision of Corona’s foundation by providing services that increase the confidence an agency has in their patrol operations. By presenting data-driven workload information on a regular basis, providing insight and analysis on patrol time statistics, and proposing options for improving service to your community, Corona is humbled and excited to be working with the people whose main goal is to protect and serve.

Name: New Jersey Division of the International Association for Identification
Description:
The New Jersey Division of the International Association for Identification (NJIAI) was chartered in 1986. The charter encompasses all 21 Counties in New Jersey.  The division operates as a not for profit professional organization formed for the following reasons: 
 
-To associate people in the professions of forensic identification, investigation, and scientific examination of physical evidence so that the professions may be standardized and effectively and scientifically practiced.
 
-To encourage maximum use and advancement of the science of forensic identification and crime detection.
 
-To encourage research in scientific crime detection.
 
-To keep its members apprised of the latest techniques and discoveries in forensic identification and crime detection.
 
-To employ the collective wisdom of the profession in order to advance the scientific techniques of forensic identification and crime detection.

Name: Lokale Politie Leuven
Description:
Lokale Politie Leuven verzekert de basispolitiezorg in Leuven en zijn deelgemeentes Kessel-Lo, Heverlee, Wijgmaal en Wilsele. De politiezone Leuven is een ééngemeentezone die gelegen is in het gerechtelijk arrondissement Leuven.

Onze korpsslogan is ‘Met oog voor iedereen’. Lokale Politie Leuven staat in de samenleving als één van de actoren die werkt aan de maatschappelijke veiligheid. Hierbij wil het korps een extern georiënteerde, dienstverlenende politieorganisatie zijn die invulling geeft aan een gemeenschapsgerichte en informatiegestuurde werking.

Hierbij houden we zoveel mogelijk rekening met de verwachtingen van iedereen die op één of andere manier een belang heeft bij de werking van het politiekorps: de Leuvense bevolking, de tijdelijke bewoners (o.m. studenten), de bestuurlijke en gerechtelijke overheden, bezoekers, uiteenlopende doelgroepen en partners, enz. Ook onze ongeveer 400 medewerkers verdienen die aandacht.

Ons korps heeft 'oog voor iedereen'. We luisteren om te begrijpen, we stellen het oordelen uit en schorten onze vooroordelen op, we stellen ons dienstbaar op, we zijn toegankelijk en geven duidelijke informatie, we werken samen aan gemeenschappelijke uitdagingen, we zijn betrokken en we nemen onze verantwoordelijkheid, we tonen respect, …

Description:
SMALL TOWN FEEL, BIG CITY OPPORTUNITY
Perfectly sized for officers looking to serve a close-knit community and engage in big-city action, Michigan City Police Department promises endless opportunities for career advancement and purposeful work. 

A MESSAGE FROM THE CHIEF OF POLICE
There’s no more noble profession than protecting and serving your community.

Our officers are among the most elite in the nation, a diverse group of men and women who wear their badges with honor and come home at the end of the day feeling accomplished, knowing they made a difference. 

We take great pride in our people, training, technology, and facilities, which makes MCPD one of the finest departments in the State— we are the best because we invest in the best.

The citizens of our great city depend on our officers to keep them safe, and it’s a responsibility we hold to the highest of standards, which is why our uniform is reserved for only the most exceptional public servants. Officers who serve with integrity, welcome challenges and thrive in the line of duty—brothers and sisters who want to do good and be good.  

We’re always looking to hire the best of the best and welcome you to apply.

Name: California Homicide Investigators Association
Description:
The California Homicide Investigators Association (CHIA) is a non-profit organization that was formed in 1968 when homicide detectives from Los Angeles and San Francisco met over dinner to discuss the Zodiac Killer investigation. This simple dinner gathering grew into what is now the largest organization of homicide and death investigation professionals in the United States. Since its inception, the CHIA membership has grown to over 3,000 members that include law enforcement representatives from all over the United States and Canada.

Mission Statement
The mission of the California Homicide Investigators Association (CHIA) is to provide investigative support, training and networking opportunities to homicide investigators, prosecutors and death investigation professionals throughout the Western United States.

Purpose
The "cornerstone" of our Association is our annual training conference that now attracts upwards of 800 homicide detectives, coroner investigators, crime scene technicians and prosecutors from across the nation. During this three-day event, the participants attend notable case presentations and training seminars. In addition, the conference provides a fertile environment for networking, individual case evaluations and discussions regarding the latest investigative techniques and technologies with some of the top investigators and prosecutors in the country.

Name: Burke County Sheriff's Office
Description: The Sheriff's Office is the principal law enforcement agency of Burke County, GA, providing service to more than 23,000 residents and hundreds of people who work here every day. Name: Santa Rosa Police Department
Description: The Santa Rosa Police Department has 250+ employees who proudly serve the citizens of Santa Rosa, CA, working around the clock to protect lives and property within a community that spans 40 square miles and is home to more than 167,816 people (as of the 2010 census). Our dedicated staff is committed to providing exceptional public safety services and taking a leadership role in building community partnerships to make our city a better, safer place to live, work and play. We are proud to protect and serve a community that has consistently expressed its appreciation and support for our efforts, while recognizing the value of continuing excellence in public safety.

Name: Murrieta Police Department
Description:
Since 1992, the men and women of the Murrieta Police Department have been committed to making Murrieta the safest city in Riverside County by forging a partnership with the community to effectively fight crime and improve the quality of life.

For the past decade, Murrieta has repeatedly been ranked as the safest city in Riverside and San Bernardino Counties, as reported annually by the FBI. Name: Grupa BOSS
Description:
Od momentu powstania w 2018 roku jako BOSS Protection Group, rozwijamy naszą działalność z pasją i zaangażowaniem. Dziś Grupa BOSS to dwie uzupełniające się marki: BOSS Protection Group, specjalizująca się w ochronie i bezpieczeństwie, oraz BOSS Cleaning, oferująca profesjonalne usługi sprzątające. 

Co znajduje się w naszej ofercie? 

– Usługi utrzymania czystości – nasze usługi to gwarancja czystości i porządku. Zadbamy o każdy szczegół, zarówno po eventach, jak i w przestrzeniach biurowych, mieszkalnych i komercyjnych. 

–  Ochrona imprez masowych – specjalizujemy się w zapewnianiu kompleksowej ochrony podczas imprez masowych, takich jak koncerty, festiwale, wydarzenia sportowe i inne. Nasz zespół dba o bezpieczeństwo uczestników i organizatorów. 

– Ochrona fizyczna – zapewniamy profesjonalnych pracowników do monitorowania, patrolowania i reagowania na sytuacje kryzysowe. Nasza obecność daje spokój i poczucie bezpieczeństwa. 

Zaufało nam już ponad 200 firm z sektora komercyjnego i państwowego. BOSS Protection i BOSS Cleaning to nie tylko usługi – to wsparcie, na którym możesz polegać. Dla nas nie ma rzeczy niemożliwych! 

Jesteś zainteresowany? Zapraszamy do kontaktu!

Name: Operation Santa's S.L.E.D.
Description:
Operation Santa’s S.L.E.D. (Support Law Enforcement Drive) supports law enforcement families thoughout New York State who are experiencing a personal crisis by purchasing Christmas gifts for the family, providing a cash donation to each family, and arranging for a special gift delivery that demonstrates support, encouragement, and fellowship.

We support families who are experiencing a physical, mental, emotional, or financial crisis due to a critical circumstance such as illness, serious physical injury, or death of a family member. Death includes on or off duty and suicide.

We collect donations through GoFundMe, Venmo and local agency efforts (No Shave November and other collections). Designated shoppers purchase and wrap gifts for each family. The ability to fulfill the children’s wish lists is dependent on the support of our fundraising effort. When the wish list is fulfilled, any remaining funds are provided as a monetary donation to the family.

Donations can be made at: 
Venmo @OpSantasSLED
GoFundMe https://gofund.me/891f1178

Nominations:  https://bit.ly/3B1GNpE

Name: Ontario Police Memorial Foundation
Description: The Ontario Police Memorial Foundation is a Charitable Foundation formed by the Police Association of Ontario (PAO), the Ontario Provincial Police Association (OPPA), the Toronto Police Association (TPA), the Ontario Senior Officers’ Police Association (OSOPA), and the Ontario Association of Chiefs of Police (OACP). The Foundation is responsible for the management of the Memorial, which includes adding names of fallen officers whenever required. The Foundation hosts the yearly Ceremony of Remembrance, which is always on the first Sunday in May each year.

Name: Field Forensics, Inc
Description:
Field Forensics develops narcotics and explosives test kits and instruments including specialized kits for the trace detection of fentanyl and fentanyl derivatives. Field Forensics supports police, military, customs and homeland security customer with the HandyRam II Raman Spectrometer, family of hand-held Raman spectroscopy instruments, DABIT drug test kits, and ELITE explosives detection kits.  Field Forensics' products are equally at home in the field with the military and police, mobile laboratories or forensics laboratories. 
Recently FFI introduced four new products:
1) HandyRam II a new handheld Raman spectrometer for narcotics, explosives and hazardous chemical identification.

) Fen-Her DXC.002 - a new test for fentanyl and heroin that virtually eliminates false positives and negatives and is much safer because it samples at trace levels.

Name: Florida Law Enforcement Property Recovery Unit (FLEPRU)
Description:
In May of 1987, a group of property recovery detectives within the Tampa Bay area, formed the first property recovery unit in the United States. They gathered to answer an age-old problem plaguing law enforcement, “How can law enforcement increase the recovery of stolen property and place the guilty in jail?” The detectives realized that property crimes were continually increasing, and the resources needed for these investigations focused on the suspect(s) and the stolen property. This group of detectives realized the need to utilize the “Dynamic” approach which focuses on property rather than relying on the arrest of the criminal. The detectives realized that a “Fence” was a major part of the problem and by using a “Fence” the criminals were able to convert the stolen property to cash. The “Fence” would bear the burden of selling the property to new property owners. Sometimes the new owners were unsuspecting of the property’s origin, due to the reputation of the seller.
This group recognized that information exchange on a statewide level was necessary to effectively track stolen goods, match recovered goods to victims, and apprehend criminals. The group decided to formally organize the “Florida Law Enforcement Property Recovery Unit” (FLEPRU), as the group is known, has divided the state into five regions, each having a District Director.
Over the last thirty (30) plus years FLEPRU has evolved and broadened the type of investigations we focus on as an organization. These topics include, Organized Retail Crime (ORC) investigations, auto/marine investigations, social media investigations, economic/cybercrimes investigations, robbery investigations, interviewing & interrogation methods, and topics dealing with the mental health and wellbeing of those within our professions. FLEPRU holds statewide training seminars and conferences annually. FLEPRU seminars/conferences utilizes guest instructors such as state legislators, judges, private sector executives and subject m

Name: LinCT Alumni Association
Description:
The LinCT-AA is a non-profit tax exempt association which is a separate affiliate of the Leadership in Counter Terrorism (LinCT) Program.
 
Membership in the LinCT-AA is limited to participants, syndicate directors and program managers who have completed the LinCT Program. Full ‘Members’ are those who are currently employed by a government agency whose mission includes the protection of the public from acts of terrorism. However, members who leave government employment can remain involved as an ‘Associate Member’.
 
First year participants of LinCT Programs automatically become member of the LinCT-AA for the following year. All participants, syndicate directors and program managers are then encouraged to remain a Member or Associate Member.
 
The principle purpose of the LinCT-AA is the promotion of personal and professional development, networking, exchange of good practice, and global thinking of its Members and Associate Members. There is an Annual Reconvention of members, hosted recently in Los Angeles in April 2011, April 2012,  June 2013 and April 2014 through the LinCT-AA Counter Terrorism practitioners’ conference. These conferences are enormously successful, with significant logistical and financial support from Los Angeles City and especially the LAPD.

Retskredsen omfatter geografisk Kolding, Vejle, Fredericia og Billund kommuner. 
Retten modtager sine straffesager fra anklagemyndigheden ved Sydøstjyllands Politi.
Vi er ca. 100 medarbejdere, heraf ca. 70 kontormedarbejdere og servicevagter samt ca. 30 jurister, som sammen løser opgaver i følgende 4 afdelinger: retsafdelingen (med straffe og civile sager), fogedretten, skifteretten og administrationsafdelingen.

Retten i Kolding har siden juni 2012 haft til huse i retsbygningen i Kolding Åpark 11, som blev tegnet og bygget til at huse en større byret. De bygningsmæssige forhold er derfor gode og tidssvarende. De er med til dagligt at højne medarbejdernes trivsel og arbejdsglæde og danne trygge og ordentlige rammer også for rettens brugere. Herudover har retten et bitingsted på Damhaven 5 A i Vejle, hvor der behandles visse notar- og fogedsager.

Vi arbejder hver dag på at leve op til Danmarks Domstoles mål og værdier, herunder at være en attraktiv arbejdsplads. Vi har travlt, men vi lægger vægt på et godt arbejdsmiljø med højt humør og god stemning. 
God service og brugerfokus er en kerneværdi, og vores sagsbehandling skal være præget af høj kvalitet og effektivitet i sagsbehandlingen.

Name: National Maritime Law Enforcement Academy
Description:
Following a vision and the leadership of Admiral Siler, a former U.S. Coast Guard Commandant, the National Maritime Law Enforcement Academy (NMLEA) was established in 2000  to fill a capabilities gap that continues to effect law enforcement and emergency response operations on the water all across America. The NMLEA provides education and training for law enforcement  personnel, emergency responders and public safety professionals, as well as for the critical infrastructure industries located on or near the maritime environment.  As an extension of an agency's training staff, the NMLEA provides nationally recognized training and exercise programs, assisting agencies and departments of all sizes to improve tactical and response operations within a department.

Our programs are uniquely designed and individually customized to meet each agency's specific needs and utilize their assets in order to increase the officers’ operational capabilities.  The key to raising the level of professionalism, proficiency, officer safety and survival is to customize the training to utilize the actual platforms in the environment they will be working in and with the equipment they will be using.  

Members of the Academy will be able to receive college credits for the training that they receive, share best practices among their peers in a closed forum,  have direct access to instructors and recognized Subject Matter Experts, receive discounts on training courses, and get special access to field tested operational gear and equipment through the Academy's online store. Most importantly, members of the Academy represent America's Maritime Guardians, and we represent them.

As a non-profit membership driven organization, we provide "Strength through Knowledge" and a unique ability to "Ready the Guardians" who patrol, protect and preserve America's waterways.

Name: HealthIM
Description:
HealthIM’s vision is to assist law enforcement, first responders, and health care professionals to better manage incidents involving persons with serious mental disorders who are in crisis. Based on a suite of mental health assessment instruments developed by the non-profit research organization interRAI, HealthIM supports assessment of patients within both the community and in-patient settings. 

Founded by two University of Waterloo graduates, HealthIM was born of a commitment to develop effective software solutions within the complex environment of healthcare informatics.

The company enjoyed early success creating contract software, including an ED geriatric risk assessment screener, a Psychiatry Resident mobile app and a medication reconciliation tool for patients with heart failure. In 2015, HealthIM was admitted to the Velocity program, an incubator run by the University of Waterloo. The company has since won two funding competitions and has been featured in numerous trade publications and conferences.

Since 2013, HealthIM has developed an application specific to law enforcement agencies. The application is a digital version of the interRAI Brief Mental Health Screener (BMHS), a questionnaire which is completed by front line officers with minimal training using mobile data terminals (MDT). The BMHS is an academically developed and peer-reviewed instrument that computes the risk (scale of 1 to 10) that an individual may harm themselves, harm others or to fail to care for themselves.

The BMHS tool was tested and refined over a 2 year period in co-operation with police services across southwestern Ontario including the Brantford Police Service and Niagara Regional Police Service. Feedback from front line officers, management, and Executive Officers was then implemented to improve the software.  HealthIM is now working with Police Services across Canada and in the US

In addition to receiving the same training as their municipal police counterparts, our officers are required to have bachelor’s degrees – rare for a police force. Our officers know what it’s like to be a college student and can “see things through a student’s eyes.”

As the third-largest law enforcement agency in Allegheny County, we cover the 132-acre Pittsburgh campus along with University facilities in outlying Pittsburgh neighborhoods such as the Surplus Warehouse on Thomas Boulevard and the South Side Sports Complex.
