Name: Decatur, Alabama Police Department
Description: Being a police officer with the Decatur Police Department is a rewarding career. Working in a town of approximately 55,000, our officers have two distinct advantages. Officers are able to get to know the people in the communities they serve; the ratio of officers to civilians is about 1 to 454. With approximately 130 officers and more than 10 specialty areas, the Decatur Police Department is a great size to provide officers with excellent opportunities for growth and career advancement.

Name: 360 Policing
Description:
The Premier Law Enforcement Training Agency in North Carolina.

Our standards are defined by the endless pursuit of perfection.  Our passion for our craft is unprecedented with a commitment to the individual success of our students that is unrivaled.  Our team of Nationally Certified instructors come from various backgrounds with a collective experience that makes them true experts within the field of law enforcement.  We strive to provide a full spectrum of training experiences for our students, creating opportunities for safe and effective decision-making under stress, in real-time.

Name: East Coast Tactical
Description:
Welcome to East Coast Tactical!

At East Coast Tactical, we are dedicated to providing top-notch ballistic solutions. As a trusted provider in the law enforcement industry, we specialize in offering state-of-the-art ballistic shields and armor plates designed to safeguard lives.

 Our Commitment:
East Coast Tactical is committed to delivering cutting-edge protective solutions tailored to meet the evolving needs of our clients. With a focus on innovation and reliability, we strive to be at the forefront of the tactical equipment industry.

 What Sets Us Apart:
- Exceptional Quality: Our ballistic shields undergo rigorous testing to ensure the highest level of protection.
- Client-Centric Approach: We prioritize customer satisfaction, providing personalized support to meet the specific requirements of each client.

 Explore Our Range:
Visit our website, EastCoastTactical.net, to explore our comprehensive range of ballistic shields and other tactical gear. Whether you're a law enforcement professional or an individual focused on personal security, we have solutions to fit your needs.

 Join Us in Ensuring Safety:
East Coast Tactical is more than a company; it's a community dedicated to promoting safety and security. Join us in our mission to make the world a safer place.

Choose East Coast Tactical – Where Protection Meets Precision.  #EastCoastTactical #SafetyFirst #BallisticShields #LawEnforcement #Police

Name: Trauma Behind the Badge
Description:
Trauma Behind the Badge is a weekly FREE webinar created for First Responders and hosted by First Responders.

This webinar series was designed to provide insight and information on the inevitable exposure to trauma through the eyes of first responders and veterans. We aim to provide hope and guidance through education and/or connecting individuals to healthy and effective resources.

Guest Presenters will join our regular hosts to discuss a theme or specific aspect about trauma that often goes undiscussed and unnoticed.
This series is open to all first responders (sworn and non-sworn), veterans, spouses, partners, family, friends, and anyone with an interest to do these jobs

Name: Rock Hill Police Department
Description:
Rock Hill, South Carolina is an award winning city and home of Winthrop University, located twenty-five miles south of Charlotte, NC. The City’s population is just under 70,000. Rock Hill has been recognized nationally for its innovative programs leading to improved candidates for careers in both law enforcement and in non-sworn positions.

All sworn personnel begin their careers in the Patrol Division. This is where the knowledge, skills and abilities required of a police officer are honed to perfection. After successful assignment in Patrol, the officer may be exposed to additional assignments.

Vision Statement:
 
To be a highly respected leader in law enforcement dedicated to providing exceptional police services while holding ourselves accountable to the highest standards of excellence and integrity.
 
 
Mission Statement:
 
To protect, serve and cultivate meaningful community partnerships to ensure the safety and security for all.

We are actively hiring at this time and would love to hear from you if you are interested in working with us. Please contact us at rhpdrecruit.com...we look forward to hearing from you!

Name: Clay County Sheriff's Office
Description:
As one of the largest full-service sheriff’s offices in Missouri, the Clay County Sheriffs Office employee over two-hundred professional men and women in a variety of jobs. In addition to deputy sheriff positions, there are also many operational and administrative jobs available for civilian employees. 

Clay County is located in western Missouri and encompasses a portion of the Kansas City Metropolitan Area. With over 221,000 residents we are the fifth most populous county in Missouri.

Name: Hunterdon County Prosecutor's Office
Description: With fortitude, the Hunterdon County Prosecutor's Office, in partnership with the citizens and our law and public safety agencies, will provide the highest standard of public service through effective investigations, prosecution, and providing justice.

Name: Sepsa Custodias
Description: Somos una empresa de seguridad en Movimiento. Contamos con 26 años de experiencia trasladando y custodiando mercancías de alto valor.
Location: Estado de México, Estado de México

Name: Bernalillo County Sheriff's Office
Description: Bernalillo County Sheriff's Office is a law enforcement company based out of 400 Roma Ave NW, Albuquerque, New Mexico, United States.

Name: Iowa Law Enforcement Academy
Description: The Iowa Law Enforcement Academy is dedicated to providing training that creates professional law enforcement personnel to serve the communities of Iowa.

Name: Police du Chablais Vaudois - EPOC
Description:
Nos valeurs institutionnelles

Disponibilité
Par notre attitude, nous nous efforçons d'être serviables, accueillants et à l'écoute du public et de nos collègues, en tout temps.

Intégrité
Afin de demeurer irréprochables, l'image dégagée de nos actes se doit d'être exemplaire, honnête et équitable.

Confiance
Notre professionnalisme et notre bienveillance attirent la confiance de la population et de nos collègues.

Respect
Chacun de nous a le droit d'être reconnu avec ses particularités et ses différences, de s'exprimer et d'être écouté dans la tolérance. Chacun de nous a le devoir d'accepter le dialogue pour vivre en bonne harmonie.

Loyauté
Nos actions et nos décisions sont dictées par l'honnêteté, la franchise et l'intégrité, au plus près de notre conscience.

Impartialité
Nous faisons constamment preuve d'objectivité afin de garantir une totale égalité de traitement et bannissons les préjugés.

Confidentialité
La confiance représente pour nous un élément essentiel. Nous préservons donc la sphère privée et respectons le devoir de réserve, aussi bien envers le public qu'envers nos collègues.

Name: New View Police Podcast
Description: The New View Police Podcast provides a fresh no holds barred perspective on policing in the United States.

Name: The Briefing Room
Description: The Briefing Room empowers law enforcement supervisors to easily provide leading-edge training to their shift of officers during briefing or roll call. Every training session is designed within our R.I.S.E. Training Block system, containing three powerful components that teach or reinforce one simple concept every day.

Name: UW-Madison Police Department
Description:
The UW-Madison Police Department is a full-service, professional law enforcement agency that strives to protect faculty, staff, students, guests and property of the University of Wisconsin-Madison campus.

As dedicated law enforcement professionals, we prepare for, research, and respond to all individual and community needs. We develop and maintain a well-equipped, well-trained, and professional team -- we work together in an effort to resolve crises and solve crimes. 

To maintain and promote the academic and research mission of the University, we work in close cooperation with the campus community. We strive to be proactive in providing a safe environment for the people and property of the University of Wisconsin-Madison.

We deliver preventive measures to the University community -- enabling a timely, effective response to incidents and emergencies, with a goal of quick recovery. We provide recommendations and allocate resources, aiding the campus in emergency preparedness and response to minimize potential risk. 

We safeguard and serve all individuals, promoting diversity and operating in an unbiased manner. We uphold the United States Constitution as we Respect and Honor our colleagues and community by working with Integrity and Compassion.

Name: Biblioteca Compliance
Description: Proyecto de la World Compliance Association (WCA) que tiene por objetivo desarrollar contenidos bajo un enfoque práctico, mediante la producción de fascículos monotemáticos sobre temáticas relacionadas con el Compliance, ordenados en cinco ejes principales: buen gobierno, gestión de riesgos, tecnología, regulación y sectorial.

Name: Ti Training
Description: Ti Training LE, LLC is a U.S.-based designer and manufacturer of advanced simulation systems for law enforcement and public safety. From immersive RECON simulators to expansive, real-world scenario libraries, we help agencies train decision-making, de-escalation, and use-of-force with realism, flexibility, and confidence—backed by innovative technology and industry-leading support.

Name: Coweta County Sheriff's Office
Description:
The mission of the Coweta County Sheriff’s Office
is to provide the highest level of professional law enforcement service to all of the citizens of Coweta County, and to create safe communities by building partnerships that work to prevent crime.

Name: Sacramento County Probation
Description: The Sacramento County Probation Department provides clients with the assessment, treatment, supervision and support necessary to prevent re-offending, resulting in a safer community. Our highly skilled, multi-disciplinary workforce uses innovative strategies to support positive change.

Name: Truckers Life Foundation
Description:
Fundacja Truckers Life powstała z myślą o poprawie warunków życia i pracy kierowców ciężarówek. Naszą misją jest wspieranie truckerów poprzez promowanie zdrowego stylu życia, poprawę warunków pracy, a także budowanie mostów między kierowcami a biznesem transportowym. Dążymy do stworzenia branży, w której truckerzy mogą liczyć na godne warunki pracy i wsparcie w realizacji ich potrzeb.
Wierzymy, że wspólnie możemy stworzyć lepsze warunki dla kierowców, co przyniesie korzyści całej branży transportowej.

Name: Albany County Sheriffs Office
Description: The Albany County Sheriff's Office is a "full-service" Law Enforcement Agency providing law enforcement services to residents and visitors of Albany County. These services include; Uniform Patrol, Criminal Investigations Unit, Drug Interdiction Unit, Fire Investigation Unit, Scuba Unit, Crime and Accident Investigation Unit, Snowmobile Unit, Marine Patrol, Emergency Response Team (S.W.A.T.) Unit, Hostage / Crisis Negotiation Team, Emergency Management Unit, Bicycle Patrol Unit, K-9 Unit, Traffic Safety Unit, STOP DWI Unit, Civil Unit, Court Security and Inmate Transportation Unit, Forensic Unit, Emergency Medical Services Unit (Paramedics), County Fire Coordinators Unit, Domestic Violence and Special Victims Unit and a Search and Rescue Unit. Additionally this Agency provides 24 hour, seven day a week law enforcement services to the Albany International Airport, located in Colonie New York.

Name: Bucks County District Attorney
Description:
The mission of the Bucks County District Attorney’s Office is to protect all people by aggressively and successfully prosecuting criminal activity in Bucks County while engaging in community-based crime prevention programs. 

The District Attorney’s Office has a staff of more than 100 dedicated prosecutors, detectives, and support staff. In addition to investigating and prosecuting crime, the Bucks County District Attorney’s Office supports several countywide public safety community measures, which include drug diversion and treatment initiatives, mental health court, Crimewatch, the Ben’s Campaign free gunlock distribution initiative, the DUI taskforce and the Drug Strike Force, and the local DNA database. 

Additionally, the Bucks County District Attorney’s Office features the premier summer internship program in the Philadelphia area for law students. Every summer the office hires second-year law students to work as Certified Legal Interns as well as first-year law students. 

For more information about careers at the Bucks County District Attorney’s Office and our internship program visit https://bucks.crimewatchpa.com/da/29567/content/internshipscareers

Name: Politie basisteam Almere Stad-Haven
Description:
Als basisteam Almere Stad - Haven staan wij midden in de maatschappij. Wij zijn daar waar het gebeurt. Onze leidende principes zijn: 
- Alles op tafel
- Iedereen wordt gehoord
- Wie het weet, mag het zeggen
- We doen recht aan de collega en het werk
- We spelen op de bal, niet op de man
- We bouwen aan een gezamenlijke visie en doel

Name: Precise Intellectual Property LLC
Description:
Precise Intellectual Property LLC was founded in 2013, and has been certified to practice patent litigation by Supreme People's Court. Precise IP LLC(updated from Precise IP firm)  provides comprehensive legal services in intellectual property industry related to patents (inventions, utility models and designs), trademarks, copyrights, freedom to operation, anti-unfair competition etc.

 

All Precise IP LLC's professionals have obtained degrees of science or engineering in famous universities and possess solid science or engineering technical backgrounds, profound legal knowledge background and excellent foreign language skills.

Name: PUNJAB POLICE ,  India
Description: The Punjab Police (abbreviated as PP) is the police agency responsible for law enforcement and investigations within the state of Punjab, India. Punjab Police has a broad array of specialized services, including the prevention and detection of crime, maintenance of law and order and the enforcement of the Constitution of India. Its headquarters are located at Jan Marg, Chandigarh.
Location: Sahibzada Ajit Singh Nagar, Punjab , India

Name: Gadsden Police Department
Description: Law enforcement services to the City of Gadsden, population of approximately 40,000.  Located in northeast Alabama 60 miles northeast of Birmingham, 90 miles south of Chattanooga.

Name: Evansville Police Department
Description:
We pledge to provide each citizen of Evansville the highest standard of performance. We are committed to making law enforcement decisions that are fair and unbiased. The Evansville Police Department understands the significance of diversity, fairness, human rights, and justice. We strongly object to and discourage all forms of unfair discrimination. The Evansville Police are dedicated to performing our law enforcement duties in a professional manner that serves all communities regardless of race, gender, age, sexual orientation, ethnicity, religion, disability, nationality, belief system, and social status.

The Evansville Police Department strives to reflect the diversity of the consumers we serve. We are committed to our partnership with the community and all city agencies as we collaborate to address crime and quality of life issues in our neighborhoods. It is our goal to fulfill the department’s public safety mission in a manner that recognizes the vast array of experiences and differences of our customer base. The very essence of our mission is to provide Evansville with the best policing services possible.

Name: L'association professionnelle des officiers (ères) de la Sureté du Québec (A.P.O.S.Q)
Description:
Les membres du conseil d’administration de l’Association professionnelle des officiers de la Sûreté du Québec vous souhaitent la bienvenue. Celle-ci a été créée le 20 août 2002 à la suite de la dissolution du CRPO (Comité des relations professionnelles des officiers de la Sûreté du Québec). 

Les objectifs pour lesquels l’association a été constituée sont ceux prévus par la Loi sur les syndicats professionnels et, plus précisément :

L’étude, la défense et le développement des intérêts économiques, sociaux et moraux de ses membres et ce, dans le but de permettre à la Sûreté du Québec de se développer.

Nous publions un bulletin vous informant de l’évolution des travaux par les membres du conseil d’administration, entre autres sur les avancées lors des rencontres du comité consultatif avec les membres de l’État-major sur le site internet de l’APOSQ. Site web
https://www.aposq.com

À la suite de votre adhésion nous vous donnerons accès au site internet de l’APOSQ, tous les documents y sont répertoriés ; Règlements généraux, bulletin, formulaire d’assistance judiciaire, photos des membres retraités, etc.

Name: Stop Stick Ltd.
Description:
For over 25 years, Stop Stick has been law enforcement's choice in pursuit termination and pursuit prevention. As a trusted partner to more than 12,000 agencies in the U.S. and world-wide. Our Stop Stick solutions equip more than 500,000 units and have helped successfully end hundreds of thousands of pursuits – with more than 40,000 documented successes – making us one of law enforcement’s most trusted brands. We are committed to serving those who serve us by providing training and tools that are proven to be safe, simple to use, and effective.
http://www.stopstick.com

Name: California College and University Police and Pubic Safety Chiefs’ Association
Description:
The California College and University Police and Public Safety Chiefs’ Association (CCUPPCA), was founded to represent and promote the interests of safety and security in the public, private and K-12 institutions throughout California.

The non-profit Association is dedicated to promoting and advancing the professional development of higher educational campus safety by acting as a focal point for the exchange of professional ideas and information, and by hosting conferences and training classes.

CCUPPCA is committed to strengthening the political voice of higher educational and K-12 campus safety by working with legislators on issues which affect the safety of the students, faculty, and staff of California's colleges, universities and K-12 campuses.

Name: Grand Prairie Police Department
Description:
The Grand Prairie Police Department in Grand Prairie, Texas, is continually seeking diverse, highly motivated, community-oriented officers with integrity, who are willing to face a variety of challenges and responsibilities. We are committed to working in partnership with the residents of our growing community to provide a safe, secure environment to enhance the quality of life for the 187,050 Grand Prairie residents. Grand Prairie Police Department's authorized strength is now nearly 270 officers.

MISSION

The Grand Prairie Police Department is dedicated to service and partnering with our community to maintain a safe environment with a high quality of life.

VALUES

Commitment– to service through community partnerships, innovation and a holistic approach to policing. 
Pride– in our appearance, in our professionalism and in safeguarding our community.
Respect– for our profession and for those we serve.

MOTTO

We Protect and Serve

Name: Merced County Sheriff's Office
Description:
Our mission is to provide responsive, professional, and caring law enforcement services to all the people of Merced County.  We will:

Respond to calls for service promptly
Protect lives and property to the very best of our abilities
Initiate and maintain crime prevention programs
Apprehend criminal offenders

We will maintain correctional facilities and operate correctional programs in the most efficient and effective manner possible.  We will be diligent in our role as Coroners to protect the rights and dignity of those who can no longer speak for themselves.

As Merced County Sheriff/Coroner employees, we will always conform to the high standards expected by the public, by being fair, compassionate, unbiased, and in conformance with the Law Enforcement Code of Ethics.

Name: Justice Management Division
Description: The mission of the Justice Management Division (JMD) is to strengthen the Department of Justice’s capacity to achieve its goals and objectives, maximize return on program investments, and deliver quality and timely services pertaining to organization, management, and administration. JMD provides advice and counsel to senior management officials relating to all administrative functions of the Department.

Name: California District Attorneys Association
Description: The mission of the California District Attorneys Association (CDAA) is to promote justice while enhancing prosecutorial excellence. To that end, CDAA is THE source of continuing legal education and legislative advocacy for its membership. In addition to offering seminars, publications, and extensive online tools, CDAA serves as a forum for the exchange of information and innovation in the criminal justice field.

Name: National Internal Affairs Investigators Association (NIAIA)
Description:
The NIAIA is a growing, professional association of IA investigators including those from a variety of public safety and academic agencies—including police, corrections, fire, and EMS—throughout the United States, Canada, and now across the globe internationally.  Our association originally formed in 1986 as a state association, changed to a regional association covering the southeast the next year, and became a national entity in 1993.

The NIAIA holds an annual training conference that provides instruction specifically geared toward IA investigators.  Go to niaia.org/conference to learn more.

Our purpose is to develop and establish professional standards of performance and integrity for Internal Affairs investigators; to provide training to bring about professional growth and development; to present a forum for discussion of common problems and current court decisions; to gather and disseminate information regarding improved administrative and technical practices; to assist law enforcement agencies in the founding and continued development of Internal Affairs Investigative units; and to foster law enforcement cooperation and the exchange of information and experience among Internal Affairs investigators.

***We invite you to please follow us here, join the LinkedIn NIAIA Group, and follow us on Facebook!

We are not an investigative agency, and cannot investigate citizen complaints. Please direct such matters to your local, state, or federal authorities.

Name: Essex County District Attorney's Office
Description: The Office of Essex County District Attorney Paul F. Tucker ensures public safety in the Massachusetts county's 34 cities and towns by prosecuting crimes, investigating unattended deaths, supporting victims, providing juvenile justice and diversion programs, and partnering with social service agencies.

Name: Aurora Illinois Police Department
Description: The Aurora Police Department is the second-largest municipal police department in the State of Illinois and is made up of dedicated members on a mission to improve the quality of life in Aurora through innovation, partnerships, and dedicated service to our community.

Name: Berkshire County Sheriff's Office
Description:
The Berkshire County Sheriff's Office is the largest law enforcement agency in Berkshire County. Located in Pittsfield, MA., the Sheriff's Office oversees the county Jail and House of Correction and Regional 911 Dispatch Center. We currently hold dual accreditation with the American Correctional Association (ACA) as well as the National Commission on Correctional Health Care (NCCHC). 

The 30 towns and 2 cities in Berkshire County are encompassed within 946 square miles of rural, mountainous terrain. The Sheriff offers an array of support services to all law enforcement agencies in the county to include: K-9 Unit, Mounted Unit, Underwater Search and Rescue Unit, Mobile Command Center, Traffic Control Trailer, Electronic sign-boards and portable light towers. 

Partnerships involving Sheriff's Deputies include: Berkshire County Law Enforcement Task Force, FBI Joint Terrorism Task Force (JTTF), US Marshalls Task Force, FBI Western MA Gang Task Force, Anti-Crime Unit, Special Response Team (SRT), and Pittsfield Student Resource Center (SRC). 

The Sheriff's Office is headed by Sheriff Thomas Bowler, who was elected Sheriff in 2010 after serving over 24 years with the Pittsfield Police Department as a detective. The Sheriff oversees a staff of over 200 employees, and manages an annual operating budget of $17.5 million.

Name: Boulder Police Department
Description:
Welcome to the Boulder Police Department's LinkedIn!

If you’re looking for a police department that prides itself on being a family, not just a job, the Boulder PD is the perfect fit. 

Boulder, Colorado, nestled against the stunning backdrop of the Rocky Mountains, is a vibrant city renowned for its outdoor recreational opportunities, including hiking, rock climbing, and mountain biking. Boulder is home to a thriving arts scene, eclectic eateries, and a plethora of craft breweries. Its picturesque downtown area, lined with charming boutiques and cafes, exudes a laid-back yet energetic atmosphere that captures the essence of Colorado living.

The Boulder Police Department is seeking the best and brightest individuals who want a challenge in life, and who also exhibit high-standards and excellence in everything they do. We believe no other police department can offer you the opportunity for growth, relationships, and benefits within a medium sized police force, like the BPD. With a higher starting salary than other entry-level positions, and dynamic work schedules, a career with the Boulder PD provides you with the financial comfort and flexibility to enjoy all the rewards that the beautiful state of Colorado has to offer.

How does the Boulder PD create such a high-standard and thriving environment? We have set some basic requirements for all of our officer applicants. You have to achieve at least one of the following to be accepted into the application process - (1) military background, (2) two years of college credits or (3) two years and good standing with another police department. 

Visit our website at BPDJobs.com to watch our recruiting video, and sign up for an in-person tour or a one-on-one conversation with a Boulder Police officer / recruiting specialist to see if we're the right fit for you.

Name: Office of the Police Ombudsman for Northern Ireland
Description: Police Ombudsman for Northern Ireland provides an independent system for the handling of complaints about the conduct of police officers.

Name: The University of Texas at Austin Police Department
Description:
The University of Texas at Austin Police Department (UTPD) is a state police agency with more than 100 employees. It is a fully-functioning, accredited state police agency serving the UT community 24 hours a day. UTPD includes an investigative division, K-9 teams, bicycle patrol team and its own 911 Dispatch Center.

The department operates on a community-oriented policing philosophy, encouraging greater communication between police and the communities they serve.

Name: Duluth Police Department
Description: Welcome to the official LinkedIn page of the Duluth Police Department in Duluth, Georgia.

Name: National Center for Gang Training
Description:
The National Center for Gang Training (NCFGT) was established in 2019 by a juvenile justice professional (and certified gang specialist) who recognized the importance of sharing information and working with law enforcement agencies, social service agencies, community-based organizations and the education system to impact the spread of gangs.

By training and bringing together those that are actively involved in the investigation, prosecution, suppression, intervention and prevention of criminal street gangs, prison gangs and extremist groups and their affiliations, the NCFGT will provide a venue not only for training, but also for collaboration and communication across disciplines.

The NCFGT works in partnership with fellow gang specialists, investigators and law enforcement experts throughout the world to gain insight and information in the areas of gang prevention, gang intervention, gang enforcement, gang suppression, gang member rehabilitation and gang member re-entry into mainstream society.

Name: Midtjysk Brand & Redning
Description: Midtjysk Brand & Redning is a Fire & Rescue company based out of Kejlstrupvej 99 C, Silkeborg, Denmark covering Viborg and Silkeborg Municipalities.

It is now the WORLD'S LARGEST provider of reality based training equipment and Instructor level certifications. Setcan is the worldwide exclusive distributor of the Shocknife, Blue Baton, DTS Tools, EyeLock Eye Tracking System, BattleField FX, Blood Mats, and the StressVest.

Setcan owns the largest private Law Enforcement training center in Canada.

Name: B.W.Y Canine Ltd
Description:
B.W.Y Canine Ltd was established to the meet the demand for canine services both in the United Kingdom and international.

With our Instructors and Handlers coming from the UK Police Services or from Ministry of Defence backgrounds we have a good mixture of experience and knowledge.
We are able to provide operational dog services and training for the UK and international in a wide variety of disciplines. 

We have considerable experience with protection and detections dogs. Our Instructors have valuable knowledge and experience with the training and operational use of:

Explosive Detection - Arms & Explosive Detection - Drug Detection - Human Remains/Cadaver Detection - Mobile Phone, Tobacco & Cash Detection - Human Scent/Body Detection - Crime Scene Investigation Dogs - Arson/Fire Investigation Dogs - General Purpose Police Dogs - Protection/Security Dogs - Trailing/Scent Article Method (SAM) Dogs.

Aswell as operational services we also have a wealth of experience delivering dog handler training. The majority of our training methods are based on the Police ACPO method and we can deliver training here in the UK or overseas.

Name: Fraternal Order of Police Lodge 40
Description:
FOP Lodge 40 represents the county law enforcement officers of Wyandotte County, Kansas including Sheriff's Deputies and Park Rangers.  The FOP is a fraternal and benevolent organization, incorporated in the state of Kansas under Section 501(c)8 of the Internal Revenue Service.

Our purpose is to foster harmony and friendship among our family of officers and to assist our "brothers and sisters in blue" in time of need.  We are also the official  bargaining unit for our deputies.

Name: PSP Corp.

PSP currently holds 7 Patents and Distributes Successfully into both the European & North American Markets. We specialize in Suicide Prevention, Restraints, Incarceration Furniture,Isolation Beds, Police Equipment, Work Place Safety Products and much more specialty psychiatric equipment as well as consultation services.

Name: Fort Lee Police Department
Description:
The Fort Lee Police Department which was founded in 1904 is the provider of municipal police services for the Borough of Fort Lee, in Bergen County New Jersey.  Fort Lee is the home of the George Washington Bridge which connects it to New York City.  The Chief of Police is Keith M. Bendul and the Deputy Chief is Patrick Kissane.

Follow us on Twitter: @FortLeePoliceNJ

Name: PoliceApp.com
Description:
Designed by law enforcement professionals and technology experts, PoliceApp.com is a central resource created to simplify the hiring process for both police agencies and applicants. For years, PoliceApp.com has served as a valuable recruitment tool, and we look forward to your continued participation and feedback.

Our Mission

The screening and selection processes can be a rigorous challenge for police applicants and departments alike. By fostering efficient communication between qualified applicants and departments with vacancies, PoliceApp.com strives to eliminate the redundancies and frustrations of law enforcement recruiting. Our process creates consistency across police departments while offering candidates an organized, streamlined application experience.

Description: At the Jersey Competition Regulatory Authority, we work to ensure that Islanders receive the best value, choice and access to high quality services, in addition to promoting competition and consumers' interests.

Name: Morgan County Sheriff's Office
Description:
Morgan County Sheriff's Office
160 North Park Avenue
Martinsville, IN 46151
Office:                 765-342-1080
Jail:                      765-342-4303
Calls for Service:  765-342-5544

Morgan County Sheriff's Office was established in 1822. The Sheriff's Office is responsible a population of about 70,000 people, 890 miles of county roads and a jail with a capacity of 440 Inmates.

Our mission at the Morgan County Sheriff's Office is to enhance the quality of life in Morgan County by serving and protecting our community through excellent law enforcement and correctional services. We are proud of the services we provide and equally proud of the confidence the public has in us and our capabilities.

The Sheriff’s Office is dedicated to providing not only superior law enforcement services to Morgan County, but also to giving back. Sheriff’s deputies and jail officers volunteer countless hours each year to numerous community events and non-profit organizations.

Name: Gulf Coast Technology Center
Description:
Imagine an environment where law enforcement agencies, private industry partners, and the academic community can conjoin together regardless of affiliation. Where one common goal, keeping our officers, citizens, and communities safe, is paramount over whose name is in the headlines. Where tools and resources of one entity could be easily shared amongst all the partners with no strings attached. A place offering a diverse atmosphere, open-door policy, and team led environment. With a focused effort centered on ingenuity, innovation, and advancement of technology to combat crime and support the latest intelligence led policing initiative. 

Welcome to the Gulf Coat Technology Center

Name: Pennsylvania Parole Board
Description:
The Parole Board was established by the Parole Act of 1941, which states “The parole system provides several benefits to the criminal justice system, including the provision of adequate supervision of the offender while protecting the public, the opportunity for the offender to become a useful member of society and the diversion of appropriate offenders from prison.”

When a judge imposes a state sentence, offenders are given a minimum and maximum sentence date. This means there is a minimum amount of time that they must serve in prison before they are eligible for parole consideration, and if they are paroled they will remain on parole until the end of their maximum sentence.
 
The Parole Board has the responsibility to parole, recommit for violations of parole, and to discharge from parole offenders sentenced to two years or more and offenders requested by the court for special probation.
 
Parole is the conditional release of an inmate from incarceration to continue serving the balance of his or her sentence under supervision within the community. Parole is a privilege, not a right; it is not automatic or guaranteed. It may be revoked for violations of the conditions of parole or for new criminal convictions.
 
Parole is not probation, which is a court sentence of supervision in the community instead of a sentence to prison or jail. An offender that violates probation could be sentenced to prison.
 
Parole is also different from a pardon or a commutation of sentence. In Pennsylvania, the Board of Pardons handles these situations, which is completely separate from the Parole Board.

AZTroopers Board is composed of sworn, civilian, and retiree leaders working together for the benefit of all DPS employees, its retirees, the DPS and the State of Arizona. Name: Dvorak Instruments, Inc.
Description:
As a leader in the field of firearms testing and training products, Dvorak Instruments is at the forefront of air recoil simulation technology. The Dvorak Tetherless Recoil System or TRS and the Dvorak Air Recoil System or ARS are drop in recoil simulators that quickly and easily convert semi-automatic and automatic firearms into safe and effective training tools.  Using compressed CO2 gas, the systems simulate recoil by acting on the gun’s bolt or slide to "cycle” the gun. Each system cycles the weapon exactly as it would during live shooting, simulating recoil and providing an audible report while marking the point of impact with the integrated laser for immediate training feedback.


By using gas instead of live ammunition, a large number of simulated rounds can be fired at low cost in any training environment, from shoot house to your house.  Not only developed for military and law enforcement, the TRS and ARS can be used by anyone, anywhere.


 Further adding to training realism, the original trigger on the firearm is not altered in any way reproducing the exact trigger pull used in real world scenarios. 


From trained professionals to beginning shooters the Dvorak TRS and ARS allow for safe, cost effective, realistic training in any environment.  Be it double taps or trigger pull, make the TRS or ARS an integral part of your training regimen. Train safe, train smart, train REAL.

Name: Peabody Police Department
Description:
The City of Peabody is a vibrant suburban community 15 miles north of Boston, bordered by Lynnfield on the west, Danvers and Middleton on the North, Salem on the east, and Lynn on the south.

Peabody has a land area of 16.5 square miles with a population of approximately 55,000. 

The Peabody Police has100 sworn officers, twenty sworn Reserve Officers, twelve civilian Dispatchers, two Animal Control Officers, Chief's Executive Assistant, Chief's Financial Assistant, seven Clerks, a Parking Clerk and multiple volunteers.

History 

In the 1850's part-time constables and "Night Watch" men were hired by the town to preserve law & order. By 1872 the town records listed officers instead of constables. Full time police officers were Lewis Manning, James E. Blanchard, and Joseph W. Skinner, the first chief & "Keeper of the Lockup". He was followed by John F. Moore (1879-1880) and Thomas W. Peaseley (1881-1895).

In 1874 the town voted to fix up the old fire house on Foster Street , to be used as a "Watch House" and police station. 

By 1883 the police station had been moved into the basement of the "new" town hall, where it remained until 1980. The first police report to the selectmen and citizens in 1893 listed the force consisting of Chief Peaseley and ten patrolmen.

Name: Clark County Sheriff's Office
Description:
In December, 1786, the General Assembly of Virginia passed an Act to deed a certain portion of land, containing 149,000 acres, North of the Falls of Ohio to General George Rogers Clark and fifteen of his soldiers. They were being rewarded for their exploration of the area and land further west in what would become the United States. This tract of land was named in honor of Clark and was know as his Grant until 1801.

 Governor William H. Harrison issued a proclamation, February 3, 1801, dividing the County of Knox and erecting a separate county of Clark. First court sessions held at Springville, February 4, 1801. In 1801 Samuel Hay was appointed first Sheriff of Clark County.

 On June 9, 1802 the Governor issued an Ordinance for the removal of the Seat of Justice to Jeffersonville. The courthouse of Clark County was built about 1836 and cost upward of $6,000. The jail was built in 1858 at the expense of $9,000.00 A Jailer's residence was attached to it. The jail at that time comprised of ten cells with a general hallway.

 In 1878 a new courthouse was built in Jeffersonville. The new jail contained 28 beds.

 In 1970 the old courthouse and jail were torn down and a new complex was constructed. The new jail had 52 beds with the Sheriff's residence attached.

 A new jail was constructed and opened April 15, 1991. The new Clark County Correctional Facility was built with 144 beds, and since added 53 beds, compared to the 52 beds in the old jail. The new facility has an indoor and outdoor recreational area and state of the art security system. To operate the new facility, additional officers had to be hired.

 In 2007 major renovations were made to the Correctional Facility bringing the total bed capacity to 446.

 Politicians, police officers and city officials paid tribute to Michael L. Becher as the Clark County Jail was named after the former sheriff in July, 2008

The Investigative Services Bureau consists of the Detective Division, Coroner’s Unit, Special Operations Section, Civil Enforcement Division, Court Security, Property Office, and Administrative Services. The Field Operations Bureau consists of Patrol Divisions, Patrol Headquarters and Auxiliary units such as Search and Rescue, Posse, Aero Squadron, Dive Team, and Sheriff’s Auxiliary Volunteer Patrol. The Custody Bureau consists of the Jail, Honor Farm, and Court Transportation. AAPLE is composed of over 700 members from City, State and Federal law enforcement agencies, private corporations, security firms, and numerous academic institutions of higher learning.

The organization continues its efforts in fostering professionalism, and provides a forum for discussing and publicizing law enforcement problems affecting society today. This is accomplished through monthly meetings, guest lectures, professional networking, professional opportunities and developing public support for improvements in the criminal justice professions.

The bureau is commanded by Captain Gregory Kirk, and is the uniformed patrol force for the city. It is the largest division of the police department and provides first response to all emergencies, performs preliminary investigations, and provides basic patrol services to our residents.

Name: International Center for Law Enforcement Studies - Use of Force Institute
Description:
The International Center for Law Enforcement Studies was founded in 1995, by a team of exemplary professionals that have a global reputation for reducing individual and organizational liability. The Center provides training and coaching for line staff, mid-management, and command staff in use of force law, application, and liability reduction systems. The Center provides an online library of use of force incidents, outcomes, cases and case law, research, statistics, and policy and procedure recommendations.

The Use of Force Institute works to dissuade, minimize and potentially eliminate excessive use of force incidents through research and evaluation of departmental polices, procedures and training curriculum. The Institute provides qualified coaching, and curriculum designed to prevent excessive use of force, while empowering and equipping law enforcement professionals with the knowledge, tools, and resources to properly manage any critical situation.

The Center and Institute have evaluated trends, causation and contributing factors to over 30 years of excessive use of force incidents, and have applied the associated findings to the development and implementation of a systemic and sustainable solution, designed to rapidly reduce excessive use of force, and the disparate impact it has on the community.

Description:
Ob kriminaltechnische Untersuchungen von Tatortspuren, Ermittlungen bei Staatsschutzdelikten und schwerer Kriminalität, landesweite Präventionsprojekte oder die Unterstützung der Polizeibehörden im Bereich der Spezialeinheiten – wir sind mit unseren breit gefächerten Aufgaben der zentrale Servicedienstleister der Landespolizei. Da Kriminalität aber nicht an Landesgrenzen Halt macht, sind wir auch Ansprechpartner in Fragen der internationalen polizeilichen Zusammenarbeit. Vor allem mit unseren osteuropäischen und skandinavischen Kollegen arbeiten wir eng zusammen, um Straftaten zu verfolgen, aber auch um ihnen vorzubeugen.
Unter unseren knapp 500 Mitarbeiterinnen und Mitarbeitern befinden sich neben Polizeivollzugskräften weitere Spezialisten aus unterschiedlichen Fachrichtungen, wie Informatiker, Biologen, Chemiker, Wirtschaftswissenschaftler usw. Dabei bauen wir auf eine vertrauensvolle Zusammenarbeit und einen offenen Umgang untereinander, um den fachlichen Anforderungen in den einzelnen Kriminalitätsbereichen gerecht zu werden. Weil uns eine hohe Arbeitszufriedenheit wichtig ist, bieten wir Ihnen u. a. verschiedene Möglichkeiten zur besseren Vereinbarkeit von Beruf und Familie.
Zum Erreichen unserer Ziele haben wir uns einer Bewertung nach dem European Foundation for Quality Management (EFQM)-Excellence-Modell unterzogen. Hier ist uns im Juni 2016 durch die Initiative Ludwig-Erhard-Preis das 3 Sterne- Prädikat „Recognised for Excellence“ verliehen und damit ein hohes Niveau an behördlicher Leistungsfähigkeit bestätigt worden.

The Division of Alcohol and Tobacco Enforcement (DATE) is a state law enforcement agency. The agency's mission is “To protect the health, safety, and welfare of people in Delaware through the enforcement of state liquor and youth access to tobacco laws while maintaining the highest state of preparedness for responding to threats against homeland security".
The Division’s officers conduct criminal and administrative investigations involving violations of the Delaware Liquor Control Act, Delaware Marijuana Control Act, and the Rules of the Delaware Alcoholic Beverage Control and Marijuana Commissioners.
Officers of the Division are certified police officers for the State of Delaware. These officers have statewide jurisdiction and arrest powers.  DATE officers have the authority to enforce all laws of the State of Delaware, including liquor, drug, criminal, and traffic statutes. 
Due to the nature of the Division’s investigations, officers primarily work in an undercover capacity. Officers conduct investigations that involve selling alcohol or marijuana without a license, selling alcohol marijuana, and/or tobacco products to minors, over-service of alcohol to intoxicated patrons, prostitution, illegal gambling on licensed premises, illegal narcotics activity on a licensed premises, hidden ownership, tobacco smuggling, tax evasion, organized criminal activity, disorderly premises and violations of other criminal statutes and administrative rules. 
Officers also collaborate with federal, state, and local law enforcement agencies on multi-jurisdictional investigations. A significant aspect of their job involves working in hazardous and potentially volatile environments, including contact with individuals who may be under the influence of alcoholic beverages and/or illegal narcotics, in possession of lethal weapons, or hostile towards law enforcement officers. 
The Division's core values are Integrity, Fairness, Accountability, Competency, and Professionalism.

In dat verband werken de landen samen op het gebied van de recherche, gericht op de aanpak van grensoverschrijdende en rechtsorde schokkende criminaliteit. Dit doen ze onder andere door capaciteit & expertise beschikbaar te stellen en de gezamenlijke verwerking van informatie te verbeteren. Dit alles is vastgelegd in ‘het Protocol Recherchesamenwerking’.

Aan de afspraken uit het Protocol werken de vier afzonderlijke politiekorpsen, maar daarnaast is het RST ingericht om een bijdrage te leveren aan deze Recherchesamenwerking. Dat doet het RST op 4 verschillende manieren:
• Door het verrichten van onderzoeken 
• Door capaciteit beschikbaar te stellen aan lokale korpsen 
• Door expertise beschikbaar te stellen 
• Door een bijdrage te leveren aan de gezamenlijke verwerking van informatie 

Voor deze vier opgaven wil het RST een betrouwbare partner en van toegevoegde waarde zijn en daarom zit samenwerking in het DNA van een RST-er: samenwerking met de korpsen, met de ketenpartners, met elkaar en met Nederland. Het motto samen doen, samen presteren en samen groeien is niet voor niets de kern van het beleid van de Recherchesamenwerking voor de komende jaren.

Name: Oklahoma Sheriffs' Association
Description:
The Oklahoma Sheriffs’ Association (OSA) is a non-profit association whose mission is to represent the elected Sheriffs in all 77 counties of Oklahoma. Currently, the association has 5,,000 active members, which includes Deputies, Detention Officers, Reserve Deputies and support staff, making it one of the largest law enforcement associations in the state. The association receives no funding from the state and relies solely on the support of its active, honorary, business and corporate members. The OSA only solicits donations by the U.S. mail. This is done to assure that 100% of the donations are used to further the mission and objectives of the OSA.

The Oklahoma Sheriffs’ Association was incorporated in its current form in 1991, although the OSA in one form or another has been in existence since statehood. Newspaper accounts, as early as 1910, revealed Sheriffs gathered to pass a resolution denouncing the fee and salary act passed by the last session of the 1910 legislature.

The mission of the Oklahoma Sheriffs' Association is to maintain the office of the Sheriff through training and education by way of developing laws and polices that promote public safety and providing technical and informational support to assist all Sheriffs to enable them to provide effective and quality law enforcement services to the citizens of the State of Oklahoma.

The OSA is registered with the Oklahoma Secretary of State as a not-for-profit corporation and is also registered with the IRS as a 501c(3) corporation. All contributions and donations to OSA are tax deductible.

Vehicular Crimes
Financial Crimes and Forfeiture
Special Victims (focused on child abuse cases)
Major Crimes
Public Integrity
Internet Crimes Against Children, Human Trafficking, and Child Exploitation
Family Violence
Additionally, we operate a 24/7 intake system, ensuring that a prosecutor reviews every instanter offense filed in the county. Our team also provides legal guidance for all arrest warrants, allowing us to:

Accept or refuse charges at the time of arrest
Offer real-time legal assistance to officers
Begin prosecuting cases immediately
Few district attorney’s offices offer such a comprehensive service, which greatly benefits both law enforcement and the citizens of Montgomery County.

Our office is also home to five accomplished appellate attorneys whose track records are second to none nationwide.

We remain committed to upholding the law with integrity, seeking justice for victims, and working tirelessly to make Montgomery County a safer place for all.

Name: U.S. Probation Office, Eastern District of Louisiana
Description:
It is the mission of the United States Probation Office for the Eastern District of Louisiana to insure the protection and safety of the community, to provide thorough and objective information to the courts, and to develop and enhance the capabilities of our staff.

We insure the protection and safety of the community through the enforcement of court-ordered conditions. The probation office offers casework services and treatment opportunities to offenders directed toward rehabilitation, focusing at all times on the accountability of the offender, reducing risks to the community, and achieving reparation for the victim. It is our mission to provide our offender population with the necessary guidance and assistance that will reduce recidivism and promote productivity.

We provide services to the court through the completion of thorough, timely, and accurate reports. We make informed decisions and recommendations to the court through appropriate assessments of the offender and statutory and guideline requirements. As officers of the court, we are dedicated to maintaining complete objectivity and upholding the highest professional standards in all services provided to the courts, attorneys, and other agencies. It is our mission to provide ongoing facilitative and consultative services to the court to assist the court in its decision-making processes.

We promote a positive work environment based on professionalism, mutual respect, and open communication. We promote commitment to duty, excellence in service, and contribution to organizational unity. While providing optimal training and expecting maximum efficiency, employees are empowered to accomplish agency goals and encouraged to initiate improvements. It is our mission to provide all staff members with the necessary resources to enhance their professional image.
Location: New Orleans, Hammond, and Houma, Louisiana

Serving with the Baton Rouge community to prevent crime and to promote the safety and well-being of all, the Baton Rouge Police Department is dedicated to serving the residents, businesses, and visitors of Louisiana’s Capital City.

Name: Frisco Police Officers Association
Description:
The Frisco Police Officers Association is an organization composed of municipal police officers, dispatchers, and civilians who are employed by the City of Frisco (TX) Police Department. The Association was organized for the purpose of perpetuating the memory and spirit of police officers who have given their lives in the pursuit of duty.  In addition, the Association supports many charitable organizations, as well as department-related activities that members are involved in.

The FPOA is affiliated with the Texas Municipal Police Association, and through that organization, works to improve the law enforcement profession statewide. Members voluntarily serve on the board of directors and various committees of TMPA, helping to lobby for legislation that keeps the Lone Star State above all others.

Name: Urbana Police Department
Description:
The Urbana Police Department serves the City of Urbana, Illinois, with a commitment to integrity, professionalism, and community partnership. Our mission is to enhance quality of life by enforcing the law, preserving peace, and reducing the fear of crime, all while upholding the rights of every individual.

We work hand-in-hand with residents, local organizations, and city leadership to build trust and strengthen public safety. Through continued training, innovation, and a focus on officer and community wellness, we strive for excellence in every aspect of our service.

Name: American Police Beat
Description: American Police Beat is the largest, most well-read and respected monthly print publication for Law Enforcement in the United States, with a 27-year history of being the definitive source for information which matters to those in the profession.  A trusted Voice of the Nation's Law Enforcement Community.

Name: Park City Police Department
Description:
The Park City Police Department works hard to engage with our community and work together to enhance and protect the life, safety, and wellness of individuals and families. Our philosophy centers on partnering with the community to provide a safe and equitable environment. We are committed to community oriented policing, which includes dedicated personnel for community education and outreach. Additionally, all officers are actively involved in community projects, police support at community events, and are dedicated to engage with individual members of our community when concerns arise.
As a department, we recognize that we have a responsibility to hold ourselves to a high standard. For this reason, we have proactively trained our officers on implicit bias, crisis intervention, and de-escalation techniques to equip officers with resources to keep the community, and themselves, safe. We established a Use of Force Review Board several years ago that reviews all uses of force within the department to ensure that there is additional oversight when force is used. We were also one of the first departments to adopt a body camera program, and we have been recognized by the federal government for the efficacy of our program.

Though we have worked hard serving all members of our community, we recognize that there is always room for improvement. As a department, we are dedicated to increasing our efforts in recruiting, hiring, and retaining officers with diverse backgrounds, bilingual language skills, and other skills that allow us to better serve our community. We appreciate your feedback on specific or broad concerns, and look forward to collectively serving the community. Park City Police Department is dedicated to working hard to ensuring the safety and wellness of everyone who lives, works, and plays here, and we need your assistance and input to achieve that mission.

Name: Crime Stoppers Association of Edmonton and Northern Alberta
Description:
Crime Stoppers is a non-profit, charitable organization that provides a tip line resource for the public where tipsters can share information about crimes and other suspicious activity anonymously. Crime Stoppers rewards tipsters whose information leads to an arrest with a reward of up to $2,000. Every year, Crime Stoppers receives thousands of tips that, without the program, may have not been reported to police. 

Crime Stoppers is a program that is run independently from the police, but has strong relationships with the Edmonton Police Service, the RCMP, Camrose Police Service, and the Military Police. Crime Stoppers is funded through the support of individuals and businesses in the community. Every year, Crime Stoppers holds fundraisers to raise money for the cause, the largest being the annual Caught by Crime Stoppers Jail Bailout (www.caughtbycrimestoppers.com).

Kancelarija zastupa klijente pred nadležnim sudovima u Republici Srbiji, javnim beležnicima, javnim izvršiteljima, organima uprave, službama za katastar nepokretnosti i drugo.

Name: Use of Force Expert Conference
Description:
Hear from top legal and subject matter experts on the research, case law, police training, and trends in national and international law enforcement use of force issues.

This conference will develop the knowledge, skills and report writing abilities essential to the professional development of the use of force instructors, police union agents, police oversight bodies and legal counsel for the provision of expert opinions for internal police processes, civil and criminal trials, Coroner's Inquests, and other related criminal justice related processes.

This conference is designed for professionals who have an interest in developing a deeper understanding of this subject matter area, or in building the foundational skills towards becoming a court-qualified use of force expert.

Learning the use of force concepts for law enforcement is a multi-layered and complex undertaking. It is rare to have the opportunity to bring together this number and calibre of subject matter experts under the umbrella of one event. This conference provides a great opportunity to learn from the top experts in their respective fields on a topic that remains one of the most challenging issues facing law enforcement today.

Today our Department consists of one Chief of Police, sixty nine sworn officers and eighty civilian personnel.  The civilian personnel consist of five information assistants, one administrative assistant, one records office manager, two secretaries, two full time records clerks, two part time records clerks, five part time parking enforcement officers and sixty-two crossing guards. Name: ArbeidsmarktResearch UvA B.V.
Description:
Arbeidsmarktresearch UvA B.V. (ARR) is onderdeel van de UvA Holding B.V. en is verbonden aan de vakgroep Arbeidsrecht van de Universiteit van Amsterdam. ARR ontwikkelt digitale, juridische kennissystemen op het gebied van het arbeidsrecht in brede zin. In 2010 is ARR opgericht door professor Evert Verhulp. Het idee is ontstaan door twee arresten van de Hoge Raad, waarin werd overwogen dat de kantonrechtersformule niet kon worden gebruikt voor de vaststelling van de schade bij een kennelijk onredelijk ontslag. Daardoor moest de schadevergoeding voor kennelijk onredelijk ontslag worden vastgesteld overeenkomstig de regels van begroting van schadevergoeding. De te verwachten werkloosheidsduur is daarbij een belangrijk element. Er was geen hulpmiddel beschikbaar om deze werkloosheidsduur te berekenen. Dat is het startschot geweest voor de ontwikkeling van een berekeningstool die de praktijk van handvatten voorziet: “Hoe Lang Werkloos?”. In eerste instantie werden de berekeningen op aanvraag handmatig gemaakt. In 2012 is de stap gezet de tool online via www.hoelangwerkloos.nl beschikbaar te stellen. Tot op heden wordt nog steeds veel gebruikgemaakt van de tool en komt deze terug in gepubliceerde rechtspraak.

Voor de WWZ heeft ARR een tweetal tools ontwikkeld. De eerste tool betreft een digitale vragenlijst om de transitievergoeding te berekenen. De tweede tool, “Mag Ontslag?” is een digitale, interactieve beslisboom, waarmee de gebruiker een inschatting van zijn kansen krijgt in een ontslagzaak onder de WWZ. Bij een kans op succes kunnen brieven worden gegenereerd, die de gebruiker in een procedure kan inbrengen.

Mocht u meer informatie willen of geïnteresseerd zijn in een samenwerking, neem dan contact op via onderstaande contactgegevens:

Naam:	Arbeidsmarktresearch UvA B.V
Adres:	Postbus 1030, 1000 BA Amsterdam
Bezoekadres:	Oudemanhuispoort 4-6, Amsterdam
E-mail:	info@magontslag.nl
Telefoonnummer:	020 - 525 23 89

We also offer an affiliate membership with the International Association of Crime Analysts. For more information on membership benefits and requirements, please visit our website.

Name: Royal Academy of Police
Description:
The Royal Police Academy in Bahrain has emerged as a center of excellence, not only in training military cadets but also in nurturing leaders through postgraduate studies in various specializations. As the demands of modern law enforcement continue to evolve, the Academy recognizes the importance of equipping its officers with advanced knowledge and specialized skills. In this article, we will highlight the success story of the Royal Police Academy in Bahrain, and how its graduate programs transformed officers into knowledgeable, multi-skilled professionals capable of dealing with the complexities of contemporary politics.

A comprehensive approach to learning and the strength of specialization

The Royal Academy of Police's postgraduate programs take a comprehensive approach to learning, providing officers with the opportunity to delve into various disciplines. From criminology to cybersecurity, officers are encouraged to pursue fields that align with their interests and the evolving needs of law enforcement. This flexibility allows officers to gain experience in several areas and contribute more effectively to modern challenges. It also adheres to the basic principles of modern law enforcement, namely ethics and respect for human rights. It places a strong emphasis on ethical behavior and education in this area to ensure police officers act fairly and professionally in all situations. Through postgraduate study, officers can leverage the strength of specialization to enhance their capabilities in particular areas of policy. Specialist knowledge enables officers to tackle complex issues with confidence and innovation, making them valuable assets to relevant departments and the wider security landscape.

Integration of theoretical and practical experience

The success of the Royal Police Academy lies in its ability to integrate theoretical knowledge and practical experience. Graduate programs are designed to balance classroom learning with practical experience.

Name: IAATI-Western Chapter
Description: The Western Regional Chapter was formed and received their IAATI Charter in 1988. The WRC is comprised of 3 Canadian Provinces of Alberta, British Columbia and Saskatchewan and the States of Alaska, Arizona, California, Colorado, Hawaii, Idaho, Montana, Nevada, Oregon, Utah, Washington and Wyoming. When the WRC was initially formed there was a concern by the local State and Provincial auto theft associations that had been in existence for many years that the WRC would somehow usurp and interfere with their stated objectives and training seminars. As a result of this concern the stated objective of the WRC became one of support for the regional auto theft associations and the training they provided and not to develop a separate Western Regional Chapter Training Seminar. As a result of this stated objective the WRC became known as the "paper chapter" as unlike the other Regional Chapters that make up IAATI, no yearly training seminar would be held so as to conflict with the State and Provincial Auto Theft Associations. This objective remains in place to this day. To further augment the WRC commitment to the local State and Provincial Auto Theft Associations the Board of Directors of the WRC is comprised of the Presidents of the State and Provincial Auto Theft Associations that exist today within the Western Regional Chapter.

Name: Viper Innovative Products, LLC

Our goal is to improve officer safety, reduce response times, and achieve better outcomes.

Our innovative approach is driven by real-world experience, not boardroom discussions. The VIPER Shield Rapid Deployment System was invented by Chad Martin, a seasoned police officer who began his career in 1998 in Lexington, KY, and currently serves just south of Cincinnati as a Chief of Police. Over the years, Chad has witnessed the evolving responsibilities of patrol officers, often requiring them to perform high-risk tactical operations without the specialized resources available to SWAT units. Recognizing the need for more protection than a bullet-resistant vest, Chad developed the VIPER Shield Rapid Deployment System to provide immediate, effective protection in critical situations.

Name: New Mexico Mounted Patrol
Description:
The New Mexico Mounted Patrol was created, at the request of Governor John Miles, by state statute in 1941 and is directed to assist all law enforcement agencies in the state. Prior to this, the agency was actively involved in law enforcement years before it was created as an unfunded state agency by New Mexico statute.

Each year, on average, Mounted Patrol Troopers donate the equivalent of twenty-three full time, trained, and equipped officers to New Mexico police agencies. Troopers have worked beside local and state police agencies during snow storms, State Fairs, rodeos, plane crashes, hazardous material spills, prison riots, pursued escaped felons, the anti-war riots in Albuquerque, and every weekend assisting agencies with the daily police duties. Troopers even went to New Orleans after Katrina to reestablish lawful presence.

When requested by a law enforcement agency, Troopers have the same authority as the requesting agency. The New Mexico Mounted Patrol is governed by a Board of Directors elected from each troop around the state. 

Interested recruits submit an application for membership is made to the Troop nearest where an they live. After a complete background investigation, selection is made by the Troop. All recruits without prior law enforcement experience attend an academy course approved by the Law Enforcement Academy Board and, upon completion, are graduated as state-certified law enforcement officers.

Description:
The City of Havre de Grace, which encompasses approximately 6.5 square miles, is a growing and diverse city with a rich history preserved in the facades of the structures in historic downtown Havre de Grace. From the City’s parks, the Promenade, and the Concord Point Lighthouse to the sprawling greens at the Bulle Rock Golf Course, Havre de Grace is a peaceful and tranquil place to live, work, and visit. It is the job of every Havre de Grace police officer to preserve the peace of our great City and enforce City, County, and State laws in a manner that reflects professionalism, courage, and commitment. The Havre de Grace Police Department strives to continuously advance the individual skills of the officers, the technology used to prevent and deter crime, and most importantly, the relationships with the members of our communities.

To meet the needs and demands of our City, the Havre de Grace Police Department is authorized 47 employees, 38 sworn officers and 9 civilian employees. The Department operates 24 hours a day, 7 days a week and is self sufficient in receiving and dispatching calls for service.

The backbone of the Police Department is the Patrol Division which includes two Police K-9 teams, four School Resource Officers, and 24 officers assigned to regular patrol duties. Supporting the Patrol Division is the Criminal Investigative Division, or CID. Our Criminal Investigative Division includes two Criminal Detectives, one Crime Scene Detective, and one Narcotics Detective who is assigned to the Harford County Joint Narcotics Task Force.

The Havre de Grace Police Department also has an Administrative Services Division. Under this Division are six civilian Police Communication Operators (Dispatch), one civilian Evidence Clerk, one Criminal Secretary (records management), one civilian Assistant to the Chief, and one Administrative Police Officer who is responsible for Community Policing, Public Information, and other various duties.

In addition to the separate Divisions, the Havre de Grace Police Department is a participating agency in the Harford County Municipal SWAT Team. Several officers from various assignments within the Havre de Grace Police Department participate in this joint initiative and were selected through a competitive, voluntary process. Members of the SWAT Team fulfill this part-time role in addition to their individual duty assignment.

The Command Staff for the Havre de Grace Police Department is comprised of the Chief of Police, a Captain responsible for the Operations Division, and a Captain responsible for the Administrative Services Division.

Na GP Segurança, priorizamos a segurança dos participantes, equipe e instalações do evento. Implementamos medidas rigorosas de prevenção e proteção, utilizando tecnologia avançada, equipamentos de ponta e práticas atualizadas. Nossa abordagem estratégica e adaptável nos permite antecipar potenciais riscos e responder de forma eficiente a qualquer eventualidade.
Nossa equipe de segurança possui conhecimento abrangente em gerenciamento de multidões, prevenção de furtos, controle de acesso, detecção de ameaças, primeiros socorros e muito mais.

Estamos constantemente atualizando nossos procedimentos e treinando nossa equipe para garantir que estejamos sempre preparados para qualquer cenário.

Além da segurança física, também fornecemos serviços de assessoria, auxiliando na avaliação de riscos, planejamento de segurança, coordenação com autoridades locais e implementação de medidas preventivas adicionais.
Com base em nossa vasta experiência, podemos oferecer soluções personalizadas que atendam às necessidades exclusivas de cada evento.

A confiança depositada em nós por inúmeros clientes ao longo dos anos é um reflexo da nossa dedicação e expertise em segurança de eventos.
Estamos comprometidos em garantir o sucesso de cada evento que protegemos, trabalhando em estreita colaboração com nossos clientes para entender seus objetivos e superar suas expectativas.

It is the mission of the Northern Arizona University Police Department to further the university’s vision of providing an outstanding undergraduate, residential education by fostering a safe, healthy environment through quality law enforcement services and community problem solving partnership

Since 1995 the department has been accredited through The Commission on Accreditation for Law Enforcement Agencies (CALEA). On April 2nd, 2022, the department received its ninth re-accreditation under CALEA’s Gold Standard.

The NAU Police Department additionally entered a self-assessment period in January 2022 in the Arizona Law Enforcement Accreditation Program (ALEAP).

Our accreditation means that you can expect to receive a consistently better level of service and accountability. It is our goal to provide the best possible police service with highly trained and community-oriented personnel.

The NAU Police Department operates 24 hours a day, 365 days a year. To report an emergency dial 9-1-1. For non-emergencies please dial 928-523-3611.

Name: Minnesota Police Reserve Officers Association
Description:
Today, the MNPROA represents over 1,000 reserve police officers and reserve deputy sheriffs from across Minnesota. We are one of the fastest growing state reserve law enforcement associations in the United States, thanks to an active and progressive leadership team and an overwhelming response from officers and agencies all over Minnesota.  The MNPROA has launched several exciting new programs with more planned for the future.  The MNPROA encourages its members to be active in their association by serving on committees or the board of directors and by bringing the services and resources of the MNPROA to their agencies. 

The MNPROA is politically nonpartisan and does not endorse nor financially support candidates for elective government office. While the MNPROA does take positions on public issues that directly affect our members and the law enforcement vocation in general, the MNPROA is not a labor union or partisan interest group.  We believe that public safety is an issue of concern to all Americans and we intend to work closely with leaders at all levels of government to ensure that the public continues to be served as effectively as possible by the dedicated men and women of reserve law enforcement. 

The MNPROA is a 501(c)(3) registered nonprofit corporation. Eligible contributions to the MNPROA are tax-deductible for individuals and businesses.

We operate a 24-hour tip line, answered 365 days a year, allowing citizens who wish to remain anonymous, an opportunity to call in and leave information to assist local law enforcement agencies.

Name: Hilltown Township Police Department
Description:
As of January 2007, the Police Department consisted of 19 sworn members and 3 administrative assistants. The Police Department provides protection around the clock and generally has 4 Officers patrolling the Township on rotating 12-hour shifts. 

The Patrol Division is the most visible and important aspect of the Police Department. It is responsible for crime prevention and detection. The Patrol Division enforces traffic laws, investigates traffic accidents, investigates crimes, performs general patrols, and responds to numerous other emergency and non-emergency calls for service. It is currently comprised of 4 Patrol Sergeants and 13 Police Officers. Several Officers are sworn as Special Bucks County Detectives to conduct narcotics investigations throughout the County. The Patrol Division also has a Bike Patrol Unit, formed in 2005, to patrol high-density areas and to have a more visible and interactive presence in the community. Thanks to the residents of the Green Meadows development, the Department also opened a Police Substation in 2005 to better serve the residents in the densely populated northwest section of the Township. 

Although the Department has seen tremendous growth, the basic mission of protecting the lives and property of those that live and work in Hilltown has remained constant. The Hilltown Township Police Department proudly serves the citizens of Hilltown Township to make this community a safe place to live, work, and play.

Name: Chattahoochee Hills Police Department
Description:
Where is Chatt hills?

Chatt Hills is a large city with a small population in southern Fulton County.  We incorporated in 2007 to preserve and protect the rural land, heritage, and culture of the area. With a population just over 3,000 residents living in a 56 sq-mile area, the land we love separates us in distance but also bonds us together to this place we call home. 

The rural communities that are now a part of Chattahoochee Hills date back more than 175 years. Many of the family’s native to this area have raised generations in the modest white frame homes that dot the countryside, and some elders, still cared for by extended families in the communities, recall life before rural electrification, paved roads, and bridges that crossed the Chattahoochee River.

The City of Chattahoochee Hills prides itself on being one of Georgia’s safest cities, where our people are our greatest asset. In addition to our dedicated sworn and civilian staff members, our reserve/volunteers help us provide outstanding services to our community. The love and dedication of our employees and volunteers contribute to the Department’s excellent reputation in the law enforcement community. The Chattahoochee Hills Police Department is committed to employing community-oriented policing strategies directed at reducing crime, reducing the fear of crime, and improving the quality of life for our citizens.

Name: Napa County District Attorney's Office
Description:
About Us

Mission
The mission of the Napa County District Attorney’s Office is to investigate and prosecute criminal and civil cases with integrity and fairness, to treat crime victims and witnesses with the highest level of respect and dignity, and to take a proactive role in crime prevention through community awareness and education. We review and prosecute criminal violations committed in Napa County, bring criminal and civil actions for environmental and consumer violations, protect and uphold the rights of victims and serve our community in various innovative ways.

Jurisdiction & Service
The District Attorney is the chief law enforcement officer in the County, responsible for investigating and prosecuting crimes and civil cases on behalf of the people. Our jurisdiction covers nearly 800 square miles and provides services to our 140,000 Napa County residents as well as millions of visitors per year. The Napa County District Attorney’s Office has about 68 employees, consisting of a dedicated and professional team of attorneys, investigative staff, victim services staff, and administrative and clerical support staff.

Functions & Assignments
The District Attorney’s Office has five key functional areas, which include:
Criminal
Consumer Protection
Environmental Protection
Juvenile
Victim-Witness Services
We also have assignments specializing in the areas of:

Domestic Violence
Elder Abuse
Fraud
Gangs
Sexual Assault

The Napa County District Attorney’s Office favors vertical prosecution of felony criminal matters as a convenience to victims and witness and to ensure consistency and uniformity in the disposition of these cases.
