Motoseguridad nació en 2002 con una misión clara: garantizar la seguridad del transporte terrestre de carga en Colombia.

Lo que comenzó como una solución para proteger las rutas cafeteras se convirtió en el modelo de referencia nacional en seguridad integral.

Hoy somos líderes en vigilancia, escolta, consultoría e innovación tecnológica, con más de 23 años de experiencia, 16.500 servicios ejecutados y cobertura en más de 15 departamentos. 

Operamos con licencia SPVSP 20204100030637 y bajo normativas BASC, OEA y SIG.

Integramos cuatro áreas estratégicas:

– Seguridad logística: escoltas motorizados y vehiculares, análisis de riesgo, reacción táctica y rastreo satelital 24/7.
 – Seguridad física y vigilancia: control de accesos y operaciones para minería, energía y retail.
 – Protección ejecutiva: esquemas VIP, transporte blindado y acompañamiento turístico.
 – Consultoría e inteligencia: poligrafía, EyeDetect y análisis predictivo con big data.

Nuestra central de monitoreo opera 24/7 con canales cifrados, control remoto de motor y alertas de desvío en tiempo real. 

Cada operación se diseña con precisión, cumplimiento legal y velocidad de respuesta.

Nuestra visión al 2027: mantenernos como la empresa más confiable del país, líder en seguridad integral con expansión tecnológica continua.

Motoseguridad no compite: establece el estándar.

Name: San Diego County Regional Leadership Institute
Description:
The San Diego County District Attorney’s Office, in collaboration with local, state and federal law enforcement agencies is facilitating the San Diego County Regional Leadership Institute (SDCRLI). The Institute is taught by over 40 regional law enforcement leaders and consists of 42 courses that focus on self-awareness, leadership competencies and police-community relations. The Institute is specifically designed to get to the core of leadership in each individual. Track I, Self-Awareness and Leadership Theory, provides the participants with 10 self-assessments that target key leadership characteristics, such as communication styles and emotional intelligence. They are also able to draw and compare their own leadership styles with the many styles discussed in Leadership Theories. In Track II, Leadership Competencies, the participants apply the knowledge from those assessments and develop leadership competencies, such as initiative, problem solving, interpersonal relationships and organizational acumen. Track III focuses on Police-Community relations, where participants are able to visit various local community centers, such as the San Diego Islamic Center, the LGBT Center, the SDPD Multi-Cultural center, as well as attend and participate in a community meeting with a diverse group of community leaders. The Institute is dedicated to building strong, positive relationships with our local communities. Through open and honest communication, we strive for better understanding and partnerships between law enforcement and all neighboring communities. 

Vision
To develop San Diego County’s regional law enforcement leadership and enhance police-community relations in a united forum. 

Mission
Through collaboration, leadership and excellence, the institute is committed to providing current, relevant and accessible training to San Diego County law enforcement leaders while also building strong community relations that are based on mutual trust and respect.

Name: EMPACT CFMLAR
Description:
Official account for the CFMLAR priority in #EMPACT.

 What is "EMPACT"? EMPACT stands for the European Multidisciplinary Platform Against Criminal Threats. It aims at tackling the most significant threats posed by organised and serious international crime to the EU. 
HOW? By improving and strengthening cooperation between the EU Member States, institutions, bodies and agencies as well as with third countries and organisations, including the private sector where relevant. EMPACT is driven by the Member States, coordinated by EUROPOL and supported by EU institutions. 

WHAT IS "CFMLAR"? CFMLAR stands for Criminal Finances, Money Laundering and Asset Recovery. EMPACT has 10 criminal priorities and CFMLAR is the one dedicated to combat and disrupt criminal networks and criminal individuals involved in criminal finances and money laundering. It also aims at fighting against corruption and facilitating asset recovery. 
HOW? Especially by supporting the automatic launch of financial investigations, developing a culture of asset recovery, sharing intelligence and funding law enforcement authorities' projects.

The driver of EMPACT CFMLAR is France  until 2025. 
Its co-drivers are Italy  and EUROPOL

Name: West Melbourne Police Department
Description:
MISSION STATEMENT
The West Melbourne Police Department’s mission is to provide exceptional service while relentlessly pursuing justice to foster a safe community.
We are drive by our calling to serve the people with honor, courage and integrity.

HISTORY
The West Melbourne Police Department traces its roots back to 1959 shortly after the City was created. Today our Department has 52 sworn officers and 10 professional staff members. Together we are responsible for the protection and safety of West Melbourne's 28,857 residents as well as the many visitors that come and go through our city every day.

SERVING THE COMMUNITY
It is important for us to stay connected within the community we serve. It is also important that you are informed about what we do, how we accomplish our work and how you can assist us in being successful with our public safety endeavors. Our website is one tool we use to achieve this goal. Thank you for visiting our Police Department's website and we welcome your suggestions, comments and views on how we can better meet your needs.

ACCREDITATION
The West Melbourne Police Department is accredited through the Commission for Florida Law Enforcement Accreditation (CFA). To be accredited we had to come into compliance with over 260 Commission approved standards, mostly relating to life, health and safety. The Department officially obtained accreditation status on June 22, 2016 and we were reaccredited in February 2020 and February 2023.  The award is in effect for a three-year period.

TO APPLY: https://westmelbourne.gov/careers

Name: Central Falls Police Department (RI)
Description:
Mission Statement
 
The fundamental mission of the Central Falls Police Department is to protect life and property; enforce the law in a fair and impartial manner; preserve the peace, order, and safety of the community we serve; safeguard the constitutional guarantees; and provide other police-related services as required by the community in a manner consistent with the values of a free society.

The Uniformed Division is charged with the primary responsibility for all law enforcement activities within the community. The Uniformed Division’s mission is to preserve the peace, protect life and property against the effects of criminal activity and to ensure the safety of the motoring public by enforcing all traffic laws.

The Investigative Division is responsible for follow-up inquiries of all major crimes, such as murder, robbery, rape, burglary, and serious assaults.  The Investigative Division also examines any other crimes that warrant an in-depth investigation as determined on a case by case basis.

The Administrative Division consists of a variety of officers within the agency which develop policy and perform the ancillary functions which support the field operations within the Central Falls Police Department.

The Central Marin Police Authority was formed under a Joint Powers Agreement (JPA) between the Town of Corte Madera, City of Larkspur, and the Town of San Anselmo.  Two members from each City/Town Council sit on and represent the Central Marin Police Council.  Overall management of the Police Authority is the function of the Management Committee comprised of City and Town Managers of each jurisdiction.

Operational function of the Authority is assigned to the Chief of Police.  The Central Marin Police Authority has an annual budget of #10.3 million with 58 employees (45 sworn).

The Authority provides police services and public safety dispatching to the communities of Corte Madera, Larkspur, San Anselmo, and portions of Greenbrae, comprising of approximately 35,000 residents.

Name: D.C. Office of Police Complaints
Description:
The Office of Police Complaints (OPC) and its governing body, the Police Complaints Board (PCB), were created by statute in 1999, and OPC opened to the public on January 8, 2001.  The agency is independent of the Metropolitan Police Department (MPD), the District of Columbia’s 3,800-member police force, and the DC Housing Authority Police Department (DCHAPD), the District's 40-member housing authority police force.  OPC's mission is to increase community trust in the District of Columbia police forces by providing a fair, thorough, and independent system of civilian oversight of law enforcement.                                             

OPC is staffed by civilians and has the authority to receive complaints involving six types of police officer misconduct: harassment, inappropriate language or conduct, retaliation, unnecessary or excessive force, discrimination, and failure to identify.  The agency's functions are to conduct fair and thorough investigations of citizen complaints, provide a reliable system of civilian oversight of law enforcement policies, procedures, and training, and promote positive community-police

Description:
BOARD OF DIRECTORS
Chief Charles W. Davall, Jr. (Ret.)  |  President
Lieutenant John J. DeVoe  (Ret.)  |  Vice President
Chief Bob Buchanan (Ret.)  |  Treasurer
Betsy McCreery  |  Financial Secretary
Special Agent Elisabete Santos  |  FBI Newark Division
Special Agent Benjamin Langel  |  FBI New York Division
Captain Kevin Creegan  |  Secretary
Inspector Tim Hardiman (Ret.)  |  Board Member
Deputy Chief Jeff Angermeyer | Sponsor Coordinator 

The Mid-Atlantic Law Enforcement Executive Development Seminar (MALEEDS) hosts its annual training conference at Princeton University each year since 1993.  This unique educational opportunity is designed to provide leadership training for police chiefs and law enforcement executives who lead agencies with 75 sworn police officers or fewer.

MALEEDS, one of twenty-one FBI-LEEDA Regional Command Colleges, is a highly successful and selective program that provides insight into some of the many challenges facing today’s police executives.  Class size is limited in order to maximize the interaction between instructors and attendees during this comprehensive training program.  The selection process draws applicants from New Jersey, New York, and Pennsylvania.

This energetic and sophisticated educational program is proudly co-sponsored by the Newark, New York, and Philadelphia Divisions of the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), along with active and retired law enforcement officers from the former Princeton Borough and the Princeton Township Police Departments, the Princeton University Department of Public Safety, and the River Vale Police Department.

Name: PPCOA
Description:
Representing the Chief Police Officers from the nine UK Ports Police Forces.

There are currently nine commercial ports within the UK who have a dedicated Ports Police Unit. Each of these units are privately funded and the Police Officers have all the powers and privileges of a constable within their respective jurisdiction area. Their aim is to prevent, detect and disrupt criminal activity, support key business areas, enforce International Ship and Port Facility Code and Port Security Regulations, and provide an expert point of contact to other law enforcement agencies working within this specialist environment. 

Members represent the following ports, Belfast, Bristol, Dover, Felixstowe, Liverpool, Portland, Teesport and Hartlepool, and Tilbury.

The PPCOA are members of the National Police Chiefs Council and collaborate and communicate effectively to ensure our ports remain the safest and most secure in the country. 

As well as being a peer support group, we exchange information on all issues port related, identifying current trends and associated threats, liaising with DfT, British Ports Association, the Joint Maritime Security Centre, Counter Terrorism Borders Policing and others.

Maurice, who knew a bit about speed, later invented the "Gatsometer", the world's first reliable speed-measuring device. Half a century of innovation and hard work have paid off -- for the hundreds of countries, states, counties, and cities that have chosen Gatso to provide their traffic enforcement systems.

Description:

Hard Shell offers a complete solution for personal protection products which includes, Personal Body Armor (Vests
and Helmets), Hard Armor Panels, Bomb Suppression Blanket, De-Mining Suits and Armored Vehicles, becoming a
complete solution provider for armed and police forces around the world.
Hard Shell skill of understanding the end-user requirement, the battlefield and special operations environments
along with technological capabilities with global reach help it to harness newer technologies to produce and identify
advanced equipment and gear for defense and security forces.
Hard Shell’s research and development team include experienced ex-defense personnel and security with
professional engineers that engage in design, development and testing of personal protection equipment in
laboratories, making sure the solution developed reacts accordingly on the battlefield.
We strive to provide our customers with the highest level of service possible, as well as offering help with every
aspect of using body armor. Ensuring every customer gets complete support throughout the order process, while

remains readily available to provide answers to any questions on body armor and to provide impartial advice on your
choice of body armor. We also provide in depth guides with details on all aspects of body armor, from choosing the
most suitable protection levels to selecting the correct sizes.

-Terrestrial laser scanners

We believe community policing is much more than that - it's a total philosophy. Members of the Department are encouraged to try new and otherwise non-traditional policing methods in working with the community and other government agencies.

The SACIA may also engage in covert actions at the appeal of law enforcement agencies. The Counter Intelligence Agency’s primary function is to collect information and to advise legal entities, public policymakers and law enforcement agencies. The agency conducts covert functions through its special law enforcement task team.

Name: SEROCU Cyber Choices
Description:
There is an acknowledged global skills gap looming, with an estimated shortfall of 1.8 million cyber security experts by 2020. The UK is in a prime place to fill much of that gap if we take the opportunity and divert talented young people into cyber careers.

We are Police Officers within the South East Regional Organised Crime Unit (SEROCU) - a joint unit covering the Hampshire, Thames Valley, Sussex and Surrey Police forces. We sit at the level between those forces and the National Crime Agency, dealing with complex and organised crime, alongside the delivery of niche and specialist capabilities to the forces. That’s where our Cyber Crime Unit fit in.

Our role as Police Officers working in the Cyber Choices team is:
* Educate young people about the dangers and penalties of getting involved in cyber * crime
* Educate young people about opportunities in cyber security
* Divert those on the cusp of committing cyber crime
* Manage the highest risk cyber offenders

We are reaching out to you in the hope that we might find partners within education, relevant agencies and industry who are willing and able to support our objectives. This may be by offering positive engagement and diversionary activity such as:

•	Work experience opportunities
•	Extra-curricular education
•	Apprenticeships
•	Mentoring

For those already working with young people we are also open to referrals for any person you are concerned may be at risk of cyber criminality, in order that we can intervene and provide positive diversion.

Should you be interested in working with us, please contact the team at:
CyberChoices@serocu.pnn.police.uk

Name: Pennsylvania State Constable
Description:
Constables in Pennsylvania are elected and serve a six-year term, they are Peace Officers by virtue of the office they hold, upon completing state certification and training, they may also serve as the Law Enforcement Arm of the Court. Constables primarily serve the District Courts but may also assist in serving the Common Pleas Court, when requested by the Sheriff.

Constables are elected at the municipal level, however, State law governs Constables.  They have statewide authority, thus, the title became "State Constable".
 
Constables are also charged with maintaining order at the election polls and ensuring that no qualified elector is obstructed from voting.  Constables are the only law enforcement officials permitted at the polls on election day.
 
While Constables primarily serve the Courts, they belong to the executive branch of government.

The program is designed to make law enforcement agencies and their officers more powerfully effective. 

To date, Sheriff Nash has trained over 10,000 law enforcement officials and government leaders from around the world in the principles of Police Dynamics, including the Romanian National Police, the Sierra Leone National Police and the Moscow Police College. He is currently serving in Afghanistan as the Rule of Law Coordinator for the International Police Coordination Board Secretariat under a contract with the US Department of State.

The official blog site for the company is www.PoliceDynamics.com where you will find over 100 character-based training videos for law enforcement and leadership...

Name: ACIMA, Asociación para la Comunicación e Información Medioambiental
Description:
Nuestra asociación se constituye en 1996 por 15 profesionales independientes del área de medio ambiente, como asociación sin ánimo de lucro de ámbito nacional,  con el fin de formar una red de profesionales interesados en potenciar y desarrollar el derecho acceso a la información en materia de medio ambiente,  reconocido en España por la Ley  38/1995 de 12 de diciembre.

Posteriormente, a partir de la ratificación en el 2005 por parte de España del Convenio  de la Comisión Económica para Europa de Naciones Unidas sobre acceso a la información , la participación del público en la toma de decisiones  y el acceso al a justicia en materia de medio ambiente, hecho en Aarhus (Dinamarca) el 25 de junio de 1998, conocido como Convenio de Aarhus,  el ámbito de actuación de ACIMA se amplió a los derechos de participación y acceso a la justicia, como instrumento básico para la defensa y protección del medio ambiente desde la ley.

El objetivo de ACIMA  es difundir, ejercitar  y facilitar el ejercicio de los tres derechos pilares del Convenio: Acceso a la Información y Transparencia, Participación pública y acceso a la Justicia en materia de medio ambiente.

Además, desde ACIMA estamos impulsando la creación de la Fundación Legal Natura con el fin de dar a conocer a la sociedad las herramientas jurídicas existentes y necesarias para la defensa del medio ambiente, en particular, el derecho de acceso a la información ambiental, el derecho a la participación ciudadana en la toma de decisiones que afectan el medio ambiente y el acceso a la justicia para la correcta aplicación de la legislación de protección ambiental, todo ello, a través de la innovación en la creación de nuevos instrumentos que fortalezcan su consecución.

Name: River Vale Police Department
Description:
On April 3, 1930, the River Vale Township Committee passed an ordinance creating the River Vale Police Department.  The ordinance called for the appointment of a Chief of Police and as many special officers as may be required for the enforcement of the law.  At the time, the population of River Vale was 871.  

A 1953 ordinance established the police department as it exists now, with full-time, salaried police officers.  As River Vale became more of a residential community, the population began to increase.  The size of the police department increased along with the population.  

By 1964, the department had grown to 13 full-time police officers.  The regular officers were assisted by the River Vale Police Reserves during times of emergencies.  The Police Reserves, which usually had a membership of 6-10 volunteers, was disbanded in the early 1980's.

Although the population has grown to over 10,500, the size of the police department remains at the manpower level established 25 years ago with 20 officers.  In addition to the current police officers, there have been 35 other officers employed by the Township.  Fifteen of these officers have retired from the department after 25-37 years of service.  The remaining 21 officers either resigned, were dismissed, or transferred to other law enforcement agencies.  As we enter the new millennium, the River Vale Police Department will continue its mission, "to provide the highest quality of service to its residents".

Name: Polícia Militar do Estado do Amazonas
Description:
The Military Police of Amazonas (PMAM) has a rich and varied history that began in Brazil's colonial period and has undergone numerous transformations up to the present day. The PMAM originated as the Police Guard in 1837, established by the President of the Province of Grão-Pará, General Soares de Andrea, with the mission of combating the Cabanos revolt. Initially, it comprised 1,339 men under the command of Lieutenant Colonel Albino dos Santos Pereira.

Over the years, the name and structure of the force have changed. In 1887, Law No. 761 reorganized the Police Corps of Amazonas. After the proclamation of the Republic in 1889, the Police Corps was dissolved and replaced by the Security Military Battalion through Decree No. 11 of 1890. The force was then called the State Military Regiment and Military Battalion.

In 1935, Law No. 55 reestablished the State Police Force, which finally, in 1938, became the Military Police of Amazonas. In 1969, Decree-Law No. 667 reorganized the Military Police and Fire Departments at the federal level, defining their functions of maintaining public order and internal security, as well as acting preventively with ostensive policing and repressively as a deterrent force.

The command of the Military Police is determined by the Governors of the States, Territories, and the Federal District, with approval from the Minister of the Army. In 1983, Decree No. 2.010 allowed the command to be exercised by an active Brigadier General or a Senior Combatant Officer, preferably of the rank of Colonel or Lieutenant Colonel. That same year, Colonel Hélcio Rodrigues Mota became the first General Commander of PMAM in a ceremony at Heliodoro Balbi Square.

PSC has come to light as a statutory organization in 2002 under the Police Staff College Act, 2002, through an auspicious inaugural by the Honourable Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina on 30th November, 2000. It is the first of its kind in the South Asia region.


PSC’s journey since its inception has gradually been very worthy and promising. It is now advancing its infrastructure facilities such as new eco-friendly academic hub and vertical extension of dormitory building etc. PSC is moving forward and endeavouring for excellence with a vision to establish it as the regional and global ‘think tank’.
 
PSC’s Vision, Mission, Values and Goals:

Vision: To enhance professionalism in policing through need based training and applied research

Mission: To develop human resources with sound capability and integrity to modernize policing within the framework of national development policy

Values: learning, innovation, readiness, integrity and teamwork.

Goals:

• To influence and enhance police leadership, prudence and professionalism 
• To establish innovative and work-based graduate learning programs 
• To provide value and insights gained from security, criminology and policing research to the national and international platforms 
• To promote mutual partnership and networking 
• To create a vibrant and quality learning environment

Name: West Midlands Police Federation
Description:
West Midlands Police Federation Joint Branch Board is a part of the Police Federation of England and Wales, which is the representative body for every Police officer below the rank of Superintendent. It was established by the Police Act 1919, to provide the Police with a means of bringing their views on welfare and the efficiency of the police service to the notice of the government and to the police authorities.

All the elected officials of the Police Federation must be serving police officers. Police officers are not permitted to belong to trades unions and are prohibited by regulations from taking any part in politics. The federation does, however, liaise with public sector trade unions and has excellent relationships with the TUC and all political parties. Under the provisions of the Police Act, the police are forbidden to strike, and must at all times obey the lawful orders of senior officers. Police pay, allowances, pensions and conditions of service are negotiated nationally in the Police Negotiating Board, which covers all police services within the United Kingdom.

The National Organization of Black Law Enforcement Executives NOBLE (Massachusetts Chapter)
Description:
History: 
The National Organization of Black Law Enforcement Executives (NOBLE) was founded in September 1976, during a three (3) – day symposium to address crime in urban low-income areas. The symposium was co-sponsored by the Police Foundation and the Law Enforcement Assistance Administration (LEAA). The Joint Center for Political Studies (JCPS) coordinated this unprecedented event in which sixty (60) top-ranking black law enforcement executives representing twenty – four (24) states and fifty – five (55) major cities gathered in the Washington, D.C. area to participate. They exchanged views about the critically high rate of crime in black urban communities and the socioeconomic conditions that lead to crime and violence. They raised questions about relevant issues such as fairness in the administration of justice, police-community relations, the hiring and promotion of black police officers, and the unique problems of the black police executives

NOBLE MASSACHUSETTS MISSION: 
To ensure equity in the administration of justice in the provision of public service to all communities, and to serve as the conscience of law enforcement by being committed to justice by action. We also strive to build a stronger rapport with the people within our communities, enhance professional development among our members, and establish robust support apparatuses for fellow law enforcement representatives that can be utilized in all facets of the profession.

Description:
The San Juan County Sheriff’s Office is a full service law enforcement agency and the primary agency for the unincorporated, non-reservation portion of San Juan County. This equates to an approximate land area of 2,000 square miles and a population of 127,000 people. Our entire team consists of 102 certified deputies and 25 civilian personnel as well as varying levels of volunteers and reserve deputies.

On Average the Sheriff’s Office handles approximately 55,000 calls for service ranging from homicides to vehicle collisions. The 102 certified deputies of SJCSO are a mixture of the following divisions: patrol, detectives, narcotics, aviation, civil and training.

Aside from promotions and divisional transfers, there are many opportunities for employee growth at the Sheriff’s Office, such as police instructor, SWAT team member, tactical flight officer, etc.

The main Sheriff’s Office is located in the county seat in Aztec, New Mexico. In addition to the main office, there are substations located in the Lee Acres area as well as the Kirtland area.

The LETA has a long history of developing and promoting the best practices in law enforcement training.  We offer realistic and effective training based on our extensive street policing experiences and rich martial arts backgrounds. 

Net proceeds of any training contracts through the not for profit Law Enforcement Training Association are applied to developing additional police judo programs for youth and law enforcement.

Name: AMERICANS FOR EFFECTIVE LAW ENFORCEMENT INC (AELE.org)
Description:
The Americans for Effective Law Enforcement, Inc. (AELE) is a resource center and legal educational provider dedicated to offering interested parties objective, timely, accurate, legal, scientific, and evidence-based information and operational guidance for the enhancement of the criminal justice community and to reduce potential criminal and civil liability of criminal justice professionals and their employers. 

AELE incorporated in 1966 and is a not-for-profit organization. In its initial charter it is dedicated to establish an “organized voice” for the law-abiding citizen regarding the United States’ crime problem, and to lend support to professional law enforcement. Growing and transitioning with changes in society and the demands of the law enforcement community, AELE is focused upon providing education seminars to law enforcement professionals, the attorneys who represent municipalities and their employees, public agency union representatives, and other interested parties. AELE is proud to offer the highest caliber of legal- scientific-, and evidence-based programs, including its Certified Litigation Specialist designation. 

Financial and Tax Status:
AELE's annual operating budget varies, based on the number of seminars and attendance.It is a not-for-profit educational corporation, with a 501(C)(3) designation by the I.R.S. Archived financial information, including our tax returns, is available on the Guidestar website.

Although AELE began as a membership organization, it no longer solicits members or contributions. 

AELE, as an independent non-partisan entity, does not solicit or receive grants from public or private sources.

Name: SVCI (Specialty Vehicle Concepts, Inc.)
Description:
Specialty Vehicle Concepts, Inc. (SVCI) was founded in February of 2005 by President and Owner, Matt Stehno.  Originating with the efforts and aspirations of a single manned operation, Matt began to seek out and take on projects from area police departments & fire departments. Based in Murray Utah, the company began to take its “baby steps” approach to building solid relationships with the local customer base.

Since the company’s inception, SVCI has grown from a one-man operation to an aspiring small business that employs several full time skilled craftsman and part time support staff.  Our current facility, located at 79 West 4500 South (on the Frontage Road) Suite # 1 in Murray Utah, is a 4400 sq. foot building, featuring 1200 sq of office / conference room / showroom space, and 3200 sq feet of shop space.

Our primary focus is in designing & up-fitting vehicle-based equipment solutions for law enforcement. We produce turnkey Covert Surveillance Vehicles, Mobile Command Vehicles / Trailers, SWAT Deployment Vehicles, and Incident Command Platforms.  We offer customized solutions to best meet the needs of each customer.

We also offer a wide variety of Covert and Overt Surveillance Products, in addition to Interview Room Management Solutions and Portable Communication Network Kits.

Name: PGR PROCURADURÍA GENERAL DE LA REPÚBLICA
Pengadilan Negeri Ungaran
Description:
Pengadilan Negeri Ungaran dulunya bernama Pengadilan Negeri Ambarawa yang berkedudukan di Jl. Mgr. Sugiyopranoto, Ngampin, Kecamatan Ambarawa. Kabupaten Semarang, Jawa Tengah. Dengan pertimbangan karena ibukota Kabupaten Semarang di Ungaran maka mulailah dirintis upaya untuk merealisasi perubahan status Pengadilan Negeri Ambarawa menjadi Pengadilan Negeri Kabupaten Semarang yang berkedudukan di Ungaran.

Usul kepindahan ini juga mengandung unsur pemikiran agar wilayah hukum Pengadilan Negeri Ambarawa nantinya bisa sama dengan wilayah daerah Kabupaten Semarang. Disamping itu, untuk memindahkan kantor tentu mrmbutuhkan dana, namun dengan dukungan dari berbagai pihak termasuk di dalamnya Bupati Semarang, maka dengan surat Keputusan Menteri Kehakiman Republik Indonesia tertanggal 17 September 1985, Nomor : M.03.AT.01.01 Tahun 1985 maka secara resmi disetujui pemindahan sekaligus perubahan status Pengadilan Negeri Ambarawa menjadi Pengadilan Negeri kabupaten Semarang yang berkedudukan di Ungaran atau yang biasa disebut Pengadilan Negeri Ungaran.

Pada tanggal 23 Juni 1986 dengan dihadiri pula oleh Ketua Mahkamah Agung Republik Indonesia Bpk. Ali Said, S.H , gedung Pengadilan Negeri Ungaran diresmikan oleh Menteri Kehakiman Republik Indonesia Bpk. Ismail Saleh, S.H. Dan sejak diresmikannya gedung Kantor Pengadilan Negeri Ungaran yang beralamat di Jl. Gatot Subroto No. 16 Ungaran, Kabupaten Semarang, Jawa Tengah maka segala kegiatan perkantoran Pengadilan Negeri Ambarawa telah beralih ke Gedung baru dengan nama baru pula.

Sedangkan gedung Pengadilan Negeri Ambarawa yang berkedudukan di Jl. Mgr. Sugiyopranoto, Ngampin, Kecamatan Ambarawa. Kabupaten Semarang, Jawa Tengah berdasarkan Surat Keputusan Kepala Kantor Wilayah Departemen Kehakiman Jawa Tengah tanggal 21 Agustus 1987 No. W9.PL.02.01-490 ditetapkan sebagai tempat Sidang Tetap ( Zitting Platzen ).

Terhitung sejak tanggal 13 Desember 2006 berdasarkan Surat Keputusan Kepala Badan Urusan Administrasi Mahkamah Agung Republik Indonesia Nomor : 46/BUA-PL/S-KEP/XII/2006 bangunan kantor lama Pengadilan Negeri Ambarawa dialih fungsi penggunaannya menjadi Kantor Pengadilan Agama Ambarawa.

Name: Bedford County Sheriff's Office
Description:
The men and women of the Bedford County Sheriff's Office are committed to the protection of life and property of the citizens and visitors of our county. We are committed to providing professional law enforcement, corrections, and criminal justice services through a partnership with our communities that builds trust, reduces crime, creates a safe environment to work and raise a family in, and enhances the quality of life for our citizens.

Our mission is profound because of its breadth, significance, and complexity. It is a diverse service, not a specific product producer. While other public and private organizations exist to protect life and property, they do not have the unique authority that law enforcement officers have to carry out the enforcement function, which is explicit and implicit in the statutory definitions of a peace officer, police officer, and law enforcement officer.

The Bedford County Sheriff's Office is comprised of employee’s who share a common belief and goal to provide the most adaptive, professional and responsive services possible to the public. To achieve this, we must accept and adhere to basic values that are a vital part of the Sheriff's Office and give us the spirit and direction to achieve our goals.

Description:
El Instituto de Derecho Empresario y Compliance es un espacio académico para el estudio, debate y promoción de todos los temas vinculados al Compliance, en particular con la Prevención del Lavado de Dinero, que se inserta dentro de las Actividades de la Asociación Argentina de Justicia Constitucional.
 
El objetivo es realizar charlas con expertos, articulando actividades que contribuyan a la formación de Recursos Humanos especializados orientados a fortalecer la integridad del sistema financiero argentino.
 
Objetivos específicos:
a) Promover la investigación en el campo de las disciplinas. 
b) Proponer normativas y analizar el alcance e interpretación de aquellas normas relacionadas con la materia. 
c) Contribuir a la formación de Recursos Humanos especializados. 
d) Articular las actividades que requieran un análisis multidisciplinario convocando la participación de investigadores, docentes, profesionales, especialistas y estudiantes que aporten conocimientos en la materia. 
e) Publicar información relevante en la materia.
 
Por cualquier consulta diríjase a instituto@idec.ar

Name: Independent Police Complaints Council
Description:
The Independent Police Complaints Council (IPCC) is an independent statutory body established under the Independent Police Complaints Council Ordinance (Cap. 604, Laws of Hong Kong) to observe, monitor and review the handling and investigation of Reportable Complaints against the Police by the Commissioner of Police. The Police have a statutory duty to comply with the IPCC’s requests.

The IPCC comprises a Chairman, three Vice-Chairmen and no less than eight Members, all appointed by the Chief Executive. They are drawn from a wide spectrum of society including the legal, medical, education, social welfare, and business sectors and Legislative Council Members. This composition enables the IPCC to draw upon the diverse expertise of its Members to monitor CAPO’s investigation of complaints against the Police in an independent, impartial and thorough manner. As of 31 March 2017, the IPCC comprises 28 Members.

Name: Europol Alumni Consulting Network (EACN)
Description:
We are a group of ex-Europol officers who have come together to pool our resources and talents in order to better collectively serve the clients that we each currently work with on an individual basis. Our network members have capability in all of the crime areas that fall within Europe’s mandate, with specific projects to date including:
- Drug trafficking
- People smuggling
- Terrorism
- Trafficking in persons
- War crimes
- Financial crimes and money-laundering
- Cybercrime

In addition, we understand and promote  the procedures and processes that facilitate Europol’s work within the wider EU-law enforcement community, including:
- International law-enforcement cooperation
- Intelligence-led policing
- Intelligence analysis, including threat- and risk-assessment
- Information management and data protection

Our services are available to assist at all stages of the project life-cycle;
- Initiation: the planning and preparation for action, including needs-assessments and performance indicators
- Implementation: putting plans into action, including on-the-spot mentoring and hands-on assistance
- Evaluation: the post-op measurement of success and efficiency, including highlighting of lessons-learned

It will be seen from the above that we have the capability of operate at the strategic, operational and tactical level. Furthermore, it is worth remembering that Europol is recognised as a state-of-the-art centre of excellence, and therefore the services offered by EACN will assist clients in aspiring to this high standard. 

N.B. 
- It should be clear that EACN does not participate in, nor have access to, Europol’s ongoing operations or data.
- All EACN members possess full security clearance from their home Member State.

Name: Mission 39A
Description:
Mission 39A is an an online non-profit, non-partisan portal that makes legal aid available and accessible to all individuals. Mission39A walks the justice journey with its users, who can now access legal information and advice at any time of the day or night. From the comfort of their own palms, Indians can learn about the law and how it could affect their everyday decisions – both big and small.

Mission 39A is inspired by Article 39-A of the Constitution, a provision that furthers the intertwined values of equal justice and equal opportunity by removing economic and social barriers. These are constitutional values of immense importance, given the manner in which multiple disparities intersect to exclude vast sections of our society from effectively accessing justice.

Name: Laboratoire ChemTox
Description: Fondé en 2003 sous la direction du Dr Vincent CIRIMELE, expert toxicologue agréé par la Cour de cassation et près la Cour d’Appel de Colmar, ChemTox est un laboratoire spécialisé en toxicologie analytique, reconnu pour son expertise dans l'analyse des matrices biologiques, notamment capillaires. Fort d’une équipe composée de plus de 20 ingénieurs et de techniciens, ChemTox a pour objectif de révéler la vérité à travers la Science. Le laboratoire s'est imposé comme une référence incontournable en France, et est également reconnu à l’International dans le domaine de l'analyse toxicologique capillaire. ChemTox a dans son arsenal des méthodes de spectrométrie de masse (LC-MS/MS, GC-MS/MS) pour la détection et la quantification de substances psychoactives, drogues et autres toxiques. ChemTox investit dans la R&D et l’amélioration continue de ses procédés afin de garantir des résultats toujours plus fiables et, dans des délais plus courts.

Name: Directorate of Prosecution, Uttar Pradesh
Description:
The Criminal Procedure Code (Amendment) Act, 2005 has brought about several changes, including a statutory Directorate of Prosecution and the Criminal Procedure Code (Amendment) Bill, 2006 promises more major reforms, based mainly on the Malimath Committee on reforms of the criminal justice system. Therefore, the role of this Directorate is to ensure and facilitate effective and competent conduct of Prosecution so as to bring the offenders to justice and to upkeep the faith of the public in our justice delivery system.

The Mission of the Directorate of Prosecution is to fulfil its constitutional mandate by providing the people of Uttar Pradesh with an independent, professional and effective prosecution service that operates with integrity, inspires public trust and confidence and safeguards the administration of justice throughout the State of Uttar Pradesh.

The Directorate of Prosecution, Uttar Pradesh, is working under the Department of Home, Govt. of Uttar Pradesh in linkage with the Department of Prosecution, Ministry of Home Affairs, Govt. of India. The key function of the Directorate is to streamline the conduct of the prosecution, for and on behalf of the State, before the Criminal Courts and to monitor the performance of the Prosecutors working in various sub-ordinate Courts in the State.

To establish an effective and impartial prosecution in State, the Directorate of Prosecution in Uttar Pradesh has been established in 1980.

Name: Western States Information Network
Description:
The mission of the Regional Information Sharing Systems (RISS) Program is to assist local, state, federal, and tribal criminal justice partners by providing adaptive solutions and services that facilitate information sharing, support criminal investigations, and promote officer safety. RISS has been supporting the law enforcement and criminal justice communities for 48 years.

RISS is composed of six regional centers and the RISS Technology Support Center (RTSC). RISS works regionally and on a nationwide basis to respond to the unique crime problems of each region while strengthening the country’s information sharing environment. More than 9,600 local, state, federal, and tribal law enforcement and public safety agencies are members of RISS. RISS is used and trusted by hundreds of thousands of law enforcement officers and criminal justice professionals in all 50 states, the District of Columbia, U.S. territories, England, New Zealand, and parts of Canada.

Name: Uniunea Națională a Notarilor Publici din România
Description:
Uniunea Națională a Notarilor Publici din România (UNNPR) este organizația profesională care reprezintă notarii publici la nivel național, având un rol esențial în garantarea siguranței circuitului civil. 
Înființată prin Legea nr. 36/1995, UNNPR este o organizație de interes public, cu personalitate juridică, patrimoniu și buget propriu, care reglementează și susține activitatea notarială în România.
 Misiunea noastră este de a asigura respectarea principiilor de legalitate, imparțialitate și confidențialitate în serviciile notariale, contribuind astfel la protecția drepturilor și intereselor cetățenilor, mediului de afaceri si statului. 
 Susținem perfecționarea continuă a notarilor publici prin programe de formare profesională, schimb de bune practici și colaborări naționale și internaționale.

Além da representatividade classista, o IEPTB-MS gerencia o sistema chamado Central de Remessa de Arquivos – CRA, ferramenta que conecta usuário do serviço de protesto com tabelioantos de forma direta e ágil, por meio de plataforma eletrônica segura.

A Diretoria do IEPTB-MS, juntamente com Tabeliães sul-mato-grossenses, atingiram no ano de 2016 a adesão de 100% das comarcas dentro da CRA, permitindo remessas eletrônicas de títulos privados e públicos, com excelentes índices de recuperação de crédito.
Location: Campo Grande, Mato Grosso do Sul

Name: California State Division of the International Association for Identification
Description:
◦This organization is a not-for-profit, professional, association dedicated to the application of the forensic sciences in the Law Enforcement and Criminal Justice communities as well as the private sector.  The Division's charter was granted in August of 1916 making us the first Division to hold a charter under the parent body (IAI).  Our membership numbers in excess of 650 persons throughout the United States with the majority of  members working in the State of California.

◦To bring persons, who are actively engaged in the profession of Identification and Scientific Investigation, into an organized body so that the profession, in all of its disciplines, may be administered and practiced in an ethical and effective manner.

◦To encourage the improvement of all disciplines of Forensic Identification and Investigation by fostering and supporting the need for original research, refinements of current methodology and professional training.

◦To promote the sharing and dissemination of information and knowledge by sponsoring training seminars, publishing papers and articles and encouraging members to openly discuss all aspects of their profession with their peers and others seeing knowledge and skill.

◦To set the highest standards of ethical, scientific and professional conduct for its members and to make those who command, manage or supervise within the Law Enforcement and Criminal Justice communities aware of these standards and encourage their adoption in these communities.

◦To inspire in our members a professionals pride and commitment to serve Law Enforcement, the Criminal Justice System and society in general by applying their knowledge and skill to the best of their abilities.

Name: Middle Tennessee State University Police Department
Description: The University Police Department at Middle Tennessee State University is a fully functioning law enforcement agency that is open and operating 24 hours a day, 7 days a week in order to provide police services to the MTSU campus. The Police Department is part of the University’s Division of Business and Finance and reports directly to the Vice President of Business and Finance.

Name: Toxic Boss Blues
Description:
Have you ever been bullied at work,micromanaged by a tyrant, or worked for a manager who struggled with telling the truth? Have you ever had the misfortune of reporting to an incompetent executive who can't seem to find their way out of a wet paper sack, had to answer to a yes sir, yes sir, three bags full foreman, or take direction from an egghead administrator who had no business being in charge?

With law enforcement as his backdrop, Steve Neal takes you inside the world of noxious mismanagement, exposing the consequences of toxic supervisory behavior. Straight talk from a tough cop, makes Toxic Boss Blues a must read for anyone who has ever tangled with a difficult supervisor.

Name: Fayetteville Arkansas Police Department
Description:
On behalf of the men and women of the Fayetteville Police Department, I would like to welcome you to our official website.  Our goal is to provide our community with as much information as possible about their police department, and the programs and services we provide to our visitors and citizens.  It is my honor and privilege to serve as the Chief of Police and represent all the members of this department who are dedicated to professionalism, customer service, and safety.  

Community policing is at the heart of our organization, and we are involved in a number of activities beyond responding to calls for service, traffic enforcement, and criminal investigations.  We value our community partnerships and strive to achieve the cooperation, trust, and confidence of those we serve. The Fayetteville Police Department is dedicated to reaching the highest standards of professionalism through the use of the most current policies, procedures, training and technology. Our team works hard to maintain a level of excellence, which is exhibited by our national and state accreditation through the Commission on Accreditation for Law Enforcement Agencies (link is external) (CALEA) and the Arkansas Law Enforcement Accreditation Program (ALEAP). 

Our primary responsibilities are to provide safety to our community, and educate our citizens with ways of reducing opportunities for crime to occur.  The men and women of the Fayetteville Police Department work hard each day to fulfill that mission through the various services we provide.  Thank you for your interest in our website, and I encourage you to visit each page to learn about the programs and/or resources that may be of interest to you.  If you need further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact a member of our department.  

Mike Reynolds | Chief of Police

Name: Legal Informatic Project
Description:
Med opbakning fra SDU's direktions strategiske satsning i fyrtårnet ODEx og stor støtte fra eksterne aktører, har Juridisk Institut indgået et 4-årigt samarbejde med en bred række aktører, herunder bl.a. institut for Matematik & Datalogi i Legal Informatic Project (LiP).

I projektet vil instituttet intet mindre end at vække retsinformatikken i Danmark. Fra alskens vinkler og i samarbejde med regionens aktører vil forskerne arbejde med de retlige rammer for civiliseret udvikling og brug af moderne teknologier. Resultaterne vil blive integreret i alle uddannelser, så SDU også i de digitale 2020´ere kan uddanne de bedst udrustede studerende med de stærkeste kompetencer.

Af initiativer i Legal Informatic Project kan bl.a. nævnes:
- Certifikat i persondataret. 
- Valgfag i persondataret. 
- Igangsættelse af et Ph.D.-projekt. 
- Som en del af LIP, vil der blive tilbudt kurser i retsinformatic på alle fakulteter
- Post.Doc. Samarbejde om et 2-årigt projekt på Institut for Matematik og Datalogi, Naturvidenskabeligt Fakultet, om de fremtidige EU-retlige rammer for brug af sundhedsdata i projektet VIND.
- Persondataretligt netværk Syddanmark. 
- Udarbejdelse af en lærebog i persondataret til brug for kurser i persondataret.
- En række konferencer vil blive afholdt i 2017 og 2018. Bl.a. er der allerede afholdt en kommunalretlig konference med overskriften "Digitalisering i kommunerne" d. 10. maj 2017 i Odense Adelige Jomfrukloster, SDU, med et flot fremmøde og inspirerende oplæg fra bl. a. Karsten Loiborg, kontorchef ved Folketingets Ombudsmand. 
... Mange flere initiativer er på vej. 

Se også www.sdu.dk/lip 

Sagt med andre ord: Når robotterne kommer, vil SDU være klar til at støtte vores region – også på juraen.

Legal Informatic Project er samlet af lektor Hanne Marie Motzfeldt og lektor Carina Risvig Hamer. Projektet er forankret i Juridisk Instituts spydsspids, Erhvervsforvaltningsret, der ledes af professor Bent Ole Gram Mortensen.

Of the 28 officers, the Department has a Chief and eight staff officers consisting of two Commanders and six Lieutenants, including two Detective Lieutenants. For the specialty positions, one officer is assigned to narcotics investigations and the West Central Wisconsin Drug Task Force and two officers are Youth Services Officers and juvenile crime investigators. 

The Department has 7 civilian support personnel that work in areas such as records, court coordinating and general administrative duties.

Name: Wisconsin Association of Women Police
Description:
The Wisconsin Association of Women Police (WAWP) is a professional organization created by women in the law enforcement profession. Founded in 1983, the WAWP is governed by a set of by-laws and is managed by a Board of Directors, representative of all regions of the State of Wisconsin. The organization strives to enhance the professional image and recognition of women in law enforcement and related work.

The WAWP is the Wisconsin Chapter of the International Association of Women Police (IAWP). The IAWP was established in Los Angeles, California USA in 1915 by the first American policewomen as an international organization to provide professional development, mentoring, training, networking, and recognition for female law enforcement officers and civilian support staff, as well as increase the number of women in policing. One-hundred years later, IAWP has members in more than 60 countries and continues the same traditions established by the founders.

Name: Maple Heights Police Department
Description:
The Maple Heights Police Department serves the public through the fair and impartial enforcement of laws and the delivery of professional police services.

Mission

“It is the mission of the Maple Heights Police Department and its employees to serve the public through the fair and impartial enforcement of laws and the delivery of professional police services. It is our mission to perform these tasks in a manner that inspires the public’s trust and confidence in our department and promotes a positive feeling of safety and security to all members of our community.

The standards by which we judge the achievement of our mission objectives are found in the professional standards established by our Oath of Office, the Professional Code of Police Ethics, the Maple Heights Police Department Core Values and the administrative directives of the Maple Heights Police Department.”

Description

The Maple Heights Police Department is currently staffed with 35 full time sworn Police Officers. The City also has an Auxiliary Police force to handle situations such as traffic control, special events, and community-oriented activities. The Department supports a dispatch center which services both the Police and Fire Departments. The dispatch center is equipped with the most up-to-date equipment available to include the enhanced 9-1-1 system, TDD machine and high band radio frequency. 

The Police Department, in partnership with other area communities, supports several specialized units. These units include a fully equipped SWAT Team, a Bomb Unit, a Hostage Negotiations Unit, a Narcotics Unit and an Emergency Command Post Team. The Department also sponsors a DARE Program for all 6th Grade classes at the local schools. 

A cash reward is offered to anonymous persons who telephone the Crime Stoppers hot line with information which led to the arrest and indictment of the person(s) responsible for felony offenses. The local hot line number is: (216) 252-7463.

Description:
A ASREB-SP - Associação dos Sargentos da Reserva do Exército Brasileiro em São Paulo é uma associação de direito privado que representa os sargentos em situação de reserva do Exército Brasileiro no estado de São Paulo. 

A associação tem como objetivo congregar e defender os interesses dos seus membros, promovendo a integração, o apoio social e o desenvolvimento profissional dos sargentos da reserva. 

A ASREB-SP atua como representante dos sargentos da reserva perante as autoridades e órgãos militares, bem como perante a sociedade civil.

A associação busca defender os direitos e benefícios dos sargentos da reserva, como salários, aposentadorias, saúde e outros.

Promove eventos de integração e atividades sociais para fortalecer o espírito de corpo e o apoio entre os membros.

A ASREB-SP oferece cursos, palestras e outras atividades para auxiliar os sargentos da reserva na transição para a vida civil e na busca por oportunidades profissionais. 

A ASREB-SP possui uma diretoria, conselhos e departamentos que atuam para alcançar os objetivos da associação. 

Endereço:

Rua Tocantins, 654 - Cidade Sao Pedro - Gleba A, Santana De Parnaíba-SP. 

CEP: 06535-100

Informações:
E-mail: asrebsp1contato@gmail.com

Interessados em se associar ou obter mais informações sobre a ASREB-SP procurem os contatos da associação.

Our police officers are determined to protect and support individual rights while providing for the security of persons and property. Name: Iowa Civil Rights Commission
Description: The Iowa Civil Rights Commission is a neutral, fact-finding law enforcement agency. The mission of the Iowa Civil Rights Commission is to end discrimination within the state of Iowa. To achieve this goal, the ICRC must effectively enforce the Iowa Civil Rights Act. The Commission's primary duty is to enforce state and federal laws that prohibit discrimination in employment, public accommodations, housing, education and credit by investigating and litigating civil rights complaints. The Commission also provides conflict resolution services including mediation and conciliation for civil rights matters. In addition to its role as a law enforcement agency, the Commission works to prevent discrimination by providing training and education to the public.

We operate a “no frills” jail that meets all humanitarian needs in a fiscally responsible manner.

We are committed to make Phelps County a better place to live and an uncomfortable place for people who break the law.

Name: WeNeverWalkAlone
Description:
Sworn Law Enforcement Officers (LEO) experience a lot of stress and trauma due to the nature of the work. Like everyone, they are also susceptible to issues not directly related to work but affecting the desired performance expectation at work.

Des Plaines Police Department has spearheaded this effort to connect LEO with Peer Support Officers and vetted mental health professionals across the nation. The grand vision of the product is to serve as a one stop shopping place for LEO to obtain varied treatment options, educational material, external sources, real time interaction with advisors and peers, share survival stories, access lifestyle information, retreat information and much more.

This endeavor is a pioneering effort and a first of its kind in the nation. It is modern, engaging, relevant, beautiful, user friendly and highly efficient. Mobile Apps for iPhone and Android devices are available for convenience.

Please make the best of this system.

The organization also provides comprehensive training in effective street crime enforcement for patrol officers and those assigned to proactive enforcement units.

The training provided by Blue Line Academy stems from the extensive hands-on, real-world experience of the staff.  The information provided is up-to-date, accurate, and relevant to members of the law enforcement, correctional, and prosecutorial communities. 

The owner of Blue Line Academy LLC has been a sworn law enforcement officer for more than twenty-five years, the majority of which was spent conducting criminal investigations including special investigations of criminal gangs, transnational criminal organizations, and homeland security threats.  Through his hands-on experience, the owner has developed comprehensive training programs in a number of areas designed for law enforcement of every level. Description: The MPS GCA is the only formally recognised and approved Staff Support Association for Greek & Cypriot Police Officers & Staff in the Metropolitan Police Service (MPS). Name: Akwesasne Mohawk Police Service
Description:
The Akwesasne Mohawk Police Service (AMPS) has been in existence since the 1960’s. In 1971, the Band Council passed a Band Council Resolution to call the police service the “St.Regis Akwesasne Police Department”. In 1973 the St. Regis Akwesasne Police Officers were accepted into the Ontario Police College.
In 1986, Band Council passed another resolution to rename the St.Regis Akwesasne Police Department, to the Akwesasne Mohawk Police.

Today the Akwesasne Mohawk Police Service has 53 members; including 38 sworn members, 10 civilian members and 5 seconded officers.  The Akwesasne Mohawk Police Service is a stand alone First Nation Police service and has jurisdiction and policing authority in both Quebec, and Ontario.

MISSION STATEMENT
The mission of the Akwesasne Mohawk Police Service (AMPS) is the enhance peace, harmony, justice and cultural values through-out Akwesasne. In addition, the Akwesasne Mohawk Police strive to honor and respect all living things and to acquire the highest level of professionalism and accountability in servicing the community members of Akwesasne and surrounding areas.

SERVICES PROVIDED

General Uniform Patrol
Community Policing
Joint Investigation Team (JIT)
Criminal Investigations
Emergency Dispatch Services
Traffic Law Enforcement
Marine Patrol Services
Anti-Drug Enforcement
Anti-Smuggling Enforcement
Victims Assistance
Emergency Response

Name: The Shooting Institute
Description:
The Shooting Institute is a Christian-based organization – furthering the name of Christ in a dark world is our most important mission. As expertly-trained shooters and instructors, we are capable of helping you achieve your goals to become a better shooter, operator, or officer. 

The Shooting Institute is a verified Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business, based in Birmingham, AL. Jared Hudson, Founder and CEO of The Shooting Institute (TSI) has combined his years of experience as a Navy SEAL Sniper and Assaulter, Contractor and Law Enforcement Officer
to develop a highly effective method of Firearms and Tactical training that has benefited multiple US Military, Law Enforcement and Corporate clients.

All of TSI’s training is based on real-world experience – not theory.

TSI’s Training Cadre consists of current and former Military and L.E. Tier One Operators, including SEALs and other SOF members, Veteran SWAT and L.E. Officers, as well as Subject Matter Experts. Project Teams are assembled on a job-specific basis in order to provide training and instruction that is second to none. TSI can travel to conduct training on-site or host your agency in central Alabama.

At our courses, each shooter will leave understanding the Fundamentals of Marksmanship and how they can be used effectively and efficiently in combat situations. Whether you are civilian, military, or law enforcement – our courses will help you and your team become better shooters and tacticians. We look forward to training with you soon.

For more information, contact us via email at Admin(at)TheShootingInstitute.com

Name: M-NCPPC Park Police, Prince George's County Division
Description:
The Maryland-National Capital Park Police, Prince George’s County Division consists of 125 authorized sworn personnel, 29 civilian employees and approximately 40 volunteers.

The Maryland-National Capital Park Police is a full service law enforcement agency tasked with protecting approximately 27,000 acres of park property in Prince George’s County, as well as those who visit and staff M-NCPPC sites. Today’s M-NCPPC properties in Prince George's County include historic sites, regional parks, sports arenas, community centers, arts venues, aquatic facilities, ice skating rinks, museums, natural areas, trails and other types of recreation amenities. 

The Park Police Division consists of Patrol Services, Investigative Services, and Support Operations.  Methods of patrol and specialty units include motorcycle, mountain bike, K-9, horse mounted, and T-3.  The Park Police Division is very Community-oriented, and has numerous programs providing positive interaction between the police, citizens, and youth in the community.

Name: Allen Park Police Department
Description: Established in 1957, the Allen Park Police Department is a community-based police department dedicated to serving our citizens any way possible.  Allen Park enjoys a rich history that spans back to the late 19th century.  From its beginning as a farming district, Allen Park has always been known as a vital, productive community with a small-town atmosphere.  The city lost none of its friendliness even when industry became the main source of income and the pace quickened substantially.  We are proud of our city's ability to keep sight of what is important while responding positively to inevitable changes.  It takes unified cooperation on the part of each citizen to accomplish this optimistic drive.  Therefore, the Allen Park Police Department's mission is to provide professional, progressive policing services to its citizens through cooperative partnerships, diverse programs and sensitivity to the changing needs of our community.

Name: True Polygraph LLC
Description:
True Polygraph LLC., offers full polygraph services to Private, Businesses and Government Departments including, but not limited to pre-employment, criminal investigations, private attorney matters (defendants or victims), divorce, private and family matters or disputes, civil issues, infidelity/cheating, juvenile issues, child custody, and employee or workplace misconduct. 

David Sifling is the owner and President of True Polygraph LLC., and a retired Police Officer with over 24 years of experience. He graduated from the Marston Polygraph Academy in San Bernardino, California which is accredited by the American Polygraph Association (APA). He holds an Advanced Certificate from the California Commission on Peace Officer Standards and Training (P.O.S.T.). He has extensive experience in criminal investigations as a detective utilizing several specialized assignment skills gained during his career as a law enforcement officer and polygraph examiner. 

True Polygraph LLC uses only Certified Polygraph examiners that have attended and passed an accredited APA (American Polygraph Association) polygraph academy course. True Polygraph LLC examiners only use the latest techniques and computerize instruments such as the Lafayette and CPS Pro Polygraph Instruments (lie detectors). We are Governed by, and are a member of the American Polygraph Association, and respect and adhere to their professional code of conduct. We administer and provide Polygraph or “Lie Detector” testing across Southern California with a professional and personal approach.  We combine a wealth of experience, knowledge and high-level execution into each polygraph test conducted, while highly respecting your privacy.

Reach out by giving us a call or sending a message today so we can meet your polygraph needs!

Call or text True Polygraph LLC at: 909-921-9126

Name: Politiezone Grensleie
Description:
De politiezone 'Grensleie' is een meergemeentezone bestaande uit de 3 fusiegemeenten Menen, Wevelgem en Ledegem. De zone bevindt zich in de Leievallei in het zuiden van West-Vlaanderen.

De politiezone grenst via Menen aan het buurland Frankrijk.

De zone behelst een oppervlakte van 96,6 km² en telt 73.549 inwoners, wat een bevolkingsdichtheid van 761 inwoners per km² betreft.

Het wegennetwerk in de politiezone is zeer dicht, waaronder 3 autosnelwegen (A19, E403 en E17). De spoorlijn Kortrijk-Poperinge doorsnijdt het landschap. In Wevelgem is er een internationale luchthaven te midden de industriezones. De Leie is een belangrijke ader voor de scheepvaart zowel op het vlak van industrie als voor het toerisme.

Het zijn vooral die verbindingswegen en de nabijheid van de Franse grens die een rol spelen in het leefbaarheids- en veiligheidsbeeld van de zone.

The Merced County Probation Department is responsible for the background investigation of offenders and the preparation of social history reports for the Merced County Superior Courts. Established in 2024 by a dedicated group of Greek police officers, P.L.E.I.A. was created with the vision of consistently improving the quality and effectiveness of security and survival training for all Law Enforcement personnel. This non-profit, non-political, and non-governmental organization has earned official recognition and stands as a vital resource for the Law Enforcement training community.
P.L.E.I.A. serves as a collaborative platform for various authorities, including the Police, Fire Brigade, Coast Guard, Customs, Judiciary, and community services, fostering communication on both national and international levels. At its core, the organization prioritizes the advancement of techniques, tactics, and knowledge critical to officer survival training.
Through partnerships with universities, higher education institutions, and security experts from Greece and abroad, P.L.E.I.A. is actively conducting research on preventative security measures. It also focuses on the development, training, and evaluation of defensive techniques and tactics, ensuring alignment with global security regulations and standards.
As a hub for Law Enforcement Instructors, P.L.E.I.A. welcomes members from across the EU and beyond, encouraging the exchange of ideas and best practices with specialists in officer safety and field training. Its events, including training conferences, seminars, workshops, and member meetings, provide unparalleled opportunities for professional growth and collaboration.
Additionally, P.L.E.I.A. partners with international organizations, security education businesses, and institutions specializing in adult learning, further enriching the training landscape for Law Enforcement professionals.
P.L.E.I.A. is not just an organization—it is a movement led by police trainers, committed to elevating the standards of Law Enforcement training and ensuring the safety of those who serve and protect.

Description:

Goals

1.	Facilitate a pathway for legal representation, on a national level, for Black 
        officers who find themselves: 
•	Under investigation for disrupting the actions of a fellow officer against 
        members of the community, particularly the Black community;
•	Experiencing documented mistreatment or discriminatory behavior based 
        on race and gender; and
•	With subpar representation from a union or other employing entity.

2.	Provide guidance and evidence-based options for re-imagining public safety 
        that:
•	Reduces racially-biased interactions in the community; 
•	Prioritizes and invests in the Black community;
•	Redefines the role of (active) Black officers; and 
•	Supports officers who choose to advocate on behalf of their community, 
        themselves, or fellow Black officers.

Name: Petros Mavrakis Law Office
Description:
As a Lawyer & Compliance Officer based in Paphos, Cyprus, I have developed a multi-dimensional professional profile that combines legal science with regulatory compliance and financial crime prevention. My experience ranges from representing individuals and businesses in corporate, commercial, real estate, contract, immigration and employment law matters, to the specialized implementation of AML/CTF procedures for multinational clients and local entities.

I hold a Law Degree (LLB) from Canterbury Christ Church University (CCCU), Kent, UK and am a member of the Cyprus Bar Association. I am also a certified professional by the International Compliance Association (ICA) with a specialization in Anti-Money Laundering, which allows me to combine legal strategy with international standard regulatory compliance practices.

Areas of Expertise:

Corporate & Commercial Law: company formation and restructuring, cross-border transactions, articles of association, corporate governance.

Real Estate & Planning: sales, purchases, transfers, reduced VAT 5%, title control, development and lease agreements.

Contract Law: drafting/reviewing contracts (sale, shareholders, trusts, inheritance, IP agreements).

Immigration Law: permanent residence applications through the Cyprus Investment Programme and fast-track procedures.

Regulatory Compliance & AML: KYC/EDD, risk profiling, suspicious transaction reporting (STR), compliance audits, liaison with supervisory authorities.

Tax & Regulatory Matters: capital gains, VAT, advice to individuals and legal entities.

Professional Approach:

I combine legal precision, regulatory acumen and business strategy to provide comprehensive solutions that protect my clients and strengthen their position in a complex international environment. Whether it is a highly complex corporate structure, a complex AML process or contested legal cases, my emphasis is on methodicality, transparency and results.

JDLSA, operating under the Rajasthan State Legal Services Authority (RSLSA), aims to ensure access to justice for all, particularly vulnerable groups like women, children, and marginalized communities. The clinic supports the fight for social justice through awareness campaigns, legal literacy programs, and pro bono legal representation in court.

JDLSA is part of a broader framework overseen by the National Legal Services Authority (NALSA), which was established under the Legal Services Authorities Act, 1987. NALSA promotes an inclusive legal system and organizes Lok Adalats, legal literacy camps, and mediation services across India to resolve disputes amicably. The Rajasthan Legal Services Authority works closely with District Legal Services Authorities like JDLSA to implement these initiatives locally, ensuring that justice is accessible and equitable.

Description:
Centrul Naţional Anticorupţie (denumit în continuare Centru) este un organ specializat în prevenirea şi combaterea corupţiei, a actelor conexe corupţiei şi a faptelor de comportament corupţional.

Cadrul juridic
Cadrul juridic al activităţii Centrului îl constituie Constituţia Republicii Moldova, Legea Nr. 1104 din 06.06.2002 cu privire La Centrul Național Anticorupție, alte acte normative, precum şi tratatele internaţionale la care Republica Moldova este parte.

Atribuţiile
În conformitate cu articolul 4 din Legea Nr. 1104 din 06.06.2002 cu privire La Centrul Național Anticorupție, În atribuţiile Centrului intră: 
-   prevenirea, depistarea, cercetarea şi curmarea contravenţiilor şi infracţiunilor de corupţie şi a celor conexe corupţiei, precum şi a faptelor de comportament corupţional;
-   efectuarea expertizei anticorupţie a proiectelor de acte legislative şi a proiectelor de acte normative ale Guvernului, precum şi a altor iniţiative legislative prezentate în Parlament, în vederea corespunderii lor cu politica statului de prevenire şi combatere a corupţiei;
-   efectuarea analizei operaţionale şi strategice a actelor de corupţie, a actelor conexe corupţiei şi a faptelor de comportament corupţional, a informaţiei privind studiile analitice ale fenomenului corupţiei.
-   efectuarea evaluării integrităţii instituţionale în condiţiile Legii nr. 325 din 23 decembrie 2013 privind evaluarea integrităţii instituţionale, monitorizarea implementării planurilor de integritate şi aprecierea progreselor realizate;
-    recuperarea bunurilor infracționale.
Atribuțiile Centrului sunt exhaustive şi nu pot fi modificate sau completate decât prin lege.

Name: Florida Law Enforcement Property Recovery Unit [FLEPRU]
Description:
In May of 1987, a group of property detectives within the Tampa Bay area, formed the first property recovery unit in the United States. They gathered in an attempt to answer an age old problem plaguing law enforcement, “How can law enforcement increase the recovery of stolen property and place the guilty in jail?” Detectives realized that property crimes were continually increasing and the resources needed to attack this investigation focused on the thief and his acquired or stolen property. This detectives realized the need to utilize the “Dynamic” approach which focuses on property rather than relying on the arrest of the criminal. The detectives realized that a “Fence” was a major part of the problem and by using a “Fence” the criminal was able to convert the stolen property to cash. The “Fence” would bear the burden of selling the property to new property owners. Sometimes the new owners were unsuspecting of the properties origin, due to the reputation of the seller.
This group recognized that information exchange on a statewide level was necessary to effectively track stolen goods, match recovered goods to victims and apprehend criminals. The group decided to formally organize the “Florida Law Enforcement Property Recovery Unit” (FLEPRU), as the group is known, has divided the state into five regions, each have a District Director.
Each director is responsible for providing members and other Law Enforcement Agencies with local, state and federal intelligence information. FLEPRU holds statewide training seminars and conferences annually. FLEPRU seminars utilize guest instructors such as state legislators, judges, private sector executives and experts in the field of property investigation and recovery. FLEPRU membership is open to all law enforcement agencies and personnel along with approved non-law enforcement investigators, such as in the insurance industry. FLEPRU has no annual membership fees and is a non-profit corporation.
