Name: Guard911
Description:
Why Guard911?
The number of active shooters in our country is increasing every year. The average workplace shooting lasts only 10 minutes. The only true defense against this threat is to reduce law enforcement response time to these incidents. 

How It Works
Guard911 app technology is simple and powerful. Approved staff download the app to their mobile phones. In the event of extreme workplace violence featuring an armed intruder or active shooting, approved staff members press the panic button provided in the app. An alert is immediately sent to the mobile phones of all participating federal, state and local law enforcement officers, ON and OFF duty, in close proximity to the property. The app simultaneously speed dials 911 and connects the staff member to emergency services. All other staff on the property with the app installed are also immediately alerted of the emergency, and a map of the location of the initial alert is displayed on their mobile phones. The map provides situational awareness to your staff, which allows them to react accordingly.

Name: Trans Doe Task Force
Description:
The Trans Doe Task Force was created to research cold cases in which the subject may have been transgender or gender-variant. TDTF also advises and educates the public, media, and law enforcement regarding the needs of and sensitive differences between Trans Doe cases and other Doe cases. The TDTF also reaches out to assist law enforcement departments, medical examiners, and forensic anthropologists with getting their cases submitted for forensic genealogy.

Transgender and gender-variant people are disproportionately targeted in violent crimes, and have a higher suicide rate. They are more likely to be estranged from their families or end up vulnerable without adequate support. There are likely to be hundreds of transgender Does filed in cold case files as either “Jane” or “John,” which may or may not match the gender they lived under or the descriptions anyone looking for them may have given. We will seek out and find these individuals who have slipped through the cracks so that they can have a chance of being identified using the latest advances in forensic science and technology.

Note: The TDTF is not yet designated as a nonprofit and is currently operating under Redgrave Research Forensic Services LLC while we work on separate incorporation.

Name: Rocky Mountain HIDTA
Description:
The Rocky Mountain High Intensity Drug Trafficking Area (RMHIDTA) program was established by the White House Office of National Drug Control Policy (ONDCP) in 1996. As of 2020, the program has 30 HIDTA designated counties in 4 states (Colorado, Montana, Utah, and Wyoming).

 

The Rocky Mountain HIDTA mission is to support the national drug control strategy of reducing drug use. Specifically, the RMHIDTA's ultimate mission is to facilitate cooperation and coordination among federal, state and local drug enforcement efforts to enhance combating the drug trafficking problem locally, regionally and nationally. This mission is accomplished through intelligence-driven joint multi-agency collocated drug task forces sharing information and working cooperatively with other drug enforcement initiatives including interdiction. The aim of RMHIDTA is to:

Reduce drug availability by eliminating or disrupting drug trafficking organizations.

Improve the efficiency and effectiveness of law enforcement organizations in their efforts within HIDTA.

Name: Anti-Corruption Commission Maldives
Description:
The Anti-Corruption Commission (ACC) is the first modern form of democratic institution with the major functions to prevent and prohibit corruption within the public sector of the Maldives. The ACC was established on 16 October 2008. Prior to 2008, the function of corruption control was mandated to the Anti-Corruption Board (ACB)—a government agency established by Presidential decree on 21 April 1991.

The first act of legislation formulated to combat corruption in the Maldives is ‘Prevention and Prohibition of Corruption Act (PPCA) 2/2000’ of 31 August 2000. It prescribes legal provisions and guidelines for state institutions to prevent and prohibit acts of corruption in public offices. Later, the democratic process lead to the ratification of the 2008 Constitution, under which an ‘Anti-Corruption Commission Act (ACCA) 13/2008’ was enacted on 24th September 2008. The ACCA mandated the establishment of the Commission as an independent statutory institution and governed the duties and responsibilities of the Commission.

Name: U.S. Customs Service
Description:
The United States Customs Service was an agency of the U.S. federal government that collected import tariffs and performed other selected border security duties.

In March 2003, as a result of the homeland security reorganization, most of the U.S. Customs Service was merged with the border elements of the Immigration and Naturalization Service, including the entire U.S. Border Patrol and former INS inspectors, together with border agriculture inspectors, to form U.S. Customs and Border Protection, a single, unified border agency for the U.S. The investigative office of U.S. Customs was split off and merged with the INS investigative office and the INS interior detention and removal office to form Immigration and Customs Enforcement, which, among other things, is responsible for interior immigration enforcement. The United States Customs Service had three major missions: collecting tariff revenue, protecting the U.S. economy from smuggling and illegal goods, and processing people and goods at ports of entry.

Ethos Forensics provide the following services:

- Expert witness services including:
- A full review and reinterpretation of the prosecution forensic evidence 
- Expert advice to counsel prior to trial
- The provision of expert testimony at court

- A range of forensic training courses for forensic practitioners, legal experts, law enforcement personnel, forensic medical experts and students, including forensic awareness, the investigation of sexual offences, DNA profiling, forensic case assessment and interpretation and the role of quality systems in forensic science.
- Ethos Forensics can provide bespoke courses and guest lectures, tailored to the specific needs of a client.

- Peer reviewing and mentoring support to forensic providers.

- Quality systems consultancy to forensic providers and testing laboratories working to ISO 17025:2005, including:
- A review of existing quality systems and recommendations for improvement.
- Design and delivery of bespoke quality audits to ISO 17025.
- Support in implementing quality systems.
- Root cause investigation of existing quality issues.

Name: SPFPA - International Union, Security, Police and Fire Professionals of America
Description:
The passage of the Taft-Hartley Act led to the first international, independent guard union, The United Plant Guard Workers of America (UPGWA) which was certified by the NLRB in 1948 and initially included some 1,250 members in twelve local unions across five states.

Over the next 52 years, the UPGWA, under the leadership of Presidents McGahey, Szczesny, Applen, and McConville, grew significantly.

The beginning of the 21st Century brought significant and enduring changes to the structure and mission of the International Union.

Delegates to the 2000 International Convention operated under the mantra of New Directions for the Union as developed and proclaimed by International President Hickey.

The name of the Union was changed to the International Union, Security, Police and Fire Professionals of America (SPFPA) in recognition of the scope and professionalism of SPFPA members occupations. Divisions were established to better represent the needs of members whose occupations include Nuclear officers, Campus Police, Court officers, Airport Screeners, and others.

President Hickey and New Directions has continued and is responsible for programs and policies which keep the Union viable to meet new challenges.

A full time organizing staff has been employed to increase union membership; and their efforts have been overwhelmingly successful.

Programs instituted by President Hickey and the International Executive Board have resulted in the affiliation of nine other security unions with SPFPA with several others currently being negotiated. These affiliations have increased membership, enhanced SPFPA’s reputation, and added additionally qualified representatives to improve member representation.

In response to a critical membership need, the SPFPA developed and maintains a nationwide 401(k) pension plan and ERISSA Health Plan for negotiation and inclusion in collective bargaining agreements. SPFPA and the New Directions team have remained true to its heritage.

Name: INSTRUQ
Description:
It is the mission of INSTRUQ to provide progressive and comprehensive advanced officer training to enhance the skills of law enforcement professionals at all levels. To utilize technology in the delivery of quality, relevant training that is both affordable and convenient to the individual and agency.

INSTRUQ was founded in 2013 in partnership with Los Angeles HIDTA and Los Angeles CLEAR to augment their current advanced officer training initiatives and provide training to law enforcement throughout the United States. We offer courses in Narcotics, Gang Investigation, Homicide Investigation, Technology, Financial Crimes Investigation, Psychology, Officer Survival and Mental Health to name a few. Our Instructors consist of active and retired law enforcement professionals who offer CONCEPTS, METHODS and EXPERTISE in their respective fields. All students who complete instruction will be issued a certificate of completion from Instruq and LA HIDTA/LA CLEAR. INSTRUQ tracks the progress of each student via our propriety web based video player. Students must have an active broadband internet connection to view courses.

When purchasing training, students have the choice to attend class in person at our Commerce CA training facility, watch the course via live video streaming or watch on demand shortly after the initial live stream. If an individual signs up to train in person and is unable to attend, they can watch the live stream from their home or office.  If during the live stream the officer is called to duty, they can watch the training on demand and pick up where they left off when time permits.

INSTRUQ is only available to sworn law enforcement personnel and civilian support staff under the supervision of sworn law enforcement personnel.  Each individual who registers must provide information that will allow Instruq to verify their employment status.

Name: Miami County Sheriff's Office Troy Ohio
Description:
The Miami County Sheriff has many responsibilities and in order to fulfill these responsibilities employs a wide range of personnel. The current staff at the sheriff's office numbers approximately 115 and is divided into three main divisions. The divisions consist of deputy sheriffs, corrections officers, and records personnel.

By law, the Sheriff is in charge of the county jail and all persons confined therein. In Miami County we have two jails. The primary jail is downtown at the Miami County Safety Building and is currently certified to house a maximum of 48 prisoners. The second jail (Miami County Incarceration Facility) is north of Troy and has the ability to house a maximum of 240 prisoners when fully funded and staffed. The Incarceration facility was reopened in July of 2013 after being closed for 3 1/2 years due to budget constraints. Funding was approved to open three of four pods for a maximum of 120 minimum security male inmates and 60 minimum security female inmates.

The Sheriff also helps maintain a secure court system by providing courtroom security. Section 311.07 of the Ohio Revised Code places the responsibility of the courtroom upon the Sheriff, under the direction and control of the board of county commissioners.

The Sheriff is also responsible for the service or "delivery" of legal documents in Miami County. This includes summonses, subpoenas, and lawsuits. The Sheriff also serves evictions, repossessions, and seizes and sells property as ordered by the court.

The sheriff's office also transports hundreds of prisoners each year. Deliveries are made to and from state institutions, holding facilities, courts, medical and mental health facilities, and extraditions are made from other states.

Our patrol division has the responsibility to provide law enforcement services. Deputies handle calls and patrol the unincorporated areas. We also provide the primary law enforcement duties through agreements and contacts with the Village of Bradford, Villa

Name: Central Illinois Police Training Center
Description:
Mission Statement

The Central Illinois Police Training Center is a Statewide System for In-Service Training (ASSIST) for continuous in-service training for current law enforcement officers. Housed at Illinois Central College, our Training Center delivers in-service training to law enforcement officers in Peoria, Tazewell, and Woodford Counties.

The objective of Mobile Team In-Service Law Enforcement Training is to encourage local and state law enforcement officers to upgrade their knowledge and skills in techniques pertinent to the law enforcement profession; to provide law enforcement officers with training opportunities within their own locality, and on flexible schedules; and, to provide quality training on topics of instruction centered on specific local needs.

The Central Illinois Police Training Center is dedicated to providing the highest quality of training available to ensure professionalism amongst all Law Enforcement Officers with in its region.

Goals and Objectives

1.	Keep Law Enforcement Officers SAFE by providing them high quality training.
2.	Keep the Public SAFE by providing LEOs high quality training.
3.	To provide the most current and relevant in-service training to Law Enforcement Officers.
4.	To keep new training technologies and equipment within the reach of the Agencies we serve.
5.	Be ever-mindful of the responsibilities of being a good servant to the public and responsible with the funds entrusted to us.

Respectfully,

Michael J Oyer
Director 
Central Illinois Police Training Center

Name: G2 Research Ltd
Description:
G2 Research is the world leader in analysis software for investigative professionals tasked with analyzing data from trackers, cellphones, license plate readers, and any other source that has time and location information.

Our tools stand alone in the world at analyzing the data - not merely visualizing it.  Our analytics drive advanced intelligence capabilities such as making predictions on future travel and destinations while also finding meetings across groups.

ICU WORKBENCH is our flagship product. The ICU WORKBENCH automatically applies structure to your tracking data and gives you the power to quickly identify important events, trends and locations of your targets. Typical tracking data analysis tools illustrate the movement of a target but lack structure and depth. This leaves the user to manipulate raw tracking data, reducing time spent on high-value analysis. The ICU WORKBENCH also allows a user to import data from disparate data sources (ATM transactions, cell phone calls, Credit Card Transactions, Public Transit usage) and assembles a track from the various data sources, and produces the target's Pattern of Life.

Description:

O sistema é o único, em todo o Brasil, que é inteiramente online, garantindo total comodidade, facilidade e agilidade aos seus usuários. A Central de Certidões Eletrônicas e-CartórioRJ disponibiliza Certidões Eletrônicas, Kits de Certidões, Alienação Fiduciária e Validação Eletrônica.

The mission of Quantico LLT/LLC is to provide the highest quality law enforcement and legal training to law enforcement and corrections communities. Quantico LLT/LLC is made up of seasoned law enforcement and academics, working in the field of law enforcement, corrections, and law. Collectively, the instructors of Quantico LLT/LLC have over 100 years of experience working as certified instructors at numerous colleges, both domestic and international. The personnel and contractors from Quantico LLT/LLC come from a wide array of law enforcement, legal, and professional backgrounds. In coordination with the FBI National Academy Associates Northwest Chapter, a portion of the proceeds will be donated to various law enforcement charitable causes. Its organizational structure includes various directorates such as Intelkam, Lantas, Reskrimum, Reskrimsus, and Resnarkoba, and oversees the Yogyakarta City Police, Sleman, Bantul, Kulonprogo, and Gunungkidul Police. Name: Port Hope Police Service
Description:
The Port Hope Police Service is committed to achieving excellence in the service of our community and ensuring the safety and security of every citizen.

Timely response, team work, community engagement, full complete investigations and assistance to victims and witnesses will be the hallmarks of our performance.

Members shall perform their duties with full regard to our tradition of performing all duties with professionalism, dignity, respect, service and honour.

Members will approach each task with a view to the foregoing as the touchstones by which we conduct every facet of our work. Common sense and fairness to all shall be valued.

Members shall perform their duties with full regard to the Charter of Rights and Freedoms, the Laws of our Country, the policies of the Port Hope Police Services Board and the directives of the Port Hope Police Service.

Name: Del City Police Department
Description:
Welcome to the official LinkedIn page of the Del City Police Department – 

 Who We Are:
At Del City PD, we are dedicated to serving and protecting our vibrant community with unwavering commitment and professionalism. Our team of highly trained officers and support staff work tirelessly to ensure the safety and well-being of the residents and businesses that call Del City home.

 Community Engagement:
We believe in the power of community partnerships. Our officers actively engage with the community through various outreach programs, events, and educational initiatives. Together, we foster a sense of trust, collaboration, and mutual respect.

 Commitment to Excellence:
Del City PD is committed to upholding the highest standards of law enforcement. Our officers undergo rigorous training to stay current with the latest developments in policing, ensuring that we are well-equipped to handle any situation with competence and professionalism.

 Stay Connected:
Follow us on LinkedIn to stay informed about community events, safety tips, and updates from the Del City Police Department. We value transparency and open communication, and this platform serves as a bridge between our department and the community we proudly serve.

 Career Opportunities:
Explore career opportunities with Del City PD! We are always on the lookout for dedicated individuals who share our commitment to public service and community safety. Join our team and be a part of making a positive impact in Del City.

Name: Hurricane City Police Department
Description: The Hurricane City Police Department strives to be a community-oriented police department.  We feel that a well-informed, involved community is one of the greatest assets to law enforcement. The Hurricane City Police Department is made up of 23 full-time officers consisting of the Police Chief, Administrative Services Division, Investigations Division and Patrol Division. Additional assignments include School Resource Officers, Traffic Enforcement Officer and Washington County Drug Task Force Officer. Our officers patrol 24 hours a day, seven days a week.  The Hurricane Police Department Patrol Area is roughly 50 square miles and includes residential, commercial, industrial, recreational, open space and farmland.

Name: Conway Police Department
Description:
The men and women of the Conway Police Department are proud to serve the Conway, New Hampshire community. We are committed to providing the highest level of safety, security, and service to all of our residents and visitors, and to preserving the quality of life we enjoy here in the Mt. Washington Valley. Our officers are determined to serve as a deterrent to crime, to develop and maintain relationships with our schools, community groups, residential and business organizations, and to promote a safe environment receptive to tourism.

We hold ourselves to a standard of excellence in the performance of our duties and are committed to upholding all laws ethically and responsibly. In addition, we are dedicated to our personnel by providing continuous training and educational development, as well as career enhancement opportunities. We welcome comments and suggestions that will help us fulfill our commitment to excellence in providing these police services.

Name: Spencer Police Department (INDIANA)
Description:
The Spencer Police Department is a full-service police agency consisting of ten Full-Time Officers, six Reserve Officers, and one Chaplain, which provides police protection to a city of approximately 2,800 residents.

This agency coordinates activities with community organizations, facilitate the safe and expedient movement of vehicular and pedestrian traffic, provide neighborhood patrols, and serve as a presence for the deterrence of crime. Through the continued education and training of sworn officers, we strive to help build and maintain a safe community

Name: Erie County Law Enforcement Foundation
Description:
The Erie County Law Enforcement Foundation, Inc. (ECLEF) is a not-for-profit organization that supports law enforcement and stake-holders in community safety with resources that are not readily obtainable due to department budget restrictions or accessibility.

ECLEF provides grant-funding for needed specialized training opportunities and assets for Erie County law enforcement departments. We coordinate specialized free training for law enforcement in speacialized areas such as bio-terorism; drug identification, bomb-threat assessment, crypto-currency among many other important and timely topics. We also widely promote and host - for free - the annual Safe Schools Initiative Seminar and the Safe Places Seminar for schools, businesses & workplaces, houses of worship, military bases, nonprofit service providers, residential complexes, public transportation and open space operations as well as law enforcement to enhance safety in our community. ECLEF also recognizes outstanding contributions to public safety by our law enforcement community at our annual Awards Dinner. 

Our work is made possible through sponsorships, fund-raisers and the support of many individuals who recognize the need for a safer community.

Name: International Latino Gang Investigators Association (ILGIA)
Description:
The International Latino Gang Investigators Association was formally established on January 1, 2002.  ILGIA is a non-profit organization dedicated to providing quality training for law enforcement professionals and meeting the need for accurate information on Latino gangs and crime groups by members of the law enforcement community.

In 2002, ILGIA held its first National Gang Conference in Seattle, Washington.  In the years since, the conference has been hosted in San Antonio, Texas, Denver, Colorado, Annapolis, Maryland, Las Cruces, New Mexico, and Las Vegas, Nevada.  Through our national and international partnerships, we track emerging trends and prolific organized crime groups.  In addition to our National  Conference, the Association provides other training opportunities each year, most recently in Montgomery County, Maryland, Campbell, California, and San Bernardino, California. 

The ILGIA membership includes federal, state, local, and tribal peace officers, prosecutors, correctional personnel, parole and probation officers, and our brother and sister officers from the international law enforcement community.

Name: Hawaii Department of Law Enforcement (DLE)
Description:
The Hawaii Department of Law Enforcement (DLE) is dedicated to ensuring the safety and security of Hawaii's residents and visitors. Our mission is to uphold justice with integrity, transparency, and respect, embodying the spirit of Aloha in all our actions. 

Key Responsibilities: 
• Enforcing state laws and the Uniform Controlled Substance Act. 
• Administering statewide public safety programs. 
• Developing and implementing comprehensive Homeland Security initiatives. 
• Providing law enforcement services, including protection of state facilities and secure transportation of detainees. 

Special Units and Programs:
• Specialized units for narcotics and explosives detection. 
• Community outreach programs to enhance safety and build trust. 
• Collaborative efforts with federal, state, county, and private sector partners to address safety concerns and improve public welfare. 

At the Hawaii DLE, we are committed to protecting our community, preventing crime, and fostering a safe environment for all. Join us in our mission to serve and protect with Aloha.

Name: Barnegat Police Department
Description:
The Barnegat Township Police Department is a forward-thinking, premier municipal law
enforcement agency. The agency’s foundation is built upon a guardian mindset. The guardian
mindset guides officers in the use of their considerable power to ensure that it is used solely for
the defense, service, and protection of the people.

The agency’s motto: a regnati consensu translates to, “from the consent of the governed”. This
phrase is borrowed from the Declaration of Independence which stated, “Governments are
instituted among Men, deriving their just powers from the consent of the governed.” This
motto is again a reminder that our power is ultimately derived from the people, and is intended
to empower us to defend, serve, and protect the people.

Name: Georgetown Police Department
Description:
The Georgetown Police Department is a premier law enforcement agency committed to achieving excellence in all we do. Guided by the core values of Guardianship, Professionalism, and Dedication, we proudly serve the citizens of Georgetown, Kentucky, with integrity, transparency, and unwavering commitment.

Our mission is simple and strong: to serve our community with unwavering dedication, uphold the highest standards of professionalism, and act as responsible guardians of the public trust.

The strength of our department lies in our people. We are proud to employ some of the most dedicated and capable officers and civilian staff in the profession—individuals who are passionate about community engagement, professional in every aspect of their service, and deeply committed to the responsibilities of modern policing.

Whether you’re a community member, law enforcement professional, or public safety partner, we invite you to learn more about our work, connect with our mission, and engage with us here on LinkedIn. If you have questions or would like to know more about our department, we welcome the opportunity to connect.

We’re here to protect, serve, and lead—with purpose.

Name: Investigation Service of the Ministry of Finance of Georgia
Description:
Investigation Service of the Ministry of Finance of Georgia is the only law-enforcement authority combating financial-economic crime in the country.
The key aim of the Investigation Service of Ministry of Finance of Georgia is to support economic development of the country, combat  economic and tax crimes, protect healthy business environment and support legal entrepreneurial activities. To achieve the aim set out, the Service carries out investigative activities in full, appropriate expertise and measures for crime prevention, detection and suppression. 
In accordance with the Law of Georgia on Investigation Service of the Ministry of Finance of Georgia, the main goals and authorities of the Investigation Service were determined as follows: 
● avoiding crimes committed in financial-economic field, preventing, detecting and suppressing  corruptive transactions.  
● carrying out investigative activities in full, according to investigative subordination set under the Criminal Procedure Code of Georgia. 
● corresponding operative-searching activities in order to detect and suppress crime. 
● obtaining and analyzing information for further usage. 
● preventing and detecting administrative violations in financial-economic field. 
● detecting reasons that are facilitating crime in the fields of financial-budgetary and monetary-credit relationship, studying and analyzing those reasons.

Nestled in the suburban area of Kansas City, Missouri, Sugar Creek offers the charm of a small town with all the advantages of a big city location. Here you will find neighbors who care for each other, a progressive and accessible city government and a vibrant business community. In short, it's a special place where life is a little sweeter.

Name: Pax Facturación
Description:
En PAX Facturación nos esmeramos en crear soluciones tecnológicas innovadoras acorde a sus requerimientos, respaldado en la experiencia y certificaciones en las mejores prácticas en empresas de Tecnologías de la Información y Comunicaciones, mejorando constantemente nuestros servicios en beneficio de nuestros clientes.
En PAX Facturación contamos con la autorización por parte del Servicio de Administración Tributaria (SAT) para operar como Proveedor Autorizado de Certificación (PAC) con el número de autorización 58359.
Como PAC brindamos los servicios de emisión y timbrado de Comprobantes Fiscales Digitales por Internet (CFDI) mediante diferentes plataformas de comunicación en función de las necesidades y tamaño de cada uno de nuestros clientes. Los cuales son: 1. Un sitio WEB, en el cual se pueden capturar las facturas de manera individual; 2. A través de servicios WEB (Web Services), el cual se puede integrar a la mayoría de los sistemas administrativos y desde este mismo poder facturar; 3. A través de servicios FTP (File Transfer Protocol), el cual usamos para altos volúmenes de facturación donde podemos timbrar hasta 10,000 facturas en tan solo un minuto.
Actualmente tenemos la capacidad para timbrar 7 millones de comprobantes fiscales diarios, escalables conforme sean las necesidades de nuestros clientes.
Al día de hoy son muchas las empresas que han depositado toda su confianza en nosotros, y nos es muy satisfactorio el poder tener soluciones de integración para la emisión de factura electrónica y aplicaciones de software de acuerdo a sus necesidades; ya que con ello contribuimos a que se logre mayor eficiencia y efectividad en su operación.
Contamos con la infraestructura para dar respuesta a grandes proyectos, con esquemas para volúmenes altos y disponibilidad inmediata, ante ello solicitamos un espacio para que su equipo técnico evalúe nuestro servicio y puedan validar la efectividad.

Name: Macon Regional Crimestoppers
Description:
Macon Regional Crimestoppers is a law enforcement company based out of P.O. Box 6276, Macon, Georgia, United States.

Our organization is a non-profit group that assists law enforcement agencies in solving crimes through anonymous tips. We assist law enforcement agencies in eight counties in central Georgia (Bibb, Baldwin, Crawford, Houston, Jones, Monroe, Peach, Twiggs).

Our assistance includes receiving telephone tips from individuals who can place a call, 24 hours a day, to report information concerning criminal activity. These individuals may remain anonymous and may receive a reward up to $1,000.00 for information, which leads to the arrest of a suspect or solves a crime. The tip information is forwarded to the appropriate law enforcement agency for their use. Our program was created in 2000. We have paid rewards, since our inception, of over $1,000,000.00. We have been recognized six times, (2003, 2004, 2005, 2006, 2007, 2008, and 2009) for the Productivity Award by the Southeastern Crime Stoppers Association. By comparison, America’s Most Wanted, a National organization, has been in operation for 22 years and has just over 1,000 arrests.

In addition, we publicize specific requests for assistance from our law enforcement partners through our relationship with the media outlets serving the eight county area.

The members of the Board of Directors for Macon Regional Crime Stoppers are private businessmen, businesswomen and citizens who devote their time and money to make Greater Macon area a safer place to live.

Name: Ogden City Police Department
Description:
The Ogden City Police Department is committed to protecting life, preserving peace, and enhancing the quality of life for those who live, work, and visit Ogden, Utah. We serve a diverse and vibrant community through professional policing, proactive crime prevention, and strong partnerships with residents, businesses, and community organizations.

Our department emphasizes integrity, accountability, and service, guided by a commitment to constitutional policing and respect for the dignity of all people. Through community-oriented policing, problem-solving strategies, and data-driven enforcement, we strive to reduce crime while building trust and legitimacy within the community.

Ogden officers and civilian staff work collaboratively across patrol, investigations, specialized units, and community support roles to address public safety challenges with compassion, professionalism, and innovation. We are dedicated to continuous improvement through training, leadership development, and community engagement.

The Ogden City Police Department is proud to serve and protect—working every day to keep Ogden safe, resilient, and connected.

Description:

Name: Thin Blue Line International Association
Description:
The Thin Blue Line is a not-for-profit International Association, established in 2018, and registered in Geneva, Switzerland.  The Thin Blue Line supports police and other law enforcement agencies and their partners in their efforts to keep their communities safe, reduce fear, and improve the overall quality of life of their citizens.

The Thin Blue Line is focused on ensuring that interests of police are accurately represented in the international community.  The Thin Blue Line strives to  bringing out the best in police throughout the word, strengthening their capacity to protect and serve their communities to the highest standard. 

The Thin Blue Line International Association maintains a roster of experts to engage in a wide range of issues that involve law enforcement, including countering the improvised explosive device (IED) threat, enforcing international sanctions efforts, countering WMD terrorism, advocating effective use of less-lethal weapons, engaging partners in maritime and border security, preventing suicide bombers, and addressing transportation security threats.

Name: Raad voor de rechtshandhaving (CN)
Description:
The Law Enforcement Council (the Council) is a legal entity based on the Kingdom Act of the July 7, 2010: the Kingdom Act on the Law Enforcement Council. The Council is charged with the general inspection of the organizations of the judicial chain – with the exception of the common Court of Justice – in Curaçao, Sint Maarten, Bonaire, Sint Eustatius and Saba. The Council looks thereby at the effectiveness, the quality of the performance of duties and management. In addition, the Council is entrusted with the general inspection of the quality and effectiveness of judicial cooperation between the countries. The Council has a secretariat with branches in Curaçao, Sint Maarten and Bonaire (for the BES- islands).

/

De Raad voor de Rechtshandhaving (de Raad) is inter-landelijk orgaan van Curaçao, Sint Maarten en Nederland (voor zover het betreft de BES-eilanden). De Raad is ingesteld bij Rijkswet van 7 juli 2010: de Rijkswet Raad voor de rechtshandhaving.

De Raad is belast met de algemene inspectie van de organisaties van de justitiële keten – met uitzondering van het Gemeenschappelijk Hof van justitie – in Curaçao, Sint Maarten, Bonaire, Sint Eustatius en Saba. De Raad kijkt daarbij naar de effectiviteit, de kwaliteit van de taakuitoefening en het beheer. Daarnaast is de Raad belast met de algemene inspectie van de kwaliteit en effectiviteit van de justitiële samenwerking tussen de landen.

De Raad heeft een secretariaat met vestigingen op Curaçao, Sint Maarten en Bonaire (voor de BES-eilanden).

Name: Ulster County District Attorney’s Office
Description:
As a prosecutor, the duty and ultimate aim is to do justice – justice for the community, justice for the victim, justice for the accused, unfettered by fear or favor, or any outside agenda or influences. The District Attorney, though elected through our Constitutional political process, is a servant of all, and is bound by the maxim, “Justice For All.”

In pursuit of justice for all, an open mind and door, a commitment to making decisions based on the facts and circumstances of each case, a positive use and exercise of discretion, respect and dignity for all even as we vigorously prosecute violent crimes and undeterred recidivists, are necessary to assure community confidence in the outcome of every case and in the execution of the functions of the office. It is the mission of the Ulster County District Attorney’s Office to seek “Justice For All.”

Name: Chromatra, LLC ColorTAC Color Night Vision
Description:
Chromatra brings to market a proven, patented product that clips to a standard green or white phosphor PVS-14 night vision device and gives the user a near-true color image down to low light levels.  The ColorTAC CVA-14, a compact Color night vision product is the first and only viable solution to provide critical color night vision to the war fighter and law enforcement professional.

Capability Gaps Filled with Color Night Vision:

Ground night vision capability gaps that color can fill:
•	Improved terrain id and mobility
•	Tactical entry- navigating unlit buildings
•	Outdoor search and rescue operations
•	PID / IFF (positive identification) 
•	Blood at night / trauma id (venous vs arterial blood) 
•	Perimeter security 
•	Target id
•	Color ID of tactical lasers
•	Night vision rifle sights

Helicopter crew and flight  night vision capability gaps that color can fill:
•	Crew chief night vision duties / operations
•	Terrain ID / NOE (nap of the earth flight)
•	On board paramedic performance
•	Vegetation ID for hover flight 
•	Ship board landings
•	Operations: horizon vs water ID
•	Landing zone ID: color lights, color smoke, colored ground markings

Name: Frank G Whitworth High Court Enforcement since 1948
Description:
Established in 1948, we are an independent, family-run business providing our clients throughout the UK with the efficient, professional and, above all, personal service that so many of our rivals fail to offer within enforcement services.
Three generations of the Whitworth family have been involved in the company as directors ensuring our excellent customer service levels have remained consistent since 1948.
In 2019, to develop a better service for Claimants across England and Wales, Frank G Whitworth decided to merge with Enforcement Bailiffs Ltd trading as  Quality Bailiffs and Derby Legal Services Ltd.
The merger took some time and in 2020 it was achieved with the companies forming the National Enforcement Group Ltd.
Between our directors we currently have over a hundred years of experience within the enforcement industry. Daily we deliver a focused service to our clients’ instructions.
Over time the enforcement industry has changed. As a customer focused company we have changed and developed with it.
In 1948 we were the Sheriff’s Officers of Huddersfield West Yorkshire and operated only county wide. Now we have progressed to offering a full enforcement service covering all England and Wales.
We currently have local enforcement agents throughout England and Wales enabling us to offer the excellent services we provided in Huddersfield West Yorkshire throughout England and Wales via our seven regional offices.
In a changing industry we have never lost our core values to provide an efficient High Court enforcement service to our clients. Our independence and old-fashioned values are what gives us such a great client retention rate and makes all the difference to our results for clients.
No call centres, no answerphones, and no pointless delays. Be assured when you contact us you can count on the same friendly, professional response every time.

Name: Recoveri Tag What's Yours
Description:
Recoveri Tag Whats Yours is an International company established in 2000.  From humble beginnings, with its primary focus on researching and developing an innovative and technologically superior microdot identification and tracking solution for vehicles and other moveable domestic and corporate assets, Recoveri is now positioned to deliver its smart, flexible identification and tracing tool to the market.  

Microdot Technology
The microdot is a minuscule 1mm diameter transparent information disc onto which is etched a series of lines of text which is a unique Asset Identification Number or Name repeated across the disc.
Fifteen thousand of these microdot discs are mixed into a polyurethane solution which is both a transport medium as well as the adhesive for deploying them across areas of an asset. The solution contains an Ultraviolet material which, when exposed to the UV black light, will glow a blue hue indicating the presence of Recoveri Dots. The solution dries in a hard, clear finish almost invisible from the surface it is on, leaving the microdots firmly fixed in place.

Name: IPTI
Description:
The Internationl Police Training Insitute (PTI) in a not-for-profit, public interest foundation registered in Budapest, Hungary and co-located at the historic International Training Center (ITC) alongside the International Law Enforcement Academy (ILEA).  The IPTI concept evolved following the needs identified by FBI National Academy Associates international graduates around the globe for access to executive dev elopement support and training for emerging leaders.

The IPTI is the first international Non-Governmental Organization convened by senior police executives to support police leaders around the globe in their quest for training and organizational development that has been authorized by the FBI National Academy Associates.
 
The IPTI is comprised of a group of prestigious police leaders, and supported by the Board of Managing Directors drawn from law enforcement around the globe and business and social service specialists, to ensure that program design and delivery is holistic and engaging of community stakeholders and best practices from the business sector, not solely police focused.
 
The IPTI mandate is to not only provide training and support, but to also assist agencies without available funding to access grant opportunities. The IPTI was convened as a not-for-profit, public interest foundation in Hungary, with transparency and financial oversight in mind, so that grant issuing agencies are assured of compliance with grant conditions.

NYTOA MISSION STATEMENT: 
 The NYTOA recognizes that not everyone is a member of a SWAT team, Tactical unit or Negotiation team; however, all officers MUST BE tactically Minded.   Which is why the NYTOA strives to advance individual and team knowledge by providing a network to share RELEVANT, SOUND, PRINCIPLE AND CONCEPT BASED training and operational experiences. Name: South African Railways Police / Suid Afrikaanse Spoorwegpolisie
Description:
The South African Railways Police was established in 1934 to police the country's railways and harbours. It was later responsible for air services too.

In the Republic of South Africa the legislators established, in addition to the South African Police, a separate police force specifically for the purpose of protecting the distinctive interests of the S.A. Transport Services and at the same time guarding the gateways to the country — the harbours and international airports. This being specialised work, attention was not only paid to ordinary police work but in particular also to the national transport organisation with all its branches. This Police force was regrettably disbanded in 1986 and all its functions, members and logistic assets amalgamated with the South African Police.

Name: ALEAN
Description:
The Airport Law Enforcement Agencies Network (ALEAN) is a non-profit professional association that supports law enforcement agencies engaged in airport policing, both domestically and internationally. ALEAN advocates with Federal and other governing agencies, providing a unified voice to improve the efficiency and relevance of airport law enforcement activities.  Additionally, ALEAN facilitates information sharing, offers training, and provides other benefits outlined below.

ALEAN opens the door to network with other airport police departments to exchange information, ideas and training.

Our Command group is dedicated to representing the needs and concerns of airport law enforcement to government entities, ensuring that emerging security challenges are promptly identified and addressed. We collaborate closely with other aviation support organizations to present a unified voice in discussions with the TSA and other government agencies, thereby enhancing security measures across airports.

Our Intel group provides information on the latest criminal activity and unique solutions that have been effective in countering that activity. The Intel group holds monthly virtual meetings where investigators can share best practices and crime trends in a collaborative environment.

Our conferences provide training and briefings on current aviation and security-related topics; case studies on incidents that have occurred at airports; and updates from our federal partners. Our survey tool leverages a robust network of airport law enforcement agencies to benchmark best practices, planning, equipment, and personnel. Our secure website catalogs past survey results as well as other airport LEO documents for review.

La identidad en principios y valores de los abogados y abogadas que laboramos en esta empresa jurídica, nos permitió crear la misión y visión de este gran proyecto jurídico que se erige sobre principios de unidad e identidad en la especialidad, así como en la experiencia de los socios y asociados en procesos judiciales, derivada de sus labores como abogados litigantes, ex funcionarios del Poder Judicial y de instituciones de seguridad del Estado.
Hemos atendido cientos de procesos penales de alto perfil, por los más graves delitos contemplados en la legislación penal panameña, muchos de ellos con tremendo impacto social y político por estar vinculados políticos y servidores públicos que han sido la causa de responsabilidad del Estado panameño, a través de sus instituciones.

Nuestra organización, espíritu ganador, conocimiento y experiencia, nos convierte en una garantía para cualquier empresa o particular que deposite en este equipo la defensa de sus intereses.

Name: CSO Programs UCLA Police Department
Description:
The Community Service Officer (CSO) Programs are a primary outlet for student participation in campus safety at UCLA. CSOs provide evening escorts throughout the year and a campus shuttle service during academic quarters. CSOs patrol or they are stationed in designated campus buildings, all residence halls, parking lots and libraries, acting as the “eyes and ears” of UCPD, and provide a visible deterrent to crime. CSOs are a liaison between students and the UCPD and assist with outreach activities. In 2013, the CSO Programs began overseeing security guard services for the UCLA campus and certain off-campus locations. Working closely with approved private security guard companies, we provide service for departments that require a security presence and for short term special events. Management of security guard services at UCPD allows for customized service for the campus, whether there is a need for police officers, CSOs, security guards, or some combination of the three.

The CSO Program services the UCLA community through several shifts and work details including but not limited to the following:

Field Escorts: We provide escorts from a CSO within the boundaries of Sunset Blvd to the north, Hilgard to the east, Wilshire to the South, and Veteran to the west.

Residence Hall Patrols: CSOs are hired by the residence halls to patrol the dorms nightly.

Library Shifts: CSOs patrol all campus libraries daily. The larger libraries have CSOs stationed at entrances to monitor traffic.

Building Securities: CSOs monitor and restrict access to various buildings on campus

Special Events: Often, the CSO Programs are hired by campus departments or private companies to provide security services for temporary special events or activities.

The CSO position is exclusively for UCLA students. If you are interested in applying or want to learn more about our services, visit our website below, or click here to access our application: https://ucla.box.com/csoapplication

Name: Consent Decree Monitor (New Orleans)
Description:
On January 11, 2013, the City of New Orleans, the New Orleans Police Department (NOPD) and the United States Department of Justice (DOJ) entered into an Agreement to ensure that the services of the NOPD improve public safety, increase public confidence, and protect the constitutional rights of all citizens. In the Agreement, the NOPD agreed to fundamentally change the way it polices throughout the New Orleans community.  The NOPD Consent Decree is the result of an extensive investigation of the NOPD conducted by the United States Department of Justice. The investigation was conducted with the full cooperation of the City of New Orleans and the NOPD, and the resulting Consent Decree is a product of a cooperative effort by all parties.

The role of the Consent Decree Monitor is to "assess and report whether the requirements of [the Consent Decree] have been implemented, and whether this implementation is resulting in the constitutional and professional treatment of individuals by NOPD." The Consent Decree is comprehensive in nature and details specific areas for corrective action including: use of force; stops, searches, seizures and arrests; photographic lineups; custodial interrogations; bias-free policing; policing free of gender bias; community engagement; recruitment; training; performance evaluations; promotions; officer assistance and support; supervision; secondary employment; and misconduct-complaint intake, investigation and adjudication.  The Consent Decree represents a proactive and robust mandate for reform of the policies, training, and practices of the NOPD with specific agreed-upon corrective actions and timeframes for implementation.

Name: TEC-Veiligheid
Description:
Wie zijn we
Wij zijn TEC (Total Events Concept) Veiligheid: experts in veiligheid met jarenlange ervaring op het gebied van veiligheid, zowel voor evenementen als overheid en bedrijfsleven. TEC Veiligheid maakt 'veiligheid' begrijpelijk en toepasbaar voor u, zowel op het gebied van evenementen alsmede aangaande het verbeteren van de veiligheid in of rond uw organisatie. Veiligheidsvraagstukken komen in alle vormen en maten voor. Wij hebben met onze kennis en know-how een duidelijke visie voor ogen en kunnen u uitstekend voorzien van een waterdicht advies of ondersteunen bij de uitvoering ervan. Wij helpen u dus bij het vormen van een visie tot het ontwikkelen en implementeren van uw veiligheidsbeleid.

Hoe we werken
TEC Veiligheid levert professioneel advies en begeleiding bij uw veiligheidsvraagstukken. Dit doen wij op het gebied van evenementen en voor overheid en bedrijfsleven.

Veiligheid voor evenementen
Met onze ‘Total Events Concept’ visie gaan wij voor een A-Z aanpak: van vergunning(saanvraag) tot veiligheidsplan. Zodat u de bezoekers van uw evenement veiligheid kunt garanderen. Want als organisator van een evenement word u geconfronteerd met verschillende risico’s. Of het nu gaat om openbare orde, crowdmanagement, het weer, mobiliteit, brandveiligheid of gezondheidsrisico’s, het is belangrijk om overzicht te houden en de risico’s te beheersen. Dit kan onder andere met behulp van één van onze MCCU's: Mobiele Communicatie en Camera Units.

Dankzij de MCCU wordt een totaaloverzicht verkregen van bezoekers, publieksstromen, belangrijkste verkeersaders rondom het evenemententerrein enzovoorts. De wagens zijn volledig uitgerust met een eigen communicatiesysteem en beschikken over de modernste videoapparatuur en camera’s. Communicatiestromingen, zoals tussen de organisatie en de vrijwilligers, kunnen ook met de MCCU's worden gecoördineerd. De veiligheid van een evenement hangt tegenwoordig dus van meerdere factoren af; organisatoren moeten op alle mogelijke zaken voorbereid zijn en overal op kunnen inspelen.

Name: The Mid-South Cold Case Initiative, a 501(c)(3)
Description:
THE PROBLEM:  
According to the Uniform Crime Reports from the FBI, the United States has accumulated over 242,355 unresolved homicides since 1980.  The  average percentage of cases solved over the past few decades is about 60% a year, leaving 40% unsolved, many of which remain cold with little to no investigative work since the preliminary investigation.  This same crime data, since 1980, reflects that the number of unresolved homicides for the city of Memphis, TN exceeds 1,500.   Besides being a public safety concern, having hundreds of families without answers, and justice not being served, the cost of conducting investigations of violent crimes amounts to hundreds of thousands of dollars a year.  As a community, we can do better.

RESPONSE:
The Memphis Police Department is plagued with not only a significant increase in the number of homicides but also with a large decline in manpower. Couple that with inadequate funding to properly address the issue, the problem is only going to get worse.   Doing little to nothing is not the answer because working cold cases is not a luxury but rather a necessity.

Memphis needs our help!  Therefore, I am coupling my forty years of experience as a Death Investigation and Cold Case Investigation expert to the design of the MID-SOUTH COLD CASE INITIATIVE (MSCCI) with the express intent to raise funds that will augment the Memphis Police Department in their ongoing effort to resolve these cases. 

Let's make the Mid-south a safer place to live and visit!

Name: INNOVATIVE MINDS - "The IPR cell of Shoolini University"
Description:
The Intellectual Property Rights (IPR) Cell of Shoolini University is dedicated to fostering innovation and creativity among students, faculty, and staff. We understand the importance of protecting original ideas and inventions, and our goal is to ensure that the intellectual property of the university community is properly safeguarded and promoted.

Our team is comprised of experts in the field of intellectual property law, and we work closely with students, faculty, and staff to provide guidance and resources on a wide range of IPR-related issues. Whether you're an inventor looking to patent your latest invention, or a student working on a research project that may have commercial potential, we're here to help.

The IPR Cell provides support and guidance on a wide range of IPR-related topics, including:

Patent and trademark law
Copyright and fair use
Licensing and commercialization
Technology transfer and entrepreneurship
Intellectual property strategy and management

We also work closely with external partners, including government agencies, legal firms, and industry leaders, to ensure that the intellectual property of the university community is properly protected and promoted.

In addition to providing support and guidance, the IPR Cell also hosts a variety of events and workshops throughout the year to educate the university community on the importance of protecting and promoting intellectual property.

If you have any questions or concerns about intellectual property, please don't hesitate to contact us. We're here to help and we're committed to ensuring that the intellectual property of the university community is properly safeguarded and promoted.

The Rockbridge County Sheriff's Office was formed in 1778 and has served Rockbridge County and the City of Lexington for over 242 years.

The Sheriff's Office employs a total staff of 43 personnel serving in areas of patrol, investigations, civil process and communications. The Rockbridge County Sheriff's Office serves 607 square miles and a population of 22,400 not including the populations of Lexington and Buena Vista. The combined population reaches 36,300.

Rockbridge County is served by Interstates 81, 64 and US Routes 11, 60, 130, and 501. It is bordered by Bath and Alleghany Counties to the west, Augusta and Nelson Counties to the north, and Amherst and Bedford Counties to the east. To its south, it is bordered by Botetourt County.

Name: Meriden Police Department
Description:
On September 28th, 1868 the Meriden Police Department was formed to provide the citizens of the City of Meriden with law, order, and various policing services. Throughout the decades the agency has grown into a budgeted department of 123 sworn officers and numerous civilian support staff positions. Over the past 150 + years, the Meriden Police Department has been led by more than twenty (20) different Police Chiefs with the current chief, Roberto Rosado, having been appointed on July 1st, 2020. 

Today, the Meriden Police Department’s primary functions include, providing police services to the community, enforcing traffic and noise regulations, investigating crimes, assisting victims of crime, and maintaining law and order throughout the City of Meriden. The Meriden Police Department offers our services 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year, and is a Tier II, State Accredited Agency. Our officers are deployed in marked and unmarked cruisers, bicycles, motorcycles, specialized patrols, and on foot to meet the needs of the citizens of our community. 

There are four (4) main Divisions within the department: Police Operations Division, Investigative Services Division, Special Services Division, and the Support Services Division with each division comprising of specialized units, teams, offices, and numerous programs that aid and enhance the mission of the agency.  

Mission Statement: 
The mission of the Meriden Police Department is to protect life, safeguard property, and enforce the law in an ethical, compassionate, and constitutional manner while using procedural justice to promote fairness, transparency, and giving the community a voice, and being impartial. The Meriden Police Department utilizes 21st Century policing concepts to provide community based and problem-solving services in an effort to reduce crime, the fear of crime, ensure the safety of our community, enhance the quality of life, and build trust and partnership with our community.

Description:
Scilife Solutions Ltd is founded on a wide range of services to both law enforcement and the commercial sector globally. With over 30 years experience in UK and international policing challenges we have a wide network of partners both in the UK and around the world to address almost any challenge.

Our hands on experience includes some of the most challenging situations in living memory such as our direct involvement at command level in responding to the Disaster Victim Identification (DVI) mission in Thailand after the 2004 South East Tsunami, the 2005 hotel bombings in Egypt, the 2009 Air France crash off the coast of Brazil, and more recent work in Libya in 2011. We are available at any time to provide advice, guidance, training and consultancy to any future disaster scenario anywhere in the world. We are also able to provide expert assistance around emergency planning and delivering training exercises.

Our International Command Course in Disaster Management and Disaster Victim Identification is designed for international law enforcement agencies and strategic managers within the commercial sector who find themselves managing the most challenging events of mass fatality incidents, whilst maintaining their organisational reputation. Our  bespoke course is delivered on site by an international team of renown experts with significsant experinece of commanding global disaster situations.

We have a strong background in traditional policing solutions from traffic, crime investigation, covert solutions, through to counter terrorism. We  adapt our capacity to address any bespoke situation. Our operatives are experienced in the field and they are all qualified trainers. We work with partners to accredit any training provided.
Commercial sector inquires also welcome.

If you think we can help you then please 'e'mail to derekforest@scilifesolutions.co.uk for a confidential but friendly assessment of our capability. We do not think you will be disappointed.

This includes road accident rescue, vertical rescue, bush search and rescue, evidence searches (both metropolitan and rural) and other forms of specialist rescue that may be required due to local threats. Volunteers in a number of isolated communities have been trained as Community First Responders by the Ambulance Service of New South Wales. The Service's trained rescuers also support the full-time emergency services during major disasters.

The SES also assists other emergency services when they are performing major operations. These services include the NSW Police Force , the NSW Rural Fire Service , the NSW Fire Brigades and the Ambulance Service of NSW .

Name: Addyston Police Dept
Description:
Small village police dept a long US 50 and the Ohio River in the southwest corner of Ohio.

Located approximately 10 miles west of downtown Cincinnati on the Ohio River, Addyston is a friendly community of about 1,000 residents, founded in 1891 by Matthew Addy. 

Addyston Village Council first met on Wednesday, September 2nd, 1891. The first meeting of council dealt with organization laws that had to be passed in order for Addyston to become a legal village and control the growing population. 

The 1900 census shows there were many Kentuckyians and other southerners who came to work in the Pipe Foundry. Also in the census were Indianaians, Ohioans, people from England, Germany plus the Irish left over from the canal building.

Immediate needs were a school, churches, and places of business. The Burr Oak school was built in 1875 to provide for surrounding farm children and Addyston children. 

The west end of the village was the main business district. There were two large brick buildings, designed by John Boll. The brick buildings, one of which is still in use, were actually built before Addyston became a village. There were many grocery stores each with its own stables as was Diller's pharmacy. There was also a smithy, a cobbler, clothing stores, stables, and barber shops.

The east end business district was First Street, sometimes referred to as Front Street. Here there were several grocery stores, a large hotel and bar, a confectionary, and a Feed and Grain store. In 1891, Peter Wycoff's lumber mill was on the creek at the north end of First Street.

Name: MDLS
Description:
Mobilier de Logement Sécurisé à ses origines dans l’équipement de matériel de bureau. Suite à une demande du ministère de l’intérieur nous avons développé un produit de classement spécifique pour les armes de poing des forces de l’ordre comme la police, police municipale, police de l’air et des frontières, gendarmes et douanes.

Ces armoires sont installées dans des lieux sécurisés tout en ayant une accessibilité aisé et individualisé.

Nous avons mis au point un coffre breveté qui permet le classement de ces armes mais aussi du matériel personnel de chaque agent.

Depuis quelques années, une solution informatique vient compléter ce système unique et breveté 
« CASAR » c’est l’informatisation des coffres avec une carte sans contact qui permet d’ouvrir les coffres en individuel mais aussi une carte générale pour l’armurier qui permet l’ouverture automatique de tous les casiers de l’armoire en cas d’urgence ou de besoin simultané.
Un logiciel disponible avec notre centrale, permet d’avoir un contrôle sur les casiers à armes.

Name: Vigo County/Indiana State University High Tech Cybercrime Unit (HTCU)
Description:
The Vigo County Prosecutors Office and Indiana State University School of Criminology and Security Studies were awarded 1 of 10 High-Tech CyberCrime Units (HTCU) in the state of Indiana by the Indiana Prosecutors Attorney Council (IPAC) in November 2021. The project is the result of collaboration between the Vigo County Prosecutor’s Office, the ISU School of Criminology and Security Studies, the Terre Haute Police Department, and the Vigo County Sheriff’s Office. 

The HTCU is a digital forensics unit under the supervision of the Vigo County Prosecutor’s Office. The overall purpose of the HTCU is the location, preservation and analysis of digital evidence related to investigations in West Central Indiana. The HTCU conducts unlocking, extraction, processing, and analysis on all digital devices, including mobile devices (phones and tablets), DVRs, computers, hard drives, thumb drives, etc.

Name: Beyond the Badge
Description:
Beyond the Badge LLC is a Colorado-based training and consulting organization dedicated to strengthening law enforcement families and the agencies that support them. Founded by law enforcement family members, we believe true officer wellness extends beyond the badge — reaching the spouses, children, and support systems who serve in their own way every day.

We partner with agencies across the country to design and deliver customized programs that promote family engagement, improve retention, and enhance long-term wellbeing for first responders and their loved ones. Our services include Family Academy development and certification, peer support and mentoring program design, and wellness and resilience training for departments of all sizes.

Beyond the Badge offers practical, low-lift, and high-impact solutions for building connection, culture, and care within public safety. Our approach blends research-based content with lived experience to create tools that resonate in briefing rooms and at kitchen tables alike.

Name: Financial Intelligence Unit - FIU Cyprus (MOKAS)
Description:
The Cyprus Financial Intelligence Unit (MOKAS) was established in 1997 according to article 54 of Law No. 61(I)/96. This Law was replaced by The Prevention and Suppression of Money Laundering and Terrorist Financing Laws (No. 188(I)/2007). The Unit functions under the auspices of the Attorney General of the Republic as an autonomous, independently operating Unit and is composed of representatives of the Attorney-General, the Chief of the Police and the Director of the Department of Customs. The Unit is presided by a representative of the Attorney-General and its representatives are appointed by secondment. In relation to the Unit’s composition, the Law was amended in 2003 to include accountants and financial analysts who are Officers under the Law Office of the Republic.

The Unit, as the national FIU, receives, evaluates and analyses suspicious transaction reports and suspicious activity reports which are submitted by obliged entities as well as other information relating to the legalization of proceeds generated from illegal activities, predicate offences and the financing of terrorism. It is also responsible for the dissemination of the results of its analyses to competent authorities for the conduct of investigations (the Police) or for information purposes. The Unit does not conduct criminal investigations.

Name: CopBlue
Description: CopBlue was created by street cops for street cops.  It's simple, CopBlue is going to get the message that really matters out to our fellow cops.  Our goal is for every one of us to go home at the end of our tour.  And we don’t just mean going home, but going home to a healthy, happy life with our family & friends.  We know the Job, and we know the life because we've been, and are, out on the streets with you.  We understand the stress of the Job, and just as important, what that stress could do to you and your family.  Cops want to go to work and do their best to serve their communities.  At the same time none of us has the desire to end up on the national news or the topic of a viral social media post.  No one can think this doesn’t play a part in how we do our jobs.  Police work continues to evolve, which we all expect, it's a fact we can't do anything about.  What CopBlue can do is help make these changes a little less stressful.  CopBlue is written in the language of street cops, our authors are encouraged to be frank and speak openly.  We want our message to come from people who've been on the job rather than someone trying to deliver a politically-correct message who's never been in a radio car. We thrive on sharing different points of view, unfortunately, in this type of work there's times it could be offensive; that’s something else we can't do anything about.  There’s also critical information we need to know to keep ourselves physically & mentally fit.  CopBlue will find experts in these fields so we can all learn what’s best & how to help each other.  All of us at CopBlue have lost friends & colleagues that were living in pain & fighting on the inside. CopBlue will keep repeating, we must watch out for each other, talk honestly to each other; forget the stigma, ask for help. Remember, you jo can't see all injuries & someone saying their OK, might not be. Please join us at CopBlue.com; like us on Facebook & follow us on Twitter.

Name: Forces Project
Description:
FORCES is an EU-funded project focused on combating organized crime by dismantling criminal networks and their business models. With a strong international dimension, FORCES promotes cooperation between Europe, Africa, Latin America, and Southeast Asia to address the convergence between environmental crime and transnational criminal activities.

 Starting date: 1st of May 2025
 End date: 30th of April 2027

The project works across four strategic areas:

 Enhancing criminal intelligence, by improving information-sharing between the EU and partner countries, and building trust through networks like the Jaguar Network and a new EU–Africa platform.

 Strengthening financial investigations, equipping law enforcement with skills in tracing financial flows, analyzing open-source and dark web data, and using digital forensics.

 Facilitating cross-border operations, especially in maritime ports linked to illicit trade in drugs, timber, and other commodities—connecting public and private actors across continents.

 Tackling environmental crime, including the deployment of innovative tools like automated detection of trafficked glass eels at airports.

The Forces Project consortium is coordinated by Guardia Civil SEPRONA (Spanish Ministry of Interior) and includes the following partners:
-TRAFFIC Europe
-French Ministry of the Interior CESAN Commandement pour l'environnement et la santé 
-IFAW Germany (International Fund for Animal Welfare)
-WWF-FR (World Wide Fund for Nature – France)
-GNR - Guarda Nacional Republicana (Portugal)
-EVOLUXER SL (Spain)

Together, these actors form a multidisciplinary alliance of public authorities, NGOs, and experts committed to strengthening law enforcement capacities and fostering long-term international cooperation against organized crime.

Name: FBI National Executive Institute Associates
Description:
The FBI National Executive Institute Associates (NEIA) is a private, non-profit organization, of chief executives of the largest law enforcement agencies throughout the U.S., Canada, Australia, and Europe. Representing a broad range of key Federal, state and local agencies, these top officials, totaling more than 1500, are all graduates of the FBI’s National Executive Institute (NEI), an intensive, three-week leadership training program held at the FBI Academy at Quantico, Virginia.

Established in 1976 in response to the Major Cities Chiefs’ Association’s (MCCA’s) request for a program designed specifically for the Nation’s highest ranking law enforcement executives, NEI soon opened its classes to international students. Today, 43 classes have graduated from NEI, which is widely recognized as one of the world’s premier law enforcement leadership training programs.

The vision of the FBI NEIA is to strengthen effectiveness, justice and foresight in law enforcement leadership and enable law enforcement leaders to build safer, more ethical and civil communities.

The FBI NEIA focuses on emerging issues of policy and practice in law enforcement globally. We assist law enforcement executives by building networks, mentoring, sharing unique experiences and future-casting. We engage with other public and private sector policy makers to build mutual understanding and collaborative action.

Name: Resource Extraction Monitoring
Description:
REM is a non-profit company incorporated in the UK. We specialise in strengthening forest law enforcement and governance through independent forest monitoring (IFM). IFM is often the only source of explicit and objective information on the realities of forest governance - a valuable resource for demand-side mechanisms like the EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR). 

We work in collaboration with governments while retaining our independence. We led projects in Cameroon, the Republic of Congo, and the Democratic Republic of Congo for over a decade before handing over to national experts on our team who are now the leading IFMs in the Congo Basin. 

Our current focus is expanding IFM to other sectors, such as cocoa, and exploring how emerging technologies like blockchain, fintech and AI can help solve global forest governance problems.

Name: New Jersey State SPCA Humane Police
Description:
The New Jersey Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals (NJSPCA) Law Enforcement Divison was created in 1868 to prevent animal cruelty and investigate cases of animal cruelty in New Jersey. The agents of the NJSPCA were established as law enforcement officers in charge of investigating and prosecuting all persons involved in animal abuse and neglect. The NJSPCA is the second oldest SPCA in the country, behind the ASPCA in New York.

The New Jersey State SPCA Humane Police focused on humane law enforcement and investigations of animal cruelty throughout New Jersey. The agency is one of just a few law enforcement agencies in the state that have statewide authority. The agency hired both sworn officers that carry firearms, as well as unarmed investigators.

In January of 2018, Governor Chris Christie signed a bill intended to dissolve the organization and transfer animal cruelty law enforcement to county prosecutors, municipalities, and county SPCA agencies. The NJSPCA will cease law enforcement operations by August 1, 2018.

Cada año, miles de bicicletas, patinetes, móviles y otros objetos de valor son robados, y la mayoría nunca se recuperan. Follow Track nace para cambiar esto.

 ¿Qué hacemos?
Hemos desarrollado una plataforma innovadora que utiliza IA, geolocalización y una red de profesionales (detectives, aseguradoras y cuerpos de seguridad) para localizar y recuperar objetos robados de manera eficiente.

 ¿Cómo funciona?
1 Los usuarios reportan el robo en la plataforma.
2 Activamos nuestra red de rastreo.
3 Con IA, analizamos patrones y ubicaciones probables.
4 Coordinamos la recuperación del objeto.

 ¿Por qué elegir Follow Track?
 Primera solución en Europa que conecta víctimas con una red profesional.
 IA y análisis de datos para detectar patrones de robo.
 Integración con aseguradoras y cuerpos de seguridad.

 Nuestra visión
Queremos ser la referencia en recuperación de objetos robados en Europa, expandiéndonos a mercados clave como España, Francia y Alemania.

 www.followtrack.es |  info@followtrack.es

#FollowTrack #Tecnología #Seguridad #IA #Innovación #MovilidadSostenible

Name: Jefferson Davis Parish Sheriff's Office
Description:
Jefferson Davis Parish is located in Southwest Louisiana, midway between Houston and New Orleans, and is intersected by the Interstate-10 corridor. Covering an area of 652 square miles, the parish has an approximate population of 32,000 residents and is comprised mostly of rural agricultural areas. Rice, sugarcane, and soybean farming, coupled with cattle and crawfish production, are all integral components of this area's economy. The oil and gas industries also continue to thrive in the area but on a much smaller scale, as opposed to oil's heyday in the early 1900s. The area is additionally well known for its enjoyable waterfowl hunting and freshwater fishing.

Interstate-10, the Union Pacific Railroad, and The Port of Mermentau, which connects to the Intracoastal Waterway, all serve the parish's local businesses and industries. Located within the parish is an industrial park consisting of 200 acres of prime industrial real estate and is located one half-mile from I-10. The park features a rail spur, state-of-the-art scale house, and all the amenities required to do business within the park. The industrial park is also home to Zagis USA, a 20 million dollar cotton-spinning mill which processes 50 million pounds of cotton annually; and to Louisiana Spirits, which operates an 18,000 square-foot rum distillery. The distillery's unique brand, Bayou Rum, is made exclusively from Louisiana sugarcane. The distillery, producing the only commercially-produced local rum in the state outside of New Orleans, also operates a museum and gift shop and can accommodate tours.

Having separated from Imperial Calcasieu Parish in 1912, Jefferson Davis Parish was one of the last incorporated parishes to be formed in Louisiana. The parish is comprised of the incorporated cities and towns of Elton, Fenton, Jennings, Lake Arthur, and Welsh. The city of Jennings, the parish seat, is known for being the location of the first discovery of oil in Louisiana in 1901.
