Name: Canine Training Academy LLC
Description:
Providing professional tracking and man trailing courses for law enforcement, military and search & rescue K9s.

The Canine Training Academy has been providing quality tracking and man trailing courses and services since 1997. We specialize in training law enforcement officers and their K9s to track or trail. We are dedicated to the advancement all dog breeds used for tracking and trailing work. This makes our program unique.

We offer a varity of services including; basic and advanced handler courses, consulting services and phone consultations, off site seminars, private one on one traiinng and trained tracking dogs for sale.  

Our Mission

To provide a practical approach to training and working a tracking or trailing dog so that handlers and their dogs can advance to an effective level of real world deployment readiness. 

To improve technique, assist handlers in gaining valuable training options and provide teams with a solid basic foundation in tracking and trailing K9 work. We believe in honest, realistic training standards that work in real world situations, not just in training or at certification time. 

Our training program was originally developed and has evolved from years of working experience, not just theories. We train teams to meet real world deployment expectations.

We have assembled a team of professional trainers from around the country who specialize in tracking and trailing work. All our traniers have working backgrounds and have been in service as full time Police K9 handlers or Search and Rescue Volunteers.

The demands and expectations we place upon our trailing K9s today far outweighs traditional expectations. Our training is designed to prepare handlers for these new challenges. We strive to produce effective K9 teams who can meet modern deployment requirements. Our professional instructors are dedicated to providing the most complete, comprehensive training program available .

On behalf of the members of the University of Maine Police Department, welcome to our LinkedIn page. We encourage you to explore it and hope it can be a source of information for you.

The University of Maine Police Department is a full-service police department staffed by state-certified police officers and support personnel 24hrs a day, 365 days a year. The training and professional standards met by UMaine’s officers are the same as those required in any municipality across the state.

Because of the unique nature of a university community and the activities that take place in it, UMaine officers are “specialists.” They are practitioners of a kind of police work that makes them a vital and positive force in the community that they protect and serve.

Sincerely,

Roland J. LaCroix
Chief of Police

Name: PROELIA Defense and Arrest Tactics
Description:
PROELIA Defense and Arrest Tactics is a complete system for all law enforcement personnel developed by working cops and based on the needs of the end users; the officers and deputies on the job who use it. It has been taught to law enforcement recruits and in-service personnel with great success for several years.

PROELIA Defense and Arrest Tactics is a constantly evolving and adapting system based on Human Performance Science, how the body reacts and performs under real world conditions. It is not an overly complicated system of multiple techniques dependent on fine motor skills. Rather, it was designed to facilitate the application of gross motor skills, and is based upon reoccurring principles that can be applied to a wide variety of situations. It has been proven effective with officers regardless of size, strength, gender and level of training. 

The developers of this system have decades of real law enforcement experience including patrol, special operations units, covert investigations and more.  They have been instructing defense and arrest tacitcs to academy level and in-service personnel for over 10 years.  They have been nationally published in use of force and defense & arrest tactics in both magazine and on-line articles.

PROELIA Defense and Arrest Tactics, LLC is an authorized Shudokai (branch dojo) of the Shudokan Ryu – Taiho Jutsu.

Go to: www.proeliadefense.com for more!

Name: East Central Dispatch Center
Description:
The East Central Dispatch Center (ECDC) is a comprehensive consolidated effort to serve the public safety communication needs of eight municipalities within St. Louis County.  An intergovernmental agreement was formalized between the cities of Brentwood, Clayton, Maplewood, Olivette, Richmond Heights, Rock Hill, Shrewsbury, and Webster Groves. This agreement serves to designate the ECDC as the multi-jurisdictional center for all emergency communications with regards to fire, police, and emergency medical services for the participating cities. 

The staff of the East Central Dispatch Center has many responsibilities including answering all incoming informational, emergency and 911 calls.  Each call is evaluated and the appropriate Public Safety personnel are dispatched to handle the situation.  They provide law enforcement with REJIS record inquiries and provide emergency medical dispatch to ensure accurate and prompt care to all callers.  ECDC employees monitor panic alarms throughout our cities, help boxes at City Halls, and monitor prisoners held in the jail/holdover facility via closed-circuit cameras, and verify warrants for other law enforcement agencies. 
 They regularly monitor additional radio frequencies which allow them to communicate with State Highway Patrol, and neighboring Police and Fire Departments. In addition to computer controlled radio communications, each console has an active 9-1-1 position, and a terminal connecting the dispatcher with REJIS, MULES, Department of Revenue, Motor Vehicle and Driver's License Bureaus, and the National Crime Information Center (NCIC). 

The mission will be accomplished through constant professional in-service training, coupled with quality assurance standards.  The goal of the training and professional standards is the recruitment and retention of dedicated staff that are confident, efficient, empathetic, and skilled professionals.  ECDC has a strong commitment to proficiency and motivated performance while serving as the vital link between the public and the agencies served.  This type of consolidated agency development puts the member cities into a unique, innovative, and leading example of functional cooperation for the entire St. Louis metropolitan region. 

Should you have interest in becoming a public safety dispatcher, please contact us.

Name: The presidency Code of Conduct Bureau (CCBNIGERIA)
Description:
The Code of Conduct Bureau (CCB) is Nigeria’s premier institution for ensuring ethical governance, transparency, and accountability in the public sector. Established under the 1999 Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria, the Bureau is mandated to enforce the Code of Conduct for Public Officers, thereby fostering a culture of integrity within government institutions.

Core Mandate and Functions:
Asset Declaration and Verification: Ensuring public officers declare their assets to promote transparency and prevent illicit enrichment.
Ethical Compliance Monitoring: Enforcing standards related to conflict of interest, abuse of office, and other ethical violations.
Investigation and Prosecution: Addressing breaches of the Code of Conduct through the Code of Conduct Tribunal (CCT).
Public Enlightenment and Policy Advocacy: Educating citizens and public officers on ethical governance and compliance with anti-corruption laws.
Through strategic enforcement, digital transformation, and public engagement, the CCB remains committed to upholding the highest standards of integrity in Nigeria’s public administration.

Name: Foro de Estudios Contra la Corrupción y la Delincuencia Económica
Description:
El Foro de Estudios Contra la Corrupción y la Delincuencia Económica (FECCDE) es una asociación de profesionales que nace de un profundo anhelo: promover el conocimiento contra las prácticas delictivas de contenido económico y la corrupción política, comercial y empresarial en España.
Aunque en los últimos años la sociedad ha tomado conciencia de los efectos corrosivos de delitos tales como el blanqueo de capitales, el cohecho, la malversación, la prevaricación o el tráfico de influencias, somos muchos los que consideramos que aún existe un desconocimiento generalizado de las implicaciones y especialidades técnicas de estas conductas y, sobre todo, de las medidas para combatirlas de manera efectiva y eficaz.
Por ello, en FECCDE queremos ser un vehículo fundamental para la divulgación de las complejidades jurídico-económicas y denuncia pública de las prácticas corruptas y delictivas, constituyéndonos como punto de encuentro, estudio y debate de todos aquellos ciudadanos que, sin perjuicio de sus diferentes planteamientos ideológico, quieran tomar parte activa en la lucha contra la corrupción y la delincuencia económica, exigiendo a nuestros poderes públicos que implementen más y mejores medidas legislativas para poner punto y final a los desmanes políticos y empresariales, impulsando  campañas de concienciación, promoviendo aquellas reformas que sean necesarias y denunciando casos individuales de corrupción en cualquiera de sus manifestaciones, de cualquier partido político, sindicato, empresa o persona, sin distinción de ideología, territorio o cualquier otra circunstancia.

Name: North Carolina Correctional Institution for Women

Description:
En LIDERES Y NEGOCIOS, diseñamos y construimos entornos laborales seguros, sanos y competitivos, para que así las empresas puedan evitar accidentes de trabajo y enfermedades profesionales. Todo ello con un objetivo de que cada colaborador pueda contar con altos estándares de bienestar y seguridad y la empresa pueda incrementar sus niveles de productividad y competitividad.
La seguridad y la salud en el trabajo (SST) es un conjunto de disciplinas que busca el bienestar físico, mental y social de los trabajadores en todas las ocupaciones, y la prevención de los accidentes y las enfermedades relacionadas con las labores desempeñadas. Para tal fin tiene por objeto mejorar las condiciones y el medio ambiente de trabajo a través de la anticipación, el reconocimiento, la evaluación y el control de los peligros que surgen en lugar de trabajo y que pudieran poner en peligro el bienestar de los trabajadores como principios fundamentales del proceso que rige la gestión de los riesgos.

En nuestra pagina encontrara información relevante referente a Seguridad y Salud en el Trabajo, normatividad, productos innovadores y demás.

Name: First Basic Public Prosecutor's Office in Belgrade
Description:
First basic public prosecutor's office in Belgrade is the largest basic public prosecutor's office in the Republic of Serbia with 50 Deputy public prosecutors and 35 prosecutor's assistants and interns and is in charge of five central municipalities of the City of Belgrade, Vracar, Savski venac, Stari grad, Zvezdara and Palilula
 
According to the Constitution of the Republic of Serbia, the public prosecutor’s office is an independent state authority which is in charge of the prosecution of perpetrators of criminal offences and other punishable acts and which undertakes measures for the protection of constitutionality and legality.

Basic public prosecutor’s offices criminally prosecute perpetrators of criminal offences which are punishable under law by a term in prison of up to 10 years

The Republic Public Prosecutor’s Office is the highest public prosecutor’s office in the Republic of Serbia. The Republic Public Prosecutor exercises jurisdiction of the public prosecutor’s office within the scope of the rights and duties of the Republic of Serbia and is responsible for the work of the public prosecutor’s office and for his/her own work before the National Assembly. Public prosecutors are held accountable for the work of the public prosecutor’s office and for their own work by the Republic Public Prosecutor and by the National Assembly whereas lower-ranking public prosecutors are also held accountable by the immediately higher-ranking public prosecutor.

The public prosecution system includes Republic Public Prosecutor’s Office, 4 appellate public prosecutor’s offices (in Belgrade, Novi Sad, Niš and Kragujevac), 26 higher public prosecutor’s offices, 34 basic public prosecutor’s offices and 2 prosecutor’s offices with special jurisdiction.

Name: GoEvidence Forensic Laboratories, LLC
Description:
GoEvidence Forensic Laboratories is an independent forensic laboratory that specializes in the processing and development of latent fingerprint evidence. We also provide latent print examination and evidence photography. All of our analyst are Certified Latent Print Examiners.

GoEvidence is the North American sales agents for West Technology Systems Limited for all of their Vacuum Metal Deposition (VMD) Systems. VMD is a long-established processing technique for the development of latent prints.  This process can also be used on fabrics to help find grab marks.  Under high vacuum, selected metals are evaporated.  These metals coat the surface of the evidence.  The metals stick to the evidence except where there may be contamination.  Latent fingerprints are contamination, therefor they become visible. 

GoEvidence provides VMD as a service too.  Our vacuum chambers are ready to process your cold case evidence. 

Now there is an independent laboratory for those that need help with fingerprint evidence. Our goal is to provide you with the highest standards of excellence with the most advanced technology available in the fingerprint industry.

Name: Lehigh Township Police
Description:
The Lehigh Township Police Department was organized in 1947 by Roland Wieand, who served as its first chief. In the early 1950s the department began providing 24 hour police services. Roland's son, Harold, and his grandson John Klutzaritz, served as subsequent chiefs through 1979. 

Chief Klutzaritz was succeeded by Jeffrey Schuler who served as Chief until he retired in May 2012. The current Chief, Scott Fogel, was appointed by the Board of Supervisors, June 2013. 

Lehigh Township
visit us on facebook
The Police Department added two Sergeants to the rank structure in 2008, Sergeant Michael McGonigle & Sergeant John Henry III. 

The department currently has one civilian employee and 12 officers, including a Detective, two firearms instructors,  and three control/defensive tactics instructors. Lehigh Township Police also operate the Lehigh Township Regional DUI taskforce which serves 17 Northampton County municipalities and is comprised of 14 police agencies. 

Lehigh Township is a rural area north of the city of Allentown and west of the City of Easton. It covers an area of approximately 31 square miles and serves a resident population of approximately 11,000. The Police Department serves a predominate rural-agricultural community with pocket residential developments throughout the township, as well as several commercial and retail businesses along the Route 145 and 248 corridors. 

The Police Department also serves a very large traffic volume due to the township's network of state highways that travel between the Pocono tourist areas, Blue Mountain Ski resort, Allentown & Easton. Route 248 traverses the township and connects all points in Northampton County from Lehigh Township to Easton. Route 145 & Blue Mountain Drive are the main north-south routes connecting the City of Allentown and Whitehall township to Lehigh Township.

Name: Northport Police Department
Description:
The Northport Police Department serves the Incorporated Village of Northport which is locatedn Suffolk County, New York.  The Village of Northport sits on the north shore of Long Island and is located about 30 miles east of New York City and it is directly across the Long Island Sound from Stamford CT.
 
 Northport was settled in 1656 and was incorporated in 1894, is about 2.5 sq. miles in area, with 32 miles of roadway with two business districts, one on Main Street which terminates at Northport Harbor and the other is on State Road 25A.  The population is about 8,000 people.

The Northport Police Department, has 17 full time officers.  All patrol and arrest processing and most investigations and training are handled in house (the Suffolk County Police Department assist in specialized cases).  The department has a marine unit which patrols the waterways.  In addition, the Northport Police Department is a 911 answering/dispatch center for multiple agencies.  The department has one Chief of Police, one Lieutenant, four Sergeants, one Detective and ten Police Officers.   There are also four part time Police Officers and two part time Harbormasters, all sworn with police/peace officer powers.  The department also employs four full time Dispatchers and one Traffic Agent.  Additional part time employees include four Traffic Agents, six School Crossing Guards, two unsworn Harbormasters and six Dockmasters.

The Northport Police Department traces its roots back to the 1880’s when there was one “Constable” for the entire village.  The department is now headed by Chief Bill Ricca who is the seventh officer to hold the title of Chief of Police in Northport, the first being appointed in 1929. ( Robert King 1929, Charles Martin 1935, Percy Erving 1954, John Trunzo 1964, Robert Howard 1980, Ric Bruckenthal 2000, and Bill Ricca 2015). 

In a typical year the Northport Police handle about 5,600 calls for service, investigate about 500 incidents and make about 300 arrests.

Name: Freehold Borough Police Department
Description:
The number one asset of the Freehold Borough Police Department is its people.  We have exceptional people.

These times have been tough, but we're doing more with less.  Many of our officers wear multiple hats, and all have chipped in to ensure that we continue to move forward.  Our officers have a wide variety of skill sets, and we've tapped just about all of them to meet all the challenges that face us each day.

Law Enforcement has made great strides over the years.  We're much better than we were decades ago, and it shows in reduced crime and a better relationship between police and the community.

Our officers are incredibly proud to serve Freehold Borough.  The Borough is an incredible town with rich history, tremendous pride, and a sense of community very few other towns will ever know.  It shows in our parades, our little league games, and our festivals.

We keep these traits in mind each day as serve and always try to take great care of those who live, work, and visit our wonderful town.

Our philosophy is about achieving the sometimes difficult balance between the always competing interests of enforcing the law, rendering assistance, and problem solving. 

We accomplish this by deploying teams of seasoned, knowledgeable and caring men and women onto our streets each day who, not only represent our established core values, but define them even further

Our philosophy is our motto:  The Highest Level of Service.

Metro New York Concerns of Police Survivors (C.O.P.S.) Chapter
Description:
The Concerns of Police Survivors (C.O.P.S.), is a National organization whose mission since 1984 has been “rebuild shattered lives” of the surviving family members and distraught co-workers of law enforcement officers who have died in the line of duty. Though the group draws strength from the collective experience and empathy of its members, C.O.P.S. quite frankly would prefer not to increase in number. Still, reality dictates there will always be a need for associations such as C.O.P.S. as statistics reflect on average 160-180 law enforcement officers are killed every year in the line of duty.

C.O.P.S. uniquely helps by providing emotional support and hands on healing programs needed to cope with a sudden or violent death. C.O.P.S. is a not-for-profit 501(c) (3) national organization with 53 chapters throughout the United States. Sadly, membership today has grown to more than 30,000 survivors. Thus, in response to this emergent need, we have chartered the Metro NY Concerns of Police Survivors chapter.

*C.O.P.S. has NEVER and will NEVER solicit via phone for donations. If you receive a call like this, please contact your local police department and local media immediately. Direct them to call Sara Slone, Public Relations Manager, at (573) 346-4911.*

Like us on Facebook
https://www.facebook.com/MetroNYCOPS

Follow us on Twitter
@MetroNYCOPS
https://twitter.com/MetroNYCOPS

We dragen we bij aan een veilige en leefbare samenleving en werken hiervoor samen met verschillende partners. Bekwame en betrokken medewerkers zorgen voor een moderne, dynamische bedrijfscultuur. 

Bij politiezone Demerdal-DSZ zijn we continu op zoek naar gedreven, gepassioneerde en talentvolle medewerkers om ons korps te versterken en bij te dragen aan een veilige samenleving.

Waarom kiezen voor politiezone Demerdal-DSZ?
- Teamgeest: Bij ons werk je in een hecht team waar samenwerking, respect en ondersteuning centraal staan. We organiseren regelmatig sport- en teambuildingactiviteiten zodat iedereen elkaar beter leert kennen.
- Diversiteit en Inclusie: Je bent een gewaardeerd lid van onze diverse familie. We erkennen en omarmen de verschillen in achtergrond, ervaring en perspectief omdat dit ons sterker maakt.
- Opleiding en Ontwikkeling: We investeren in jouw groei. Of je nu nieuw bent in het vak of een doorgewinterde professional, wij bieden training en ondersteuning om jouw vaardigheden verder te ontwikkelen.
- Moderne Faciliteiten en Technologie: Onze zone maakt gebruik van de nieuwste technologieën en voorzieningen om onze taken efficiënt en effectief uit te voeren. Zo kunnen onze medewerkers op het hoogste niveau presteren.
- Betrokkenheid: Bij ons krijgt iedereen de kans om echt betrokken te zijn bij het werk. We moedigen je aan om je passies en interesses te volgen en waarderen jouw input. Samen maken we het verschil.

Name: Dickson County Sheriff's Office
Description:
Since 1803, the Dickson County Sheriff's Office, led by an elected Sheriff with approximately 150 Deputies, Corrections Officers and civilians, have continuously served the over 53,000 citizens of Dickson County by ensuring the 491 square miles of Dickson County remains a safe, and desirable place to live, work or visit.

The Dickson County Sheriff's Office consists of dedicated and committed professionals who partner with the community and other agencies as needed to solve problems, protect property and improve public safety in a manner that is fair, impartial, transparent and consistent with our core values of:

Integrity
We are committed to the enforcement of laws and the preservation of order and property. We are honest, truthful and consistent in our words and actions, and therefore worthy of the public's trust. We exercise discretion in a manner that is beyond reproach.

Professionalism
We treat everyone with courtesy and respect. We understand that our appearance, words and demeanor contribute to the public's confidence in us. We are responsive to the community and deliver services promptly and efficiently.
 
Fairness and Impartiality
We act with fairness, restraint and impartiality in carrying out our duties. We understand that our actions, combined with the way we treat members of the community, contributes to the legitimacy in the eyes of the public.
 
Teamwork
We work together as one organization in carrying out the mission of the department in order to solve problems and serve the community.

Name: Despacho de Abogados Angulo y Asociados
Description:
Contamos con  un equipo de profesionales  especialistas en las diferentes  áreas jurídicas, colegiados, Abogados ejercientes y con la colaboración de  profesores que nos dieron su apoyo y hoy día continuamos aún más trabajando. Al inicio como toda empresa no fue fácil pero un cliente nos recomendó a uno y este a su vez a otro, creando nuestra propia cartera de clientes,  desde entonces estamos ahí, confiando en nuestro trabajo.
Desde el inicio nos hemos  preocupado por solucionar cualquier problema a nuestros clientes, sin distinguir entre su profesión, nacionalidad, clase social, y siempre digo a los colaboradores: “Primero el cliente y el cliente, y segundo informarle si es viable su caso o que es lo que se le recomienda ya que todo se parece y los clientes nos vienen que si su amigo y su situación puede ser parecida pero no la misma.”
Nosotros asesoramos jurídicamente por ejemplo: desde un caso penal por un delito de malos tratos por violencia doméstica, hasta si después se quiere divorciar el cliente, luego si trabajaba en una empresa y lo despiden como queda su situación,  después si desea constituir su propia empresa, si desea ampliar su capital, si necesita regularizar a sus trabajadores o contratar a un ciudadano extranjero solicitando la tarjeta de residencia correspondiente o presentar una demanda contra la administración al existir un error en una denegación de tarjeta, realizarle un cambio de nombre en una compra venta de vehículo, hacer la declaración de la renta, luego asesoramos si quieren traer a sus familiares de turistas o a residir en España,  en fin, tantos procedimientos.
Estando hoy día después de más de 10 años en el ejercicio de esta profesión orgullosa de estar donde estamos y de la confianza que hemos recibido de nuestros clientes como del apoyo de todos los colaboradores en este proyecto ya que sin cada uno de ellos esto no hubiera sido posible.

Hiram Police Department Core Values:

We, the Hiram Police Department, show in our words and actions that we value:

Commitment – to the highest levels of professionalism with honesty and truthfulness in our actions.
Obligation - to serve the community with respect and dignity.
Perseverance – towards the diligent pursuit of our organizational mission and vision despite hardships, obstacles, or opposition.
Service – through acting professionally with the interest of the community foremost by providing impartial and fair police services.

These things constitute the Core Values by which we live as officers of the Hiram Police Department.

Name: Arizona Crime Prevention Association
Description:
The Arizona Crime Prevention Association (ACPA) was created in 1977 by crime prevention officers, civilian employees, volunteers, and members of security and loss prevention industries, dedicated to the promotion of Crime Prevention Awareness throughout the state of Arizona.


The goals of the ACPA are to focus attention on local, state and regional issues related to crime prevention and provide a forum for the exchange, coordination and teaching of ideas, concepts and programs pertaining to the reduction of crime. This is done by providing:

Crime Prevention training to law enforcement officers, crime prevention specialists, business loss prevention experts and private citizens.
  
Various Powerpoint presentations, brochures and forms on a variety of crime prevention topics that are available for download in the Members Only section.
  
A vast and constant support network of crime prevention specialists, both private and law enforcement, with a variety of professional expertise

Name: The Police Society for Problem Based Learning
Description:
The Police Society for Problem Based Learning is a not for profit organization created by law enforcement professionals for the purpose of improving the quality of police training. Our goal has always been to promote the use of Problem Based Learning and to support the Police Training Officer (PTO.)

OUR MISSION:
1. To promote the advancement of law enforcement towards community-oriented policing and problem solving through problem-based learning.

2. To introduce modern educational methods  to promote the values of problem-solving, critical thinking and ethical decision-making in law enforcement culture.

3. To facilitate and sustain transformation to problem based learning at training academies and at all levels of police departments, corrections and allied social institutions.

4. To develop and sustain critical thinking and problem-solving skills through the Police Training Office (PTO) program.

5. To form strategic alliances that promote and sustain interdisciplinary connections and communications among services committed to transforming policing and correctional services to community based problem-solving cultures.

6. To promote related training standards for academies and instructors engaged in public safety training.

The society is run by volunteers who believe in a better training for police professionals. If you are interested in learning more or joining our society, please contact us and we will be happy to start a conversation.

Sincerely,
The PSPBL Board Members

Name: Psicobalance, Psicología integral
Description:
Nuestra función principal es el peritaje, evaluación y elaboración de informes. También ofrecemos mediación y terapias de pareja, familiar y a menores.

PERITAJES CIVILES: En casos que se debe valorar la responsabilidad civil, y las valoraciones para incapacitaciones

PERITAJES DE FAMILIA: Competencia parental, tipo de guarda y custodia mas conveniente para los menores, régimen de visitas, valoración de la modificación de medidas cautelares. Valoración psicosocial. Adopciones y acogimiento familiar.

PERITAJES PENALES: Valoración de responsabilidad criminal, imputabilidad, credibilidad de testimonio, en personas inmersas en delitos penales, mediante el estudio de personalidad y estudio de factores atenuantes. Valoración de secuelas en las victimas.

PERITAJE LABORAL: Valoraciones de incapacidades y/o bajas laborales, mobbing o acoso laboral. Secuelas psicológicas producidas por accidentes laborales.

PERITAJES EN VIGILANCIA PENITENCIARIA: Dirigidos a la evaluación de reclusos que solicitan permisos de salida, libertad condicional o la modificación de grado.

CONTRAINFORMES O SEGUNDA OPINION: Cuando el usuario inmerso en un proceso judicial, no esta satisfecho con la evaluación y conclusión de otro peritaje psicológico.

Name: Poindexter Global Intelligence
Description:
A multi-state licensed investigative and competitive intelligence firm. The firm's owner and all members of the firm's investigative cadre have been trained and employed by, or are currently contracted by governmental agencies to perform intelligence, counterintelligence or defense contract functions. The operative members of Poindexter Global are licensed where appropriate, and possess an average experience level of 15 years in various investigative and countermeasures fields. 

The firm's principal maintains unique relationships with various national administrations which allow unencumbered private intelligence operations in most of the western hemisphere. Poindexter operatives are provided state-of-the art equipment appropriate to their respective investigative or countermeasures assignments. Discreet movement from nation to nation is extended by special agreement with several air carriers, and customs clearances for operatives and sensitive equipment facilitated in most locales under diplomatic accord.

Member of:

Association For Intelligence Officers (AFIO)
Council of International Investigators (CII)
